54323 | Supabase Studio | Web UI |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Data Model\n\n### 3.1 Entity Relationship Summary\n\n```\nOrganization (tenant)\n  ├── Units (physical spaces: apartments, condos)\n  │     ├── Unit Rooms (rooms within a unit + measurements)\n  │     ├── Unit Documents (linked architectural drawings)\n  │     └── Work Orders (one per trade per unit)\n  │           ├── Work Order Tasks (room × template checklist)\n  │           └── Work Order Notes (comments, voice logs)\n  │\n  ├── Rooms (master list: Kitchen, Bathroom, Bedroom...)\n  │\n  ├── Work Order Templates (trade checklists)\n  │     └── Template Definition (JSONB: sections → columns → steps)\n  │\n  ├── WO Projects (properties/job sites)\n  │     ├── Project Documents (uploaded PDFs, drawings)\n  │     │     └── Document Images (extracted pages/floor plans)\n  │     └── Project-scoped Template overrides\n  │\n  └── Template Libraries (importable template collections)\n```\n\n### 3.2 The Template Hierarchy (Three-Tier)\n\nThis is the most architecturally significant design decision in the system:\n\n| Tier | Scope | Purpose | Example |\n|------|-------|---------|--------|\n| **App** | System-wide | Factory defaults, read-only | \"Standard Painting Checklist v1\" |\n| **Company** | Per organization | Tenant customizations | \"Acme Painting Checklist\" (adds extra steps) |\n| **Project** | Per wo_project | Job-specific overrides | \"123 Main St Painting\" (removes N/A rooms) |\n\nTemplates cascade: a project template inherits from its company parent, which inherits from the app default. The `parent_template_id` and `copied_from_template_id` columns track lineage.\n\n### 3.3 The JSONB Strategy\n\nRelational tables handle entities that need indexing, joins, and permissions. JSONB handles deeply nested hierarchical data:\n\n| Table | JSONB Column | Contains |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| `work_order_templates` | `definition` | `{ sections: { \"Prep\": { columns: [...] }, \"Finish\": { columns: [...] } }, logic: { rules: [...] } }` |\n| `work_order_tasks` | `task_data` | `{ steps: [{ key, label, status, completed_by, completed_at, not_applicable }] }` |\n| `units` | `metadata` | Flexible unit-specific notes, special instructions |\n| `project_definitions` | `floorplan_definitions` | Nested room/item trees per floor plan type |\n| `products` | `attributes` | Spec matching data (dimensions, colors, materials) |\n\nA SQL view (`view_project_bom`) flattens the JSONB trees into rows for NocoDB consumption.\n\n### 3.4 Key Database Tables (Public Schema)\n\n**Core Business:**\n- `organizations` — Multi-tenant root\n- `units` — Physical spaces (apartments)\n- `rooms` — Room type catalog\n- `wo_projects` — Job sites/properties\n- `work_order_templates` — Trade checklist definitions\n- `work_orders` — Template instances per unit\n- `work_order_tasks` — Room-level checklist execution\n- `work_order_notes` — Comments and voice log entries\n- `template_libraries` — Importable template collections\n\n**Documents & Media:**\n- `project_documents` — Uploaded PDFs, drawings\n- `document_images` — Extracted page images\n- `unit_documents` — Links documents to units\n- `image_links` — Links images to any entity\n- `unit_rooms` — Room instances with measurements\n- `unit_room_measurements` — Detailed dimension data\n- `unit_room_calculations` — Computed areas/volumes\n\n**Tenant Portal:**\n- `portal_table_config` — Per-organization table access configuration (access levels, column visibility, editable columns)\n\n**Materials (Planned/Partial):**\n- `metric_definitions` — Measurement metrics\n\n**NocoDB (~90 tables):**\n- `nc_*` — NocoDB's internal metadata for its spreadsheet interface\n\n---\n\n## 4. Shared Packages\n\n### 4.1 `packages/schemas` — The Contract\n\nThe most critical package. Every data shape is defined here as Zod schemas:\n\n| File | Exports | Used By |\n|------|---------|--------|\n| `work-orders.ts` | Organization, Unit, Room, Template, WorkOrder, Task, Note schemas + API request/response types | API routes, Work Orders app |\n| `materials.ts` | Supplier, Product, ProjectSupply schemas | Material Admin, API |\n| `renovation.ts` | Unit, UnitScopeItem schemas (legacy?) | Site Companion |\n| `taxonomy.ts` | Taxonomy categories (actions, room types) | Material Admin, API |\n| `delivery.ts` | Delivery tracking schemas | API |\n| `todo.ts` | Simple todo schemas | Web Dashboard |\n| `zod.ts` | Shared Zod utilities | All |\n\n### 4.2 `packages/config` — Centralized Configuration\n\nHardcoded Supabase URLs, API keys, port assignments, and CORS origins. All apps import from here for consistency.\n\n### 4.3 `packages/db` — Database Types\n\nAuto-generated TypeScript types from Supabase schema via `pnpm db:types`. Provides compile-time safety for direct database queries.\n\n### 4.4 `packages/ui` — Shared Components\n\nReusable SolidJS components (buttons, layouts) for consistent design across apps.\n\n### 4.5 `packages/logger` — Structured Logging\n\nShared logging utility for consistent output formatting.\n\n### 4.6 `packages/tsconfig` — TypeScript Configuration\n\nBase TypeScript configuration inherited by all packages and apps via `workspace:*` symlinks.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Applications\n\n### 5.1 API (`apps/api`) — The Brain\n\n**Framework**: Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js\n**Port**: 3000\n**Key Dependencies**: `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `@supabase/supabase-js`, `pdfjs-dist`, `@napi-rs/canvas`\n\n**Routes:**\n\n| Route | Purpose | Key Operations |\n|-------|---------|----------------|\n| `/work-orders` | Work order CRUD | Create from template, update task steps, manage lifecycle |\n| `/work-orders` (documents) | Document management | Upload PDFs, link to projects/units |\n| `/materials` | Supplier/product catalog | CRUD for suppliers, products, project supplies |\n| `/taxonomy` | Category management | Actions, room types, item categories |\n| `/projects` | Project definitions | JSONB scope trees, floorplan definitions |\n| `/units` | Unit management | CRUD with metadata, scope items |\n| `/voice` | Voice processing | Audio → Whisper transcription → LLM structured extraction |\n| `/delivery` | Delivery tracking | Delivery scheduling and status |\n| `/todos` | Simple todos | Basic CRUD (reference/demo) |\n| `/a2a` | Agent-to-Agent protocol | JSON-RPC endpoint for AI agent interop |\n| `/ui` | Swagger UI | Interactive API documentation |\n| `/doc` | OpenAPI JSON | Machine-readable API spec |\n\n**Services Layer** (`src/services/`): Business logic separated from route handlers.\n**Middleware** (`src/middleware/`): Auth, validation, error handling.\n**Scripts** (`scripts/`): 50+ utility scripts for seeding, migration, testing, and verification.\n\n### 5.2 Work Orders App (`apps/work-orders`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS + Vite\n**Port**: 3004\n**Purpose**: Primary management interface for work order lifecycle\n\n**Pages:**\n- `Dashboard` — Overview of all work orders, status summaries\n- `Units` — Browse and manage physical spaces\n- `Templates` — View/edit work order template definitions\n- `TemplateDetail` — Deep dive into a single template's sections and columns\n- `WorkOrderDetail` — Execute a work order: room-by-room checklist with step completion\n- `Projects` — Manage wo_projects (job sites)\n- `ProjectDetail` — Single project view with units, work orders, documents\n- `NewProject` — Project creation wizard\n- `ProjectDocuments` — Upload and manage architectural drawings/PDFs\n\n### 5.3 Site Companion (`apps/site-companion`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS PWA\n**Port**: 3003\n**Purpose**: Mobile-optimized field worker interface\n\n**Pages:**\n- `UnitList` — Browse assigned units\n- `UnitDetail` — View unit scope, execute work items\n- `VoiceTest` — Voice recording → transcription → structured data extraction\n\n**Key Feature**: Designed as a Progressive Web App for offline-capable field use. Workers can record voice memos that get transcribed and parsed into structured work log entries.\n\n### 5.4 Material Admin (`apps/material-admin`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS\n**Port**: 3002\n**Purpose**: Supplier catalog, product management, taxonomy editing\n\n**Features:**\n- Supplier CRUD with contact info\n- Product catalog with JSONB attributes for spec matching\n- Project supplies (consumables) management\n- Taxonomy editing (room types, actions, categories)\n- JSON tree editor for complex nested definitions\n- BOM (Bill of Materials) flattened view\n\n### 5.5 Web Dashboard (`apps/web-dashboard`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS (minimal)\n**Port**: 3001\n**Purpose**: Legacy/reference admin view\n\n**Features:**\n- Login via Supabase Auth\n- Basic todo management (reference implementation)\n- Hono RPC client integration example\n\n### 5.6 Tenant Portal (`apps/tenant-portal`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS + Vite\n**Port**: 3005\n**Purpose**: Configurable, embeddable data portal for external tenants\n\n**Features:**\n- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with email/password and magic link support\n- **Dynamic Table Viewer**: Renders any configured table with pagination, sorting, and column filtering\n- **Inline Editing**: Click-to-edit cells for tables marked as editable, with automatic type detection\n- **Config-Driven Access**: `portal_table_config` table controls per-organization visibility:\n  - **Hidden**: Table not shown to tenant\n  - **Read Only**: Tenant can view but not modify data\n  - **Editable**: Tenant can inline-edit specified columns\n- **Column-Level Control**: Admins can hide columns, restrict which are editable, and set custom display order\n- **Admin Configuration Page**: Service-role powered interface to configure table access per organization\n- **Embeddable**: Hash-based routing (`#/dashboard`, `#/table/:name`, `#/admin`) works in iframes\n- **RLS Enforced**: All tenant queries use the anon key with Row-Level Security for automatic data isolation\n\n**Pages:**\n- `LoginPage` — Email/password + magic link toggle\n- `DashboardPage` — Card grid of available tables with access level badges\n- `TableViewPage` — Dynamic data grid with sort, pagination, inline editing\n- `AdminConfigPage` — Per-organization table access configuration (uses service role key)\n\n**Data Flow:**\n```\nTenant → Login (Supabase Auth) → Dashboard\n  → portal_table_config (RLS filtered) → shows available tables\n  → Click table → supabase.from(tableName).select() (RLS filtered)\n  → DataGrid renders with readonly/editable mode per config\n  → Editable cells → supabase.from(tableName).update() → RLS enforced\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Supporting Services\n\n### 6.1 Doc Parser (`doc-parser/`)\n\n**Framework**: FastAPI (Python)\n**Port**: 5000\n**Infrastructure**: Docker with GPU support (Detectron2, LayoutParser)\n\n**Capabilities:**\n- PDF upload and page rendering\n- Layout analysis (text blocks, tables, figures)\n- Floor plan detection and extraction\n- Image upload to Supabase Storage\n- Text extraction via PyMuPDF\n\n**Integration**: Called via HTTP from the Hono API or directly from Agent Zero. Uploads extracted images to Supabase Storage and returns structured metadata.\n\n### 6.2 NocoDB (`nocodb/`)\n\n**Port**: 8082\n**Purpose**: Spreadsheet interface directly connected to the Supabase PostgreSQL database\n\n**Use Cases:**\n- Bulk editing relational data (units, rooms, taxonomy)\n- Viewing flattened SQL views of JSONB data (`view_project_bom`)\n- Ad-hoc queries without building custom UI\n- Deep linking to the Material Admin app for complex JSONB editing\n\n### 6.3 A2A Protocol\n\nThe API exposes an Agent-to-Agent JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a` with an agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`. This enables:\n- External AI agents (like Agent Zero) to discover and interact with the platform\n- Structured task delegation (e.g., \"create a work order for unit 101\")\n- Multi-agent workflows across different AI systems\n\n---\n\n## 7. Data Flow Patterns\n\n### 8.1 Work Order Lifecycle\n\n```\n1. Admin creates Template (definition JSONB with sections/columns)\n         ↓\n2. Admin creates Work Order for Unit + Trade\n         ↓\n3. System \"snapshots\" template → creates WorkOrderTasks (one per room)\n         ↓\n4. Field worker opens Site Companion → sees room checklist\n         ↓\n5. Worker completes steps (task_data JSONB updated)\n         ↓\n6. Inspector reviews → signs off → work order marked complete\n```\n\n### 8.2 Voice Processing Pipeline\n\n```\n1. Field worker records voice memo in Site Companion\n         ↓\n2. Audio sent to API /voice endpoint\n         ↓\n3. Whisper transcribes audio → text\n         ↓\n4. Anthropic Claude extracts structured data:\n   - Hours worked\n   - Items completed\n   - Issues/notes\n         ↓\n5. Structured data creates/updates work_logs, work_order_tasks\n```\n\n### 8.3 Document Processing Pipeline\n\n```\n1. Admin uploads PDF (architectural drawings) via Work Orders app\n         ↓\n2. Extracted images stored in document_images table\n         ↓\n5. Images linked to units/rooms via image_links table\n```\n\n### 8.4 NocoDB ↔ Custom App Integration\n\n```\n1. NocoDB reads relational data directly from PostgreSQL\n         ↓\n2. Material Admin's JsonTreeEditor modifies JSONB directly\n         ↓\n5. Changes visible in NocoDB immediately (same database)\n```\n\n### 8.5 Tenant Portal Access Flow\n\n```\n1. Tenant clicks a table → TableViewPage queries supabase.from(tableName)\n   (RLS automatically filters to tenant's organization data)\n         ↓\n5. For editable tables: click cell → inline edit → supabase.update()\n   (RLS prevents cross-tenant writes)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Production requires proper `organization_id` scoping and role-based access.\n\n### 9.2 Architecture Strengths\n\n- **Schema-first approach** prevents data model drift across 5+ applications\n- **JSONB hybrid** elegantly handles the tension between structured queries and deeply nested domain data\n- **Template hierarchy** is a sophisticated solution to the \"every project is slightly different\" problem\n- **Monorepo with Turborepo** enables rapid iteration without dependency hell\n- **A2A protocol** future-proofs for AI agent integration\n\n### 9.3 Architecture Risks\n\n- **JSONB complexity**: The flattened SQL views are read-only.     "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "Great question — since you plan to mount it in multiple places, a **named volume** is the right call.\n\n---\n\n## Named Volume vs Bind Mount\n\n| | **Bind Mount** (what you have now) | **Named Volume** |\n|---|---|---|\n| Syntax | `C:\\Users\\...\\path:/container/path` | `myvolume:/container/path` |\n| Data lives | On your host at a specific path | Managed by Docker (in Docker's storage area) |\n| Portable | ❌ Tied to host path | ✅ Works anywhere, any OS |\n| Shareable | ⚠️ Repeat full path everywhere | ✅ Just reference the name |\n| Performance | Slower on Windows/Mac (filesystem translation) | ✅ Faster (native Docker storage) |\n| Backup | Manual | `docker volume` commands |\n| Pre-populate | No — host folder wins | ✅ Docker copies container contents into empty volume |\n\n---\n\n## How to Create & Use\n\n### Option A: Let `docker-compose.yml` create it (recommended)\n\n```yaml\nversion: \"3.8\"\n\nservices:\n  agent-zero:\n    # ... your existing config ...\n    volumes:\n      - ./data:/a0/usr\n      - voicelog:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold\n\n  another-service:\n    image: some-image\n    volumes:\n      - voicelog:/app/data  # 👈 Same volume, different mount point\n\n# Declare the named volume at the top level\nvolumes:\n  voicelog:  # Docker creates & manages this automatically\n```\n\nThat's it. Run `docker compose up` and Docker creates the `voicelog` volume if it doesn't exist.\n\n---\n\n### Option B: Create manually first\n\n```bash\n# Create\ndocker volume create voicelog\n\n# Inspect (see where Docker stores it)\ndocker volume inspect voicelog\n\n# List all volumes\ndocker volume ls\n\n# Remove\ndocker volume rm voicelog\n```\n\nThen reference it in compose with `external: true`:\n\n```yaml\nvolumes:\n  voicelog:\n    external: true  # Don't create it, it already exists\n```\n\n---\n\n## Your Specific Case: Seeding with Existing Files\n\nSince your code already exists at `C:\\Users\\3900X\\...\\voicelog-scaffold`, you need to **get those files into the named volume**. Named volumes start empty. Options:\n\n### Seed from host → volume (one-time)\n```bash\n# 1. Create the volume\ndocker volume create voicelog\n\n# 2. Copy files into it using a temporary container\ndocker run --rm -v voicelog:/data -v \"C:\\Users\\3900X\\Documents\\GitHub\\agentZero\\voicelog-scaffold:/src\" alpine cp -a /src/. /data/\n```\n\n### Or: Use a bind mount for the \"source of truth\" + named volume elsewhere\n\nIf you want your Windows folder to remain the **source of truth** (you edit files there), keep the bind mount on Agent Zero and use a named volume only for other services that need a copy:\n\n```yaml\nservices:\n  agent-zero:\n    volumes:\n      - ./data:/a0/usr\n      - C:\\Users\\3900X\\Documents\\GitHub\\agentZero\\voicelog-scaffold:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold  # bind mount - live files\n\n  other-service:\n    volumes:\n      - voicelog:/app/data  # named volume - independent copy\n\nvolumes:\n  voicelog:\n```\n\n---\n\n## TL;DR Recommendation\n\nSince you're actively developing and want to **see changes live**:\n\n1. **Keep the bind mount** on Agent Zero (so I can see your live files)\n2. **Use a named volume** for other services that just need the data\n3. # Schema Design: Hybrid Relational + JSONB

## Strategy
We use **PostgreSQL** with a hybrid approach to balance performance and flexibility.
*   **Relational Columns**: Used for Foreign Keys, Indexing, Sorting, and Permissions.
*   **JSONB Columns**: Used for the deep hierarchical definition of the "Scope of Work", Attributes, and flexible metadata.

## Tables

### 1. `projects`
High-level container for a job site.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `name` (Text)
*   `code` (Text, Unique) - e.g., "PRJ-2026-001"
*   `status` (Enum: bidding, active, archived)

### 2. `project_definitions`
The "Brain" of the project. Steps away from relational rows to store the Template hierarchy.
*   `project_id` (FK)
*   `taxonomy_version` (Text)
*   **`floorplan_definitions` (JSONB)** - A dictionary of floorplans (`Plan A`, `Plan B`) and their default rooms/items.

### 3. `units`
The inventory of saleable/workable spaces.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `project_id` (FK)
*   `unit_number` (Text) - "101"
*   `floor_level` (Int)
*   `floorplan_key` (Text) - Links to `project_definitions`.
*   **`specific_overrides` (JSONB)** - Stores exceptions for *this unit only*.

### 4. `site_items` (The BOM)
Derived table. Exploded from JSON for tracking status.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `unit_id` (FK)
*   `subject` (Text) - "toilet"
*   `location` (Text) - "Master Bath"
*   `status` (Enum: pending, installed, verified)
*   `percent_complete` (Int)
*   `labor_estimate_hours` (Decimal)

### 5. `timesheets`
The container for a day's work.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `user_id` (FK)
*   `date` (Date)
*   `approval_status` (Enum)

### 6. `work_logs`
The link between Time and Scope.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `timesheet_id` (FK)
*   **`site_item_id` (FK)** - Links time to the specific BOM item.
*   `hours` (Decimal)
*   `milestone_id` (Text) - e.g., "Prime (30%)"

### 7. `suppliers` & `products`
The Catalog.
*   `suppliers`: Vendors.
*   `products`: Major items with `attributes` (JSONB) for spec matching and `image_url`.
*   `project_supplies`: Consumables list (brushes, tape).

# Hybrid Architecture Specification: Materials App + NocoDB

> **Goal**: Combine the rapid bulk-editing capabilities of a Spreadsheet/No-Code tool (NocoDB) with the specialized complex data handling of a Custom Web App, using a single PostgreSQL database as the source of truth.

## 1. Architecture Overview

```mermaid
graph TD
    DB[("PostgreSQL / Supabase")] <-->|Relational Data| NocoDB["NocoDB Spreadsheet UI"]
    DB <-->|JSONB Trees| App["Materials Admin App"]
    
    DB -.->|Flattened into| SQLView(view_project_bom)
    NocoDB -->|Read-Only View| SQLView
    
    NocoDB -->|Deep Link URL| App
    
    subgraph "Realtime Sync"
        DB -->|CDC / WebSocket| App
    end
```

## 2. Component Roles

| Component | Primary Role | Data Responsibility |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **PostgreSQL** | **The Brain** | Single Source of Truth for all data. |
| **NocoDB** | **The Spreadsheet** | Managing **Relational Data** (Taxonomy, Lists of Units, Users) and **Viewing** flattened Project Scopes. |
| **Materials App** | **The Specialist** | Managing **Deeply Nested JSON** (Project Scopes, Floorplan Definitions) and complex workflows. |

## 3. Data Integration Strategy

### 3.1 Taxonomy Management (Relational)
*   **Tables**: `taxonomies` (actions, lists, room_types).
*   **Workflow**: Users add new Room Types or Actions directly in **NocoDB**.
*   **Sync**: The App queries these tables via API. If NocoDB updates the DB, the App sees the new options immediately.

### 3.2 Scope Management (JSONB Trees)
The `project_definitions` table uses a `JSONB` column (`floorplan_definitions`) to store the nested tree of rooms and items. NocoDB cannot edit this easily.

#### Solution: The "Flattened" SQL View
We created a SQL View (`view_project_bom`) that "explodes" the JSON tree into rows:
`Plan A -> Kitchen -> Sink`  becomes  `| Plan A | Kitchen | Sink |`

*   **NocoDB Access**: Users open `view_project_bom` to see, sort, and search the entire project scope as a flat grid.
*   **Limitations**: This view is **Read-Only** in NocoDB because it is calculated data.

## 4. Deep Linking Workflow (The "Edit" Button)

To allow users to "Edit" the complex JSON data starting from NocoDB, we use a Deep Link pattern.

### The Workflow
1.  **User in NocoDB**: Spots an error in a row (e.g., "Kitchen Sink count is wrong").
2.  **Action**: Clicks the **"Edit in App"** link in that row.
3.  **System**: Opens the Materials App in a new browser tab.
4.  **App**: Reads the URL parameters (e.g., `?project_id=...&key=Kitchen`) and automatically navigates to and expands that specific item in the `JsonTreeEditor`.

### Implementation Details

#### A. Database / NocoDB
Create a **Virtual Column** (Formula) in NocoDB for the `projects` or `view_project_bom` table:

```sql
-- Conceptual Formula
CONCAT("http://localhost:3009/definitions?project_id=", {project_id})
```

#### B. Frontend App (`ProjectDefinitionsView.tsx`)
Update the component to check for URL Search Parameters on mount:

```typescript
// Pseudo-code implementation
onMount(() => {
    const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    const id = params.get('project_id');
    if (id) {
        setProjectId(id);
        fetchDefinitions(); // Auto-load
    }
});
```

## 5. Development Checklist

- [x] **Database**: Deployment of `view_project_bom` SQL View.
- [x] **NocoDB**: Connection to local Supabase instance.
- [ ] **Frontend**: Update `ProjectDefinitionsView.tsx` to handle `?project_id=xyz`.
- [ ] **NocoDB**: Configure the "Edit Link" formula column.

import { z } from 'zod';

// ============================================================================
// Work Orders Schemas
// ============================================================================

// Organization
export const OrganizationSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    name: z.string().min(1),
    slug: z.string().min(1),
    settings: z.record(z.any()).optional(),
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
    updated_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type Organization = z.infer<typeof OrganizationSchema>;

// WorkOrderUnit (renamed to avoid conflict with renovation.ts Unit)
export const WorkOrderUnitSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    organization_id: z.string().uuid(),
    unit_number: z.string().min(1),
    unit_type: z.string().nullable(),
    floor: z.number().int().nullable(),
    area_sf: z.number().nullable(),
    status: z.enum(['not_started', 'in_progress', 'complete']),
    metadata: z.record(z.any()).optional(),
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
    updated_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type WorkOrderUnit = z.infer<typeof WorkOrderUnitSchema>;

// Room
export const RoomSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    organization_id: z.string().uuid(),
    name: z.string().min(1),
    display_order: z.number().int(),
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type Room = z.infer<typeof RoomSchema>;

// Template Column Definition
export const TemplateColumnSchema = z.object({
    key: z.string(),
    label: z.string(),
    type: z.enum(['checkbox', 'text', 'number', 'date']),
    synonyms: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    default_na: z.boolean().optional(), // Default to "Not Applicable"
    tooltip: z.string().optional(),
});

export type TemplateColumn = z.infer<typeof TemplateColumnSchema>;

// Template Section
export const TemplateSectionSchema = z.object({
    label: z.string(),
    columns: z.array(TemplateColumnSchema),
});

export type TemplateSection = z.infer<typeof TemplateSectionSchema>;

// Work Order Template Definition (JSONB structure)
export const TemplateDefinitionSchema = z.object({
    sections: z.record(TemplateSectionSchema),
    logic: z.object({
        rules: z.array(z.object({
            if: z.record(z.any()),
            then: z.record(z.any()),
        })),
    }).optional(),
});

export type TemplateDefinition = z.infer<typeof TemplateDefinitionSchema>;

// Work Order Template
export const WorkOrderTemplateSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    organization_id: z.string().uuid(),
    trade_name: z.string().min(1),
    version: z.number().int(),
    is_active: z.boolean(),
    definition: TemplateDefinitionSchema,
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
    updated_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type WorkOrderTemplate = z.infer<typeof WorkOrderTemplateSchema>;

// Work Order
export const WorkOrderSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    organization_id: z.string().uuid(),
    unit_id: z.string().uuid(),
    template_id: z.string().uuid(),
    trade_name: z.string().min(1),
    status: z.enum(['pending', 'in_progress', 'complete', 'on_hold']),
    assigned_contractor_id: z.string().uuid().nullable(),
    inspector_id: z.string().uuid().nullable(),
    start_date: z.string().datetime().nullable(),
    completion_date: z.string().datetime().nullable(),
    inspector_signoff_date: z.string().datetime().nullable(),
    metadata: z.record(z.any()).optional(),
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
    updated_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type WorkOrder = z.infer<typeof WorkOrderSchema>;

// Task Step (within task_data JSONB)
export const TaskStepSchema = z.object({
    key: z.string(),
    label: z.string(),
    status: z.enum(['pending', 'complete', 'blocked']),
    completed_by: z.string().uuid().nullable().optional(),
    completed_at: z.number().nullable().optional(), // Unix timestamp
    not_applicable: z.boolean().optional(),
});

export type TaskStep = z.infer<typeof TaskStepSchema>;

// Work Order Task Data (JSONB structure)
export const TaskDataSchema = z.object({
    steps: z.array(TaskStepSchema),
});

export type TaskData = z.infer<typeof TaskDataSchema>;

// Work Order Task
export const WorkOrderTaskSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    work_order_id: z.string().uuid(),
    room_id: z.string().uuid(),
    row_order: z.number().int(),
    task_data: TaskDataSchema,
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
    updated_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type WorkOrderTask = z.infer<typeof WorkOrderTaskSchema>;

// Work Order Note
export const WorkOrderNoteSchema = z.object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    work_order_id: z.string().uuid(),
    author_id: z.string().uuid(),
    content: z.string().min(1),
    created_at: z.string().datetime(),
    updated_at: z.string().datetime(),
});

export type WorkOrderNote = z.infer<typeof WorkOrderNoteSchema>;

// ============================================================================
// API Request/Response Schemas
// ============================================================================

// Create Work Order Request
export const CreateWorkOrderRequestSchema = z.object({
    unit_id: z.string().uuid(),
    template_id: z.string().uuid(),
    assigned_contractor_id: z.string().uuid().optional(),
});

export type CreateWorkOrderRequest = z.infer<typeof CreateWorkOrderRequestSchema>;

// Update Task Step Request
export const UpdateTaskStepRequestSchema = z.object({
    step_key: z.string(),
    status: z.enum(['pending', 'complete', 'blocked']),
    not_applicable: z.boolean().optional(),
});

export type UpdateTaskStepRequest = z.infer<typeof UpdateTaskStepRequestSchema>;

// Create Note Request
export const CreateNoteRequestSchema = z.object({
    content: z.string().min(1),
});

export type CreateNoteRequest = z.infer<typeof CreateNoteRequestSchema>;

import { z } from 'zod';

// --- Suppliers ---
export const SupplierSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().uuid(),
  name: z.string().min(1, "Name is required"),
  website: z.string().url().optional().or(z.literal('')),
  contact_info: z.record(z.any()).optional(), // JSONB
  created_at: z.string().nullish()
});

export const CreateSupplierSchema = SupplierSchema.omit({
  id: true,
  created_at: true
});

export type Supplier = z.infer<typeof SupplierSchema>;
export type CreateSupplier = z.infer<typeof CreateSupplierSchema>;


// --- Products (Major Items) ---
export const ProductSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().uuid(),
  supplier_id: z.string().uuid(),
  subject_key: z.string().min(1, "Subject key is required"), // e.g. "gas_stove"
  name: z.string().min(1, "Name is required"),
  model_number: z.string().nullish(),
  description: z.string().nullish(),
  attributes: z.record(z.any()).default({}), // JSONB for spec matching
  unit_price: z.number().min(0),
  product_page_url: z.string().nullish(),
  image_url: z.string().nullish(),
  notes: z.string().nullish(),
  availability_status: z.enum(['in_stock', 'order', 'discontinued']).default('in_stock'),
  created_at: z.string().nullish()
});

export const CreateProductSchema = ProductSchema.omit({
  id: true,
  created_at: true
});

export type Product = z.infer<typeof ProductSchema>;
export type CreateProduct = z.infer<typeof CreateProductSchema>;


// --- Project Supplies (Consumables) ---
export const ProjectSupplySchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().uuid(),
  supplier_id: z.string().uuid(),
  name: z.string().min(1, "Name is required"),
  description: z.string().optional(),
  product_page_url: z.string().nullish(),
  image_url: z.string().nullish(),
  notes: z.string().optional(),
  created_at: z.string().nullish()
});

export const CreateProjectSupplySchema = ProjectSupplySchema.omit({
  id: true,
  created_at: true
});

export type ProjectSupply = z.infer<typeof ProjectSupplySchema>;
export type CreateProjectSupply = z.infer<typeof CreateProjectSupplySchema>;

import { z } from 'zod';

export const UnitStatusEnum = z.enum(['pending', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'verified']);

export const UnitSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().uuid(),
  project_id: z.string().uuid().nullable(),
  unit_number: z.string(),
  unit_type: z.string().nullable(),
  floor_plan_image: z.string().nullable(),
  created_at: z.string().datetime().optional()
});

export const UnitScopeItemSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().uuid(),
  unit_id: z.string().uuid(),
  zone: z.string().nullable(),
  category: z.string().nullable(),
  item: z.string().nullable(),
  action: z.string().nullable(),
  trade: z.string().nullable(),
  notes: z.string().nullable(),
  status: UnitStatusEnum.default('pending'),
  created_at: z.string().datetime().optional()
});

export type Unit = z.infer<typeof UnitSchema>;
export type UnitScopeItem = z.infer<typeof UnitScopeItemSchema>;
export type UnitStatus = z.infer<typeof UnitStatusEnum>;

# Construction Voice Platform (Scaffold)

> **Current Status**: 🚧 Clean Scaffold / Starter Kit
> This repository is a configured foundation for building a high-performance, distributed application suite. It is not yet a feature-complete product.

## What is this?

This is a **Modern Monorepo Architecture** designed for teams building a "Collection of Apps" that share a single data model and "Brain".

It is pre-configured to build:
1.  **Admin Dashboards**: High-density office interfaces (SolidJS + Vite).
2.  **Field Companion Apps**: Offline-capable mobile PWAs for workers (SolidJS).
3.  **Voice-Enabled APIs**: A backend optimized for Audio processing, LLM extraction, and Realtime data (Hono).
4.  **Native Mobile Apps**: Ready for Flutter integration via OpenAPI generation.

## Why this stack?

*   **Type Safety**: If you change the database, the Mobile App break at *compile time*, not in production.
*   **Speed**: Uses `pnpm` and `Turborepo` for instant builds.
*   **Reactivity**: Uses `SolidJS` for raw performance (no Virtual DOM).
*   **Unified Logic**: Shared `packages/schemas` ensure the API and Frontend always speak the same language.

## Directory Structure

```text
/
├── apps/
│   ├── api/             # Hono Backend (The Brain)
│   │                    #  - Exposes OpenAPI / Swagger
│   │                    #  - Handles Voice Processing & Auth
│   ├── web-dashboard/   # SolidJS (Office Admin Admin)
│   ├── site-companion/  # SolidJS PWA (Mobile Field Worker)
│   └── mobile-flutter/  # (Placeholder) Future Native App
├── packages/
│   ├── schemas/         # THE SOURCE OF TRUTH (Zod definitions)
│   ├── db/              # Database Types (Auto-generated from Supabase)
│   └── ui/              # Shared Design System
```

## Where to Begin Development?

**1. The Mental Model: "Schema First"**
Don't start by building a button. Start by defining the **Data**.
*   **Step 1**: Go to `packages/schemas/src`. Define what a "Task" or "Material" looks like.
*   **Step 2**: Go to `apps/api/src/routes`. Create an endpoint that accepts that Schema.
*   **Step 3**: Go to `apps/web`. Build the UI that sends that Data.

**2. Setup**
```bash
# 1. Install dependencies
pnpm install

# 2. Start the Database (Docker)
npx supabase start

### 3. Start Development
```bash
pnpm dev
```
This runs all apps in parallel.
*   **API**: http://localhost:3000
*   **Web Dashboard**: http://localhost:3001
*   **Material Admin**: http://localhost:3002
*   *(See [docs/ports.md](./docs/ports.md) for full list)*

**3. Useful Commands**
*   `pnpm db:types`: Run this after you add a table to Supabase. It updates the TypeScript definitions in configured packages.
*   `http://localhost:3000/ui`: View the Swagger API documentation.

## Documentation
See the `/docs` folder for deep dives:
*   [A2A Protocol (Agent-to-Agent)](./docs/a2a/README.md) - JSON-RPC endpoint for AI agent interop
*   [Gemini Plan / Handbook](./docs/gemini_plan.md)
*   [Flutter Integration](./docs/flutter_integration.md)
*   [Architecture Explainer](./docs/understanding_architecture.md)

## 🔧 Troubleshooting & Known Issues

### Supabase Local Config
If `supabase start` fails with parsing errors, ensure `supabase/config.toml` does **not** contain deprecated keys for your CLI version:
*   `deno_version`: Comment this out if using standard CLI.
*   `health_timeout`: Comment this out if causing parsing errors.
*   `major_version`: Must match your valid data directory (default is **17**, do not downgrade to 15 unless starting fresh).

### Realtime Updates Not Appearing
If the Dashboard connects (`SUBSCRIBED`) but receives no data:
1.  **RLS Policies**: Realtime respects RLS. If you are "Anonymous", strict policies (`uid=user_id`) will hide data. Use a permissive policy for testing or ensure `Authorization` headers are sent.
2.  **Versioning**: Ensure the table is in the `supabase_realtime` publication (`alter publication supabase_realtime add table ...`).

### Login/Seed Issues
`supabase db reset` might fail on complex seed files. Use the API scripts in `apps/api/scripts/` to reliably seed data:
*   `create_user.ts`: Creates the default 'Alice' user.

# Document Parser Service (FastAPI)

This service provides a GPU-accelerated document parsing API using **Detectron2**, **LayoutParser**, and **PyMuPDF**. It extracts text, renders pages to images (uploaded to Supabase), and identifies structured data like tables and landmarks (floor plans).

## Getting Started

The service is configured to run automatically as a FastAPI server on port **5000** when you start the container.

### 1. Configure Environment
Ensure your `docker-compose.yml` has the correct `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`.

### 2. Start the Service
```bash
cd doc-parser
docker compose up -d
```

## API Endpoints

### `GET /`
Health check endpoint. Returns GPU availability and Supabase connection status.

### `POST /parse`
Main parsing endpoint.
- **Form Data**:
    - `project_name`: The name of the project (used for Supabase storage paths).
    - `file`: The PDF or image file to parse.

**Returns**: JSON containing page metadata, text content, image URLs, and detected landmarks.

## Integration Examples

### Node.js (using fetch)

Instead of executing commands directly, you can now call the service via HTTP. This is much faster and more robust.

```javascript
const fs = require('fs');
const FormData = require('form-data');

async function parseDocument(projectId, filePath) {
    const form = new FormData();
    form.append('project_name', projectId);
    form.append('file', fs.createReadStream(filePath));

    try {
        const response = await fetch('http://localhost:5000/parse', {
            method: 'POST',
            body: form,
            headers: form.getHeaders()
        });

        if (!response.ok) {
            throw new Error(`Error: ${response.statusText}`);
        }

        const data = await response.json();
        console.log('Parsed Data:', data);
        return data;
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Parsing failed:', error);
    }
}
```

## Cross-Container Networking

If you are trying to reach this service from another Docker container (e.g., **Agent Zero** or another microservice), you have two main options:

### 1. Using `host.docker.internal` (Recommended for Windows/Mac)
If your other container is also running on Docker Desktop, it can reach the host machine using a special DNS name. Use this address in your other container:
- **Address**: `http://host.docker.internal:5000/parse`

### 2. Using a Shared Docker Network (Cleaner)
You can create a shared network so containers can talk to each other using their service names.

**Step 1: Create a network (if you don't have one)**:
```bash
docker network create agent-zero-network
```

**Step 2: Update `docker-compose.yml`**:
Add the network to your `doc-parser` service:
```yaml
services:
  doc-parser:
    ...
    networks:
      - agent-zero-network

networks:
  agent-zero-network:
    external: true
```

**Step 3: Access from another container**:
Once the other container is also joined to `agent-zero-network`, it can reach this service using the service name as the hostname:
- **Address**: `http://doc-parser:5000/parse`

## Development

The container is set up with `--reload`, so changes to `server.py` will automatically restart the FastAPI server inside the container.

import { config } from 'dotenv';
import { resolve } from 'path';

// Load .env from project root (two levels up from apps/api/src)
config({ path: resolve(__dirname, '../../../.env') });
import { serve } from '@hono/node-server';
import { OpenAPIHono } from '@hono/zod-openapi';
import { swaggerUI } from '@hono/swagger-ui';
import { logger } from 'hono/logger';
import { cors } from 'hono/cors';
import { z } from 'zod';
console.log('[API] Zod Version:', (z as any).version || 'undefined');

// Import Routes
import { deliveryRoute } from './routes/delivery';
import { todosRoute } from './routes/todos';
import { voiceRoute } from './routes/voice';
import materials from './routes/materials';
import taxonomyRoute from './routes/taxonomy';
import projectDefinitionsRoute from './routes/project-definitions';
import unitsRoute from './routes/units';
import { workOrdersRoute } from './routes/work-orders';
import { documentsRoute } from './routes/documents';
import { a2aRoute } from './routes/a2a';

const app = new OpenAPIHono();

// Middleware
app.use('*', logger());
app.use('*', cors());

// Routes
app.route('/delivery', deliveryRoute);
app.route('/todos', todosRoute);
app.route('/voice', voiceRoute);
app.route('/materials', materials);
app.route('/taxonomy', taxonomyRoute);
app.route('/projects', projectDefinitionsRoute);
app.route('/units', unitsRoute);
app.route('/work-orders', workOrdersRoute);
app.route('/work-orders', documentsRoute);

// A2A Protocol (Agent-to-Agent)
app.route('', a2aRoute);       // /.well-known/agent.json
app.route('/a2a', a2aRoute);   // POST /a2a (JSON-RPC endpoint)

// The OpenAPI Documentation JSON
app.doc('/doc', {
  openapi: '3.0.0',
  info: {
    version: '1.0.0',
    title: 'Construction Voice Platform API',
    description: 'API for Web Dashboard, Site Companion, and Flutter Mobile App'
  },
});

// The Swagger UI (Visual Documentation)
app.get('/ui', swaggerUI({ url: '/doc' }));

const port = 3000;
console.log(`Server is running on port ${port}`);
console.log(`Swagger UI available at http://localhost:${port}/ui`);

serve({
  fetch: app.fetch,
  port
});

export type AppType = typeof app;

import { Component } from 'solid-js';
import { Router, Route } from '@solidjs/router';
import { isSupabaseConfigured } from './lib/supabase';

// Pages
import UnitList from './pages/UnitList';
import UnitDetail from './pages/UnitDetail';
import VoiceTest from './pages/VoiceTest';

const Layout: Component = (props: any) => {
    return (
        <div style={{ "min-height": "100vh", "background-color": "#f7fafc" }}>
            <nav style={{ padding: "1rem", "background-color": "#ffffff", "border-bottom": "1px solid #e2e8f0", display: "flex", gap: "1rem" }}>
                <a href="/units" style={{ "font-weight": "bold", "text-decoration": "none", color: "#2d3748" }}>🏢 Units</a>
                <a href="/voice" style={{ "font-weight": "bold", "text-decoration": "none", color: "#2d3748" }}>🎤 Voice Test</a>
            </nav>
            <main style={{ padding: "1rem" }}>
                {props.children}
            </main>
        </div>
    );
};

const App: Component = () => {
    return (
        <>
            {!isSupabaseConfigured && (
                <div style={{ "background-color": "#e53e3e", color: "white", padding: "1rem", "text-align": "center", "font-weight": "bold" }}>
                    CONFIG ERROR: Missing Supabase Environment Variables. Check .env file.
                </div>
            )}
            <Router root={Layout}>
                <Route path="/" component={UnitList} />
                <Route path="/units" component={UnitList} />
                <Route path="/units/:id" component={UnitDetail} />
                <Route path="/voice" component={VoiceTest} />
            </Router>
        </>
    );
};

export default App;

-- ============================================================================
-- Work Orders Foundation Schema
-- Multi-tenant construction project management system
-- ============================================================================

-- Organizations (Multi-tenancy)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.organizations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    slug TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    settings JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

-- Units (Physical spaces being renovated)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.units (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    unit_number TEXT NOT NULL,
    unit_type TEXT, -- e.g., "A", "B", "C" (from architectural drawings)
    floor INTEGER,
    area_sf NUMERIC,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_started', -- not_started, in_progress, complete
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, -- For unit-specific notes, special instructions
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(organization_id, unit_number)
);

-- Rooms (Spaces within units)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.rooms (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL, -- e.g., "Kitchen", "Bedroom", "Bathroom"
    display_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

-- Work Order Templates (Definitions of work for each trade)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.work_order_templates (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    trade_name TEXT NOT NULL, -- e.g., "Painting", "Carpentry", "Plumbing"
    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    definition JSONB NOT NULL, -- The template structure (columns, logic rules, etc.)
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(organization_id, trade_name, version)
);

-- Work Orders (Instance of a template for a specific unit)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.work_orders (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    unit_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.units(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    template_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.work_order_templates(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    trade_name TEXT NOT NULL, -- Denormalized for easy filtering
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending, in_progress, complete, on_hold
    assigned_contractor_id UUID, -- References auth.users (contractor)
    inspector_id UUID, -- References auth.users (inspector)
    start_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    completion_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,

-- ============================================================================
-- This migration implements a three-tier template hierarchy:

-- 3. Project-level (project-specific)

BEGIN;

-- ============================================================================
-- 1. CREATE PROJECTS TABLE (Work Orders)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS wo_projects (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'completed', 'archived')),
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    
    CONSTRAINT unique_wo_project_name_per_org UNIQUE (organization_id, name)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_wo_projects_org ON wo_projects(organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_wo_projects_status ON wo_projects(status);

COMMENT ON TABLE wo_projects IS 'Work Order Projects/properties within an organization';

-- ============================================================================
-- 2. UPDATE WORK_ORDER_TEMPLATES TABLE
-- ============================================================================

-- Add new columns
ALTER TABLE work_order_templates
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS scope TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'company'
        CHECK (scope IN ('app', 'company', 'project')),
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS project_id UUID REFERENCES wo_projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parent_template_id UUID REFERENCES work_order_templates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS copied_from_template_id UUID REFERENCES work_order_templates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS updated_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id);

-- Make organization_id nullable (for app-level templates)
ALTER TABLE work_order_templates
    ALTER COLUMN organization_id DROP NOT NULL;

-- Add constraints
ALTER TABLE work_order_templates
    ADD CONSTRAINT valid_app_scope CHECK (
        (scope = 'app' AND organization_id IS NULL AND project_id IS NULL)
        OR scope != 'app'
    ),
    ADD CONSTRAINT valid_company_scope CHECK (
        (scope = 'company' AND organization_id IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL)

    ),

# Understanding the Monorepo & Supabase

You asked some great questions about the folder structure. Here is exactly what is happening under the hood.

## 1. The Mystery Folder: `node_modules/@repo/tsconfig`

You noticed this path:
`packages/schemas/node_modules/@repo/tsconfig`

### What is it?
This is a **Symlink** (Symbolic Link). Think of it as a "Portal" or "Shortcut".

### Why is it there?
In `packages/schemas/package.json`, we have this dependency:
```json
"devDependencies": {
  "@repo/tsconfig": "workspace:*"
}
```
The `workspace:*` tells pnpm: "Don't go to the internet (npm registry) to find this. Look in my modules folder."

So, pnpm creates a shortcut:
*   **From**: `packages/schemas/node_modules/@repo/tsconfig`
*   **To**: `packages/tsconfig` (The actual folder where your JSON lives)

**Benefit**: If you edit `packages/tsconfig/base.json`, the changes apply *instantly* to `packages/schemas` because they are literally looking at the same file on your disk.

---

## 2. "Installing" Supabase

Supabase is unique. It is not just a library; it is a full **Infrastructure Stack** (Postgres Database, Auth Server, Realtime Server, Storage Server).

### How to Install it
You don't "npm install" the database itself. You run it using **Docker**.

1.  **Initialize Config** (I am doing this for you now):
    ```bash
    npx supabase init
    ```
    This creates a `/supabase` folder with a `config.toml`.

2.  **Start the Engine**:
    ```bash
    npx supabase start
    ```
    This command:
    *   Downloads Docker images for Postgres, Auth, etc.
    *   Spins them up on your computer.
    *   Gives you an **API URL** and **Anon Key**.

---

## 3. The Relationship: `packages/db` vs. "Supabase"

It is easy to confuse the **Tool** with the **Code**.

| Component | What it is | Where it lives |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Supabase Service** | The Engine. Holds your actual data (Users, Tasks). | **Docker Deployment** (or Cloud) |

# Construction Voice Platform: Comprehensive Developer Handbook

> [!NOTE]
> This document serves as the master architectural guide for the Construction Voice Platform. It is designed to be a living document that teaches *why* we made these technical choices, not just *how* to use them.

## 1. The Philosophy: Lean, Type-Safe, and Distributed

We are building a **Collection of Apps**, not a monolithic disaster. A construction company needs a Dashboard for the office, a Mobile App for the site, and potentially a Kiosk for the warehouse. These apps are different, but they share the same DNA: the **Data Model**.

### Core Principles

1.  **Monorepo (Single Source of Truth)**: We use a monorepo so that when we update a data model (e.g., "A Brick now weighs 2kg"), that change propagates instantly to the Backend, the Dashboard, and the Mobile App. No desyncs. No "it works on my machine."
2.  **Schema-First Development**: We do not write code until we define the Shape of the data. We use **Zod** as our contract. If it's not in the Zod schema, it doesn't exist.
3.  **Visual Grounding**: Voice without context is useless. When a user says "Delete it," our system must know what "it" is by inspecting the user's screen state.
4.  **Local-First / Edge-Ready**: We develop locally with Supabase and Hono, but our architecture is designed to run on the Edge (Cloudflare Workers) for low latency.

---

## 2. The High-Performance Stack

This stack is chosen for speed (both runtime and development velocity) and type safety.

| Component | Technology | Why? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Monorepo Manager** | **Turborepo** + **pnpm** | Fast builds, efficient caching, and top-tier workspace support. |
| **Frontend** | **SolidJS** | True reactivity (signals) without the virtual DOM overhead. Performance defaults. |
| **Backend** | **Hono** | Ultra-lightweight web standard compliant framework. Runs anywhere (Node, Cloudflare, Deno). |
| **API Communication** | **Hono RPC** | End-to-end type safety without generating SDKs. The frontend imports the backend's *types*. |
| **Database & Auth** | **Supabase** | Postgres power with easy Auth, Realtime subscriptions, and Storage. |
| **Validation** | **Zod** | Runtime validation + Static Type inference. The backbone of our system. |
| **Voice Processing** | **OpenAI Whisper** + **LLM** | High-fidelity transcription followed by structured data extraction. |

---

## 3. Project Architecture

The directory structure is critical. It separates "Runnable Apps" from "Shared Logic".

```text
/construction-voice-platform
├── /apps                     # EXECUTABLE APPLICATIONS
│   ├── /web-dashboard       # SolidJS (Admin/Office view)
│   ├── /site-companion      # SolidJS PWA (Mobile view for workers)
│   └── /api                 # Hono server (The Brain / Voice Orchestrator)
├── /packages                 # SHARED LOGIC (The "Glue")
│   ├── /db                  # Supabase generated types + DB utilities
│   ├── /schemas             # THE CONTRACT (Shared Zod schemas)
│   ├── /ui                  # Shared SolidJS components (Buttons, Layouts)
│   └── /tsconfig            # Shared TS configurations
├── /supabase                 # Database config
│   ├── config.toml
│   └── /migrations          # SQL files (Single source of truth for DB structure)
├── /docs                     # Documentation (ADRs, Guides)
├── turbo.json                # Build orchestration config
├── package.json              # Root dependencies
└── README.md
```

> [!TIP]
> **Junior Dev Tip**: Never import from `apps/` into `packages/`. Logic flows *down* from packages to apps. Packages are stateless utilities; Apps are stateful implementations.

# Port Allocation Contract

To avoid conflicts during development, we strictly assign specific ports to each application in the monorepo.

## Assigned Ports

| Port      | Application         | Path                  | Notes                                                                 |
| :-------- | :------------------ | :-------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **3000**  | **API**             | `apps/api`            | The Backend (Hono) + A2A Protocol (`/a2a`, `/.well-known/agent.json`) |
| **3001**  | **Web Dashboard**   | `apps/web-dashboard`  | Admin/Office View (Legacy/Reference)                                  |
| **3002**  | **Material Admin**  | `apps/material-admin` | Material & Taxonomy Manager                                           |
| **3003**  | **Site Companion**  | `apps/site-companion` | Mobile Companion App (Voice Log)                                      |
| **3004**  | **Work Orders**     | `apps/work-orders`    | Work Orders Management                                                |
| **5000**  | **Doc Parser**      | `doc-parser`          | Python FastAPI Service for LayoutParser                               |
| **8082**  | **NocoDB**          | -                     | No-code Database Interface (Internal Tools)                           |
| **54321** | **Supabase**        | -                     | Local Database (Postgres)                                             |
| **54323** | **Supabase Studio** | -                     | Local Dashboard UI                                                    |
| **5080**  | **Agent Zero**      | `agent-zero`          | Agent Zero                                                            |

## Rules

1.  **Strict Ports**: In `vite.config.ts`, always set `server.port` to the assigned value.
2.  **No Random Ports**: Do not let Vite pick a random port. This breaks CORS configurations.
3.  **Check this doc**: Before creating a new app, claim the next available port here.

## Troubleshooting

### Windows "Zombie" Processes

If you encounter `EADDRINUSE` or random port assignments (e.g., 3009 instead of 3002), it is likely that a previous Node process is still running in the background.

**Fix**: Run this command in PowerShell to Force-Kill all Node processes:

```powershell
taskkill /F /IM node.exe
```

---

## Docker / Compose port mapping (local development)

The repository provides a `docker-compose.yml` (root of `agent-zero`) that maps the service ports for the single "agent-zero" service. By default the compose file maps:

- Container port 80 -> Host 80
- Container port 9000 -> Host 9000
- Container port 22 -> Host 22

If you run multiple services from the monorepo in parallel, ensure these host ports are free or modify the `docker-compose.yml` port mappings to avoid conflicts.

Tip: use `docker compose ps` and `docker compose logs -f <service>` to inspect running services and assigned ports.

|\n\n---\n\n## 3. Data Model\n\n### 3.1 Entity Relationship Summary\n\n```\nOrganization (tenant)\n  ├── Units (physical spaces: apartments, condos)\n  │     ├── Unit Rooms (rooms within a unit + measurements)\n  │     ├── Unit Documents (linked architectural drawings)\n  │     └── Work Orders (one per trade per unit)\n  │           ├── Work Order Tasks (room × template checklist)\n  │           └── Work Order Notes (comments, voice logs)\n  │\n  ├── Rooms (master list: Kitchen, Bathroom, Bedroom...)\n  │\n  ├── Work Order Templates (trade checklists)\n  │     └── Template Definition (JSONB: sections → columns → steps)\n  │\n  ├── WO Projects (properties/job sites)\n  │     ├── Project Documents (uploaded PDFs, drawings)\n  │     │     └── Document Images (extracted pages/floor plans)\n  │     └── Project-scoped Template overrides\n  │\n  └── Template Libraries (importable template collections)\n```\n\n### 3.2 The Template Hierarchy (Three-Tier)\n\nThis is the most architecturally significant design decision in the system:\n\n| Tier | Scope | Purpose | Example |\n|------|-------|---------|--------|\n| **App** | System-wide | Factory defaults, read-only | \"Standard Painting Checklist v1\" |\n| **Company** | Per organization | Tenant customizations | \"Acme Painting Checklist\" (adds extra steps) |\n| **Project** | Per wo_project | Job-specific overrides | \"123 Main St Painting\" (removes N/A rooms) |\n\nTemplates cascade: a project template inherits from its company parent, which inherits from the app default. The `parent_template_id` and `copied_from_template_id` columns track lineage.\n\n### 3.3 The JSONB Strategy\n\nRelational tables handle entities that need indexing, joins, and permissions. JSONB handles deeply nested hierarchical data:\n\n| Table | JSONB Column | Contains |\n|-------|-------------|----------|\n| `work_order_templates` | `definition` | `{ sections: { \"Prep\": { columns: [...] }, \"Finish\": { columns: [...] } }, logic: { rules: [...] } }` |\n| `work_order_tasks` | `task_data` | `{ steps: [{ key, label, status, completed_by, completed_at, not_applicable }] }` |\n| `units` | `metadata` | Flexible unit-specific notes, special instructions |\n| `project_definitions` | `floorplan_definitions` | Nested room/item trees per floor plan type |\n| `products` | `attributes` | Spec matching data (dimensions, colors, materials) |\n\nA SQL view (`view_project_bom`) flattens the JSONB trees into rows for NocoDB consumption.\n\n### 3.4 Key Database Tables (Public Schema)\n\n**Core Business:**\n- `organizations` — Multi-tenant root\n- `units` — Physical spaces (apartments)\n- `rooms` — Room type catalog\n- `wo_projects` — Job sites/properties\n- `work_order_templates` — Trade checklist definitions\n- `work_orders` — Template instances per unit\n- `work_order_tasks` — Room-level checklist execution\n- `work_order_notes` — Comments and voice log entries\n- `template_libraries` — Importable template collections\n\n**Documents & Media:**\n- `project_documents` — Uploaded PDFs, drawings\n- `document_images` — Extracted page images\n- `unit_documents` — Links documents to units\n- `image_links` — Links images to any entity\n- `unit_rooms` — Room instances with measurements\n- `unit_room_measurements` — Detailed dimension data\n- `unit_room_calculations` — Computed areas/volumes\n\n**Materials (Planned/Partial):**\n- `metric_definitions` — Measurement metrics\n\n**NocoDB (~90 tables):**\n- `nc_*` — NocoDB's internal metadata for its spreadsheet interface\n\n---\n\n## 4. Shared Packages\n\n### 4.1 `packages/schemas` — The Contract\n\nThe most critical package. Every data shape is defined here as Zod schemas:\n\n| File | Exports | Used By |\n|------|---------|--------|\n| `work-orders.ts` | Organization, Unit, Room, Template, WorkOrder, Task, Note schemas + API request/response types | API routes, Work Orders app |\n| `materials.ts` | Supplier, Product, ProjectSupply schemas | Material Admin, API |\n| `renovation.ts` | Unit, UnitScopeItem schemas (legacy?) | Site Companion |\n| `taxonomy.ts` | Taxonomy categories (actions, room types) | Material Admin, API |\n| `delivery.ts` | Delivery tracking schemas | API |\n| `todo.ts` | Simple todo schemas | Web Dashboard |\n| `zod.ts` | Shared Zod utilities | All |\n\n### 4.2 `packages/config` — Centralized Configuration\n\nHardcoded Supabase URLs, API keys, port assignments, and CORS origins. All apps import from here for consistency.\n\n### 4.3 `packages/db` — Database Types\n\nAuto-generated TypeScript types from Supabase schema via `pnpm db:types`. Provides compile-time safety for direct database queries.\n\n### 4.4 `packages/ui` — Shared Components\n\nReusable SolidJS components (buttons, layouts) for consistent design across apps.\n\n### 4.5 `packages/logger` — Structured Logging\n\nShared logging utility for consistent output formatting.\n\n### 4.6 `packages/tsconfig` — TypeScript Configuration\n\nBase TypeScript configuration inherited by all packages and apps via `workspace:*` symlinks.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Applications\n\n### 5.1 API (`apps/api`) — The Brain\n\n**Framework**: Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js\n**Port**: 3000\n**Key Dependencies**: `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `@supabase/supabase-js`, `pdfjs-dist`, `@napi-rs/canvas`\n\n**Routes:**\n\n| Route | Purpose | Key Operations |\n|-------|---------|----------------|\n| `/work-orders` | Work order CRUD | Create from template, update task steps, manage lifecycle |\n| `/work-orders` (documents) | Document management | Upload PDFs, link to projects/units |\n| `/materials` | Supplier/product catalog | CRUD for suppliers, products, project supplies |\n| `/taxonomy` | Category management | Actions, room types, item categories |\n| `/projects` | Project definitions | JSONB scope trees, floorplan definitions |\n| `/units` | Unit management | CRUD with metadata, scope items |\n| `/voice` | Voice processing | Audio → Whisper transcription → LLM structured extraction |\n| `/delivery` | Delivery tracking | Delivery scheduling and status |\n| `/todos` | Simple todos | Basic CRUD (reference/demo) |\n| `/a2a` | Agent-to-Agent protocol | JSON-RPC endpoint for AI agent interop |\n| `/ui` | Swagger UI | Interactive API documentation |\n| `/doc` | OpenAPI JSON | Machine-readable API spec |\n\n**Services Layer** (`src/services/`): Business logic separated from route handlers.\n**Middleware** (`src/middleware/`): Auth, validation, error handling.\n**Scripts** (`scripts/`): 50+ utility scripts for seeding, migration, testing, and verification.\n\n### 5.2 Work Orders App (`apps/work-orders`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS + Vite\n**Port**: 3004\n**Purpose**: Primary management interface for work order lifecycle\n\n**Pages:**\n- `Dashboard` — Overview of all work orders, status summaries\n- `Units` — Browse and manage physical spaces\n- `Templates` — View/edit work order template definitions\n- `TemplateDetail` — Deep dive into a single template's sections and columns\n- `WorkOrderDetail` — Execute a work order: room-by-room checklist with step completion\n- `Projects` — Manage wo_projects (job sites)\n- `ProjectDetail` — Single project view with units, work orders, documents\n- `NewProject` — Project creation wizard\n- `ProjectDocuments` — Upload and manage architectural drawings/PDFs\n\n### 5.3 Site Companion (`apps/site-companion`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS PWA\n**Port**: 3003\n**Purpose**: Mobile-optimized field worker interface\n\n**Pages:**\n- `UnitList` — Browse assigned units\n- `UnitDetail` — View unit scope, execute work items\n- `VoiceTest` — Voice recording → transcription → structured data extraction\n\n**Key Feature**: Designed as a Progressive Web App for offline-capable field use. Workers can record voice memos that get transcribed and parsed into structured work log entries.\n\n### 5.4 Material Admin (`apps/material-admin`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS\n**Port**: 3002\n**Purpose**: Supplier catalog, product management, taxonomy editing\n\n**Features:**\n- Supplier CRUD with contact info\n- Product catalog with JSONB attributes for spec matching\n- Project supplies (consumables) management\n- Taxonomy editing (room types, actions, categories)\n- JSON tree editor for complex nested definitions\n- BOM (Bill of Materials) flattened view\n\n### 5.5 Web Dashboard (`apps/web-dashboard`)\n\n**Framework**: SolidJS (minimal)\n**Port**: 3001\n**Purpose**: Legacy/reference admin view\n\n**Features:**\n- Login via Supabase Auth\n- Basic todo management (reference implementation)\n- Hono RPC client integration example\n\n---\n\n## 6. Supporting Services\n\n### 6.1 Doc Parser (`doc-parser/`)\n\n**Framework**: FastAPI (Python)\n**Port**: 5000\n**Infrastructure**: Docker with GPU support (Detectron2, LayoutParser)\n\n**Capabilities:**\n- PDF upload and page rendering\n- Layout analysis (text blocks, tables, figures)\n- Floor plan detection and extraction\n- Image upload to Supabase Storage\n- Text extraction via PyMuPDF\n\n**Integration**: Called via HTTP from the Hono API or directly from Agent Zero. Uploads extracted images to Supabase Storage and returns structured metadata.\n\n### 6.2 NocoDB (`nocodb/`)\n\n**Port**: 8082\n**Purpose**: Spreadsheet interface directly connected to the Supabase PostgreSQL database\n\n**Use Cases:**\n- Bulk editing relational data (units, rooms, taxonomy)\n- Viewing flattened SQL views of JSONB data (`view_project_bom`)\n- Ad-hoc queries without building custom UI\n- Deep linking to the Material Admin app for complex JSONB editing\n\n### 6.3 A2A Protocol\n\nThe API exposes an Agent-to-Agent JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a` with an agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`. This enables:\n- External AI agents (like Agent Zero) to discover and interact with the platform\n- Structured task delegation (e.g., \"create a work order for unit 101\")\n- Multi-agent workflows across different AI systems\n\n---\n\n## 7. Current State Assessment\n\n### 7.1 What's Built and Working\n\n| Component | Status | Maturity |\n|-----------|--------|----------|\n| Monorepo infrastructure | ✅ Complete | Production-ready |\n| Supabase schema + migrations | ✅ Complete | 6 migrations, well-structured |\n| Shared schemas (Zod) | ✅ Complete | Comprehensive type coverage |\n| API routes (Hono) | ✅ Complete | 10 route modules, OpenAPI docs |\n| Work Orders app | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD, template hierarchy, documents |\n| Material Admin app | ✅ Complete | Suppliers, products, taxonomy, JSON editor |\n| Site Companion app | ⚠️ Scaffold | Basic unit list, voice test page |\n| Web Dashboard | ⚠️ Legacy | Login + todos only |\n| Doc Parser | ✅ Complete | GPU pipeline, Supabase integration |\n| NocoDB integration | ✅ Complete | Connected to Supabase DB |\n| A2A Protocol | ✅ Complete | Agent card + JSON-RPC endpoint |\n| Template hierarchy (3-tier) | ✅ Complete | App/Company/Project scoping |\n| Document storage | ✅ Complete | Upload, extraction, linking |\n| Voice processing | ⚠️ Partial | Transcription works, structured extraction in progress |\n| Timesheet system | 📋 Designed | Schema designed, not yet implemented |\n| Auth / RLS | ⚠️ Partial | Supabase Auth configured, RLS policies need hardening |\n| Realtime subscriptions | ⚠️ Partial | Infrastructure ready, limited app integration |\n\n### 7.2 What's Designed but Not Yet Implemented\n\n**Timesheet System** (documented in `docs/timesheet/`):\n- `timesheets` table — daily time containers per user\n- `work_logs` table — links hours to specific BOM items (`site_item_id`)\n- Milestone tracking (e.g., \"Prime 30%\", \"Finish 100%\")\n- Earned value calculations\n- Voice-to-timesheet pipeline (speak → transcribe → extract hours + items → create work_log)\n\n**Materials/BOM System** (partially documented):\n- `site_items` table — exploded BOM from JSONB definitions\n- Product-to-scope matching via JSONB attributes\n- Supplier order management\n- Project supplies tracking\n\n---\n\n## 8. Data Flow Patterns\n\n### 8.1 Work Order Lifecycle\n\n```\n1. Admin creates Template (definition JSONB with sections/columns)\n         ↓\n2. Admin creates Work Order for Unit + Trade\n         ↓\n3. System \"snapshots\" template → creates WorkOrderTasks (one per room)\n         ↓\n4. Field worker opens Site Companion → sees room checklist\n         ↓\n5. Worker completes steps (task_data JSONB updated)\n         ↓\n6. Inspector reviews → signs off → work order marked complete\n```\n\n### 8.2 Voice Processing Pipeline\n\n```\n1. Field worker records voice memo in Site Companion\n         ↓\n2. Audio sent to API /voice endpoint\n         ↓\n3. Whisper transcribes audio → text\n         ↓\n4. Anthropic Claude extracts structured data:\n   - Hours worked\n   - Items completed\n   - Issues/notes\n         ↓\n5. Structured data creates/updates work_logs, work_order_tasks\n```\n\n### 8.3 Document Processing Pipeline\n\n```\n1. Admin uploads PDF (architectural drawings) via Work Orders app\n         ↓\n2. File stored in Supabase Storage, metadata in project_documents\n         ↓\n3. Doc Parser service called:\n   - Renders pages to images\n   - Detects layout (tables, figures, floor plans)\n   - Extracts text\n   - Uploads images to Supabase Storage\n         ↓\n4. Extracted images stored in document_images table\n         ↓\n5. Images linked to units/rooms via image_links table\n```\n\n### 8.4 NocoDB ↔ Custom App Integration\n\n```\n1. NocoDB reads relational data directly from PostgreSQL\n         ↓\n2. Complex JSONB data exposed via SQL views (view_project_bom)\n         ↓\n3. \"Edit in App\" deep links open Material Admin with URL params\n         ↓\n4. Material Admin's JsonTreeEditor modifies JSONB directly\n         ↓\n5. Changes visible in NocoDB immediately (same database)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Strategic Direction & Recommendations\n\n### 9.1 Immediate Next Steps\n\n1. **Implement Timesheet Tables**: The schema is designed (`docs/timesheet/schema_design.md`). Create the migration for `timesheets`, `work_logs`, and `site_items` tables.\n\n2. **Voice-to-Timesheet Pipeline**: Connect the existing voice transcription to the timesheet data model. This is the platform's killer feature — workers speak their hours and the system creates structured time entries.\n\n3. **Site Companion Buildout**: The PWA scaffold exists but needs the full work order execution UI (room checklists, step completion, photo capture).\n\n4. **RLS Policy Hardening**: Current policies are permissive for development. Production requires proper `organization_id` scoping and role-based access.\n\n### 9.2 Architecture Strengths\n\n- **Schema-first approach** prevents data model drift across 5+ applications\n- **JSONB hybrid** elegantly handles the tension between structured queries and deeply nested domain data\n- **Template hierarchy** is a sophisticated solution to the \"every project is slightly different\" problem\n- **Monorepo with Turborepo** enables rapid iteration without dependency hell\n- **A2A protocol** future-proofs for AI agent integration\n\n### 9.3 Architecture Risks\n\n- **JSONB complexity**: The flattened SQL views are read-only. Any write path for nested data requires the custom app, creating a dependency on the Material Admin for certain operations.\n- **NocoDB coupling**: ~90 NocoDB tables in the same database adds metadata overhead and potential migration conflicts.\n- **Config package hardcoding**: Supabase URLs and keys are hardcoded in `packages/config`. Should migrate to environment variables for deployment flexibility.\n- **Single API server**: All routes in one Hono instance. As the platform grows, consider splitting into domain-specific services.\n\n---\n\n## 10. -- Migration: Utility Functions
-- Description: Helper functions used by tables, triggers, and RLS
-- =============================================================


CREATE FUNCTION public.current_user_organization_id() RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- For now, return from auth.users metadata
    -- In production, this would query a user_organizations table
    RETURN (auth.jwt() -> 'user_metadata' ->> 'organization_id')::UUID;
END;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.get_default_library_for_type(p_project_type text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE
    AS $$
    SELECT id FROM template_libraries
    WHERE project_type = p_project_type
    AND scope = 'app'
    AND is_default = true
    AND is_active = true
    LIMIT 1;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.get_project_organization_id(p_project_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
    SELECT organization_id FROM wo_projects WHERE id = p_project_id;
$$;



CREATE FUNCTION public.search_agent_memories(query_embedding public.vector, match_threshold double precision DEFAULT 0.7, match_count integer DEFAULT 5, filter_area text DEFAULT NULL::text, filter_source text DEFAULT NULL::text, filter_metadata jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS TABLE(id uuid, content text, metadata jsonb, area text, source text, similarity double precision, created_at timestamp with time zone)
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    am.id,
    am.content,
    am.metadata,
    am.area,
    am.source,
    1 - (am.embedding <=> query_embedding) AS similarity,
    am.created_at
  FROM agent_memories am
  WHERE
    1 - (am.embedding <=> query_embedding) > match_threshold
    AND (filter_area IS NULL OR am.area = filter_area)
    AND (filter_source IS NULL OR am.source = filter_source)
    AND (filter_metadata IS NULL OR am.metadata @> filter_metadata)
  ORDER BY am.embedding <=> query_embedding
  LIMIT match_count;

-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.organizations (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    slug text NOT NULL,
    settings jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.organizations
    ADD CONSTRAINT organizations_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: metric_definitions
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.metric_definitions (
    metric_key text NOT NULL,
    metric_label text NOT NULL,
    category text NOT NULL,
    trade_key text,
    unit_of_measure text,
    default_formula text,
    description text,
    sort_order integer DEFAULT 0
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.metric_definitions
    ADD CONSTRAINT metric_definitions_pkey PRIMARY KEY (metric_key);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: rooms
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.rooms (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    display_order integer DEFAULT 0,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.rooms
    ADD CONSTRAINT rooms_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: template_libraries
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.template_libraries (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    project_type text NOT NULL,
    scope text DEFAULT 'app'::text NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid,
    icon text,
    color text,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT true NOT NULL,
    is_default boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT template_libraries_scope_check CHECK ((scope = ANY (ARRAY['app'::text, 'company'::text]))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_app_library CHECK ((((scope = 'app'::text) AND (organization_id IS NULL)) OR (scope <> 'app'::text))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_company_library CHECK ((((scope = 'company'::text) AND (organization_id IS NOT NULL)) OR (scope <> 'company'::text)))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.template_libraries
    ADD CONSTRAINT template_libraries_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

CREATE TABLE public.wo_projects (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,

-- Table: work_orders
-- -----------------------------------------------

    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,

    template_id uuid NOT NULL,

);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.work_orders
    ADD CONSTRAINT work_orders_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);

-- -----------------------------------------------
-- Table: agent_memories
-- -----------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE public.agent_memories (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    content text NOT NULL,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    area text DEFAULT 'main'::text NOT NULL,
    source text DEFAULT 'agent_zero'::text,
    source_memory_id text,
    embedding public.vector(384),
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT agent_memories_area_check CHECK ((area = ANY (ARRAY['main'::text, 'fragments'::text, 'solutions'::text])))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.agent_memories
    ADD CONSTRAINT agent_memories_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id);
