-- Migration: Views
-- Description: Reporting and cost analysis views
-- =============================================================

CREATE VIEW public.v_all_calculations AS
 SELECT u.unit_number,
    u.floor,
    r.name AS room_name,
    c.metric_key,
    c.metric_label,
    c.category,
    c.trade_key,
    c.rate,
    c.rate_unit,
    c.quantity,
    c.quantity_source,
    c.value_numeric,
    c.value_text,
    c.unit_of_measure,
    c.product_estimated,
    c.product_actual,
    c.price_estimated,
    c.price_actual,
    (c.price_actual - c.price_estimated) AS price_variance,
    c.hours_estimated,
    c.hours_actual,
    (c.hours_actual - c.hours_estimated) AS hours_variance,
    c.percent_complete,
    c.is_calculated,
    c.is_editable,
    c.source,
    c.formula
   FROM (((public.unit_room_calculations c
     JOIN public.unit_rooms ur ON ((ur.id = c.unit_room_id)))
     JOIN public.units u ON ((u.id = ur.unit_id)))
     JOIN public.rooms r ON ((r.id = ur.room_id)))
  ORDER BY u.floor, u.unit_number, r.name, c.category, c.metric_key;


CREATE VIEW public.v_budget_tracker AS
 WITH trade_budget AS (
         SELECT c.trade_key,
            u.unit_number,
            u.floor,
            r.name AS room_name,
            sum(COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)) FILTER (WHERE ((c.category = ANY (ARRAY['cost'::text, 'cost_actual'::text])) AND (c.unit_of_measure = '$'::text))) AS estimated,
            sum(c.price_actual) FILTER (WHERE ((c.category = ANY (ARRAY['cost'::text, 'cost_actual'::text])) AND (c.unit_of_measure = '$'::text))) AS actual,
            string_agg(DISTINCT c.product_estimated, ', '::text) FILTER (WHERE (c.product_estimated IS NOT NULL)) AS products_planned,
            string_agg(DISTINCT c.product_actual, ', '::text) FILTER (WHERE (c.product_actual IS NOT NULL)) AS products_installed,
            sum(c.hours_estimated) FILTER (WHERE (c.hours_estimated IS NOT NULL)) AS hours_planned,
            sum(c.hours_actual) FILTER (WHERE (c.hours_actual IS NOT NULL)) AS hours_worked,
            avg(c.percent_complete) FILTER (WHERE ((c.percent_complete IS NOT NULL) AND (c.percent_complete > (0)::numeric))) AS pct_complete
           FROM (((public.unit_room_calculations c
             JOIN public.unit_rooms ur ON ((ur.id = c.unit_room_id)))
             JOIN public.units u ON ((u.id = ur.unit_id)))
             JOIN public.rooms r ON ((r.id = ur.room_id)))
          GROUP BY c.trade_key, u.unit_number, u.floor, r.name
        )
 SELECT trade_key,
    unit_number,
    floor,
    room_name,
    estimated,
    actual,
    (actual - estimated) AS variance,
        CASE
            WHEN (estimated > (0)::numeric) THEN round((((actual - estimated) / estimated) * (100)::numeric), 1)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END AS variance_pct,
    products_planned,
    products_installed,
        CASE
            WHEN ((products_planned IS DISTINCT FROM products_installed) AND (products_installed IS NOT NULL)) THEN true
            ELSE false
        END AS product_changed,
    hours_planned,
    hours_worked,
    (hours_worked - hours_planned) AS hours_variance,
    pct_complete
   FROM trade_budget
  WHERE ((estimated IS NOT NULL) OR (actual IS NOT NULL))
  ORDER BY trade_key, floor, unit_number, room_name;


CREATE VIEW public.v_project_cost_summary AS
 WITH measurement_agg AS (
         SELECT ur.unit_id,
            count(DISTINCT ur.id) AS room_count,
            sum(m.area_sf) AS total_room_area_sf,
            sum(m.wall_area_sf) AS total_wall_area_sf,
            sum(m.ceiling_area_sf) AS total_ceiling_area_sf,
            sum(m.wall_length_total_lf) AS total_wall_lf
           FROM (public.unit_rooms ur
             LEFT JOIN public.unit_room_measurements m ON ((m.unit_room_id = ur.id)))
          GROUP BY ur.unit_id
        ), calculation_agg AS (
         SELECT ur.unit_id,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'material_cost_total'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_material_est,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_labor_est,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'total_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_cost_est,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'appliance_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_appliance_est,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'flooring_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_flooring_est,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = ANY (ARRAY['paint_wall_gal'::text, 'paint_ceiling_gal'::text])) THEN c.value_numeric
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_paint_gal,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'sub_invoice_allocated'::text) THEN c.value_numeric
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_invoice_allocated,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'material_cost_total'::text) THEN c.price_actual
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_material_actual,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_labor_actual,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'total_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_cost_actual,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'appliance_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_appliance_actual,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'flooring_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_flooring_actual,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.hours_estimated
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_hours_est,
            sum(
                CASE
                    WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.hours_actual
                    ELSE (0)::numeric
                END) AS total_hours_actual,
            avg(
                CASE
                    WHEN ((c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) AND (c.percent_complete IS NOT NULL)) THEN c.percent_complete
                    ELSE NULL::numeric
                END) AS avg_pct_complete
           FROM (public.unit_rooms ur
             LEFT JOIN public.unit_room_calculations c ON ((c.unit_room_id = ur.id)))
          GROUP BY ur.unit_id
        )
 SELECT u.unit_number,
    u.floor,
    u.unit_type,
    u.bedrooms,
    u.area_sf AS unit_area_sf,
    ma.room_count,
    ma.total_room_area_sf,
    ma.total_wall_area_sf,
    ma.total_ceiling_area_sf,
    ma.total_wall_lf,
    ca.total_material_est,
    ca.total_labor_est,
    ca.total_appliance_est,
    ca.total_flooring_est,
    ca.total_paint_gal,
    ca.total_invoice_allocated,
    ca.total_cost_est,
        CASE
            WHEN (u.area_sf > (0)::numeric) THEN round((ca.total_cost_est / u.area_sf), 2)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END AS cost_per_sf_est,
    ca.total_material_actual,
    ca.total_labor_actual,
    ca.total_appliance_actual,
    ca.total_flooring_actual,
    ca.total_cost_actual,
        CASE
            WHEN (u.area_sf > (0)::numeric) THEN round((ca.total_cost_actual / u.area_sf), 2)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END AS cost_per_sf_actual,
    (ca.total_cost_actual - ca.total_cost_est) AS cost_variance,
    ca.total_hours_est,
    ca.total_hours_actual,
    ca.avg_pct_complete
   FROM ((public.units u
     LEFT JOIN measurement_agg ma ON ((ma.unit_id = u.id)))
     LEFT JOIN calculation_agg ca ON ((ca.unit_id = u.id)))
  ORDER BY u.floor, u.unit_number;


CREATE VIEW public.v_trade_cost_comparison AS
 SELECT c.trade_key,
    u.unit_number,
    u.floor,
    r.name AS room_name,
    c.metric_key,
    c.metric_label,
    c.category,
    c.rate,
    c.rate_unit,
    c.quantity,
    COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric) AS price_estimated,
    c.price_actual,
    (c.price_actual - COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)) AS price_variance,
    c.product_estimated,
    c.product_actual,
    c.hours_estimated,
    c.hours_actual,
    c.percent_complete,
    c.value_numeric,
    c.unit_of_measure,
        CASE
            WHEN ((c.unit_of_measure = '$'::text) AND (m.area_sf > (0)::numeric)) THEN round((COALESCE(c.price_actual, c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric) / m.area_sf), 2)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END AS cost_per_sf
   FROM ((((public.unit_room_calculations c
     JOIN public.unit_rooms ur ON ((ur.id = c.unit_room_id)))
     JOIN public.units u ON ((u.id = ur.unit_id)))
     JOIN public.rooms r ON ((r.id = ur.room_id)))
     LEFT JOIN public.unit_room_measurements m ON ((m.unit_room_id = ur.id)))
  ORDER BY c.trade_key, u.floor, u.unit_number, r.name;


CREATE VIEW public.v_unit_cost_sheet AS
 SELECT u.unit_number,
    u.floor,
    u.unit_type,
    u.bedrooms,
    r.name AS room_name,
    ur.id AS unit_room_id,
    m.area_sf,
    m.area_m2,
    m.wall_length_total_lf,
    m.wall_length_cabinets_lf,
    m.wall_length_paintable_lf,
    m.baseboard_length_lf,
    m.ceiling_height_ft,
    m.wall_area_sf,

    m.window_count,
    m.door_count,
    m.width_ft,
    m.depth_ft,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'paint_wall_gal'::text) THEN c.value_numeric
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS paint_wall_gal,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'paint_ceiling_gal'::text) THEN c.value_numeric

    max(

            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'baseboard_lf'::text) THEN c.value_numeric
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS baseboard_lf,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'paint_wall_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS paint_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'flooring_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS flooring_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'baseboard_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS baseboard_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'appliance_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS appliance_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'door_cost_total'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS door_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS labor_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'total_cost'::text) THEN COALESCE(c.price_estimated, c.value_numeric)
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS total_cost_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'paint_wall_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS paint_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'flooring_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS flooring_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'baseboard_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS baseboard_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'appliance_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS appliance_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'door_cost_total'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS door_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS labor_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'total_cost'::text) THEN c.price_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS total_cost_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'appliance_cost'::text) THEN c.product_estimated

        END) AS appliance_spec_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'appliance_cost'::text) THEN c.product_actual
            ELSE NULL::text
        END) AS appliance_spec_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'door_cost_total'::text) THEN c.product_estimated

        END) AS door_spec_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'door_cost_total'::text) THEN c.product_actual

        END) AS door_spec_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.hours_estimated
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS labor_hours_est,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.hours_actual
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS labor_hours_actual,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'labor_cost'::text) THEN c.percent_complete
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS labor_pct_complete,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'flooring_cost'::text) THEN c.rate
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS flooring_rate,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'paint_wall_cost'::text) THEN c.rate
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS paint_rate,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'baseboard_cost'::text) THEN c.rate
            ELSE NULL::numeric

    max(

        END) AS labor_rate,
    max(
        CASE
            WHEN (c.metric_key = 'sub_invoice_allocated'::text) THEN c.value_numeric
            ELSE NULL::numeric
        END) AS sub_invoice_allocated
   FROM ((((public.unit_rooms ur
     JOIN public.units u ON ((u.id = ur.unit_id)))
     JOIN public.rooms r ON ((r.id = ur.room_id)))
     LEFT JOIN public.unit_room_measurements m ON ((m.unit_room_id = ur.id)))
     LEFT JOIN public.unit_room_calculations c ON ((c.unit_room_id = ur.id)))
  GROUP BY u.unit_number, u.floor, u.unit_type, u.bedrooms, r.name, ur.id, m.area_sf, m.area_m2, m.wall_length_total_lf, m.wall_length_cabinets_lf, m.wall_length_paintable_lf, m.baseboard_length_lf, m.ceiling_height_ft, m.wall_area_sf, m.ceiling_area_sf, m.window_count, m.door_count, m.width_ft, m.depth_ft
  ORDER BY u.floor, u.unit_number, r.name;

This guide details how to connect NocoDB to your local Supabase instance to enable the **Hybrid Architecture**: managing relational data in NocoDB while editing complex JSON trees in the Custom App.

## 1. Prerequisites

*   **Supabase/Postgres** running locally (Port `54322`).
*   **Docker** installed and running.
*   **Materials App** running on `http://localhost:3002`.

## 2. Launch NocoDB

Run this command to start NocoDB in a simplified mode (using SQLite for its own metadata, but connecting to Postgres for data):

```bash
docker run -d --name nocodb -p 8080:8080 nocodb/nocodb:latest
```

*   **Access**: Open [http://localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080) in your browser.
*   **First Run**: Create a local email/password (this is just for NocoDB login, not your database).

## 3. Connect to Database

1.  Click **"New Project"** > **"Create by connecting to an external database"**.
2.  **Connection Details**:
    *   **Display Name**: `Voicelog Local`
    *   **Database Type**: `PostgreSQL`
    *   **Host Address**: `host.docker.internal` (Crucial for Docker to see your host machine)
    *   **Port**: `54322`
    *   **Username**: `postgres`
    *   **Password**: `postgres`
    *   **Database Name**: `postgres`
3.  Click **"Test Database Connection"**. If green, click **"Submit"**.

## 4. Setup Content Views

### A. The Flattened BOM (Read-Only)
1.  In the left sidebar, click `Table`.
2.  Find `view_project_bom` (This is the SQL view we created).
3.  **Verify**: You should see your project scopes flattened into rows (`Plan AStudio`, `Kitchenette`, `Cabinets`, `qty: 12`).
4.  **Edit**: You CAN now edit `quantity` and `action` cells directly.
5.  **Test Update**: Change a quantity from `12` to `99`. Refresh the table. The change persists.
6.  **Verify JSON**: Check the `project_definitions` table or your App to see the JSONB has been updated.

### B. The Taxonomy (Editable)
1.  Find the `taxonomies` table (or whatever we named the master list).
2.  **Test**: Add a new row (e.g., `Category: Room`, `Value: Wine_Cellar`).
3.  **Verify**: Go to your App -> Project Definitions. Open a "Room" dropdown. "Wine_Cellar" should appear instantly.

## 5. Setup "Deep Link" Edit Button

To edit the complex trees in `view_project_bom`, we link back to the App.

1.  Open the `view_project_bom` table in NocoDB.
2.  Click **"+"** to add a new column.
3.  **Column Type**: `Formula`.
4.  **Column Name**: `Open in App`.
5.  **Formula**:
    ```sql
    CONCAT('http://localhost:3002/definitions?project_id=', {project_id})
    ```
6.  **Set Primary Key (CRITICAL)**:
    *   By default, NocoDB sees Views as "Read Only" because they lack a Primary Key.
    *   Locate the **`id`** column (it should be the first one).
    *   **Right-click** the column header -> Select **Set as Primary Key**.
    *   *If you don't see this option:* Go to **Project Settings** (gear icon) -> **Data** -> Select `view_project_bom` -> Click **Set PK** next to the `id` column.
    *   Once set, a "key" icon will appear, and the grid will become editable.

7.  **Verify**: Click the link in any row. It should open your App and auto-load that project.

## 6. Configure "Select" Options

To match the App's validation, configure these columns as **SingleSelect** in NocoDB.

### A. Action (Column: `action`)
*   `replace`
*   `keep`
*   `install_new`
*   `repair`
*   `complete`

### B. Measure (Column: `measure`)
*   `length_ft`
*   `area_sqft`
*   `count`
*   `volume_cuft`

### C. Floorplan Base Type (Column: `base_type`)
*   `Studio`
*   `One_Bedroom`
*   `Two_Bedroom`

### D. Room Name (Column: `room_name`)
*   `Living_Room`, `Kitchen`, `Bedroom`, `Bathroom`, `Hallway`
*   `Main_Living_Sleeping`, `Kitchenette`, `Master_Bedroom`
*   `Master_Bath`, `Guest_Bedroom`, `Guest_Bath`, `Closet`
*   `Exterior_Envelope`, `Exterior_Grounds`

## Troubleshooting

*   **Connection Refused**: Ensure your local Postgres is actually on port `54322`. If using default Postgres, try port `5432`.
*   **Link doesn't load project**: Ensure your App is running on port `3002` and that you updated `ProjectDefinitionsView.tsx` with the `onMount` logic.
*   **No Data in View**: If NocoDB shows headers but no data for a SQL View, it likely needs a Primary Key. We added a generated `id` column to `view_project_bom` to fix this. Ensure you **Sync Metadata** in NocoDB.

(venv) root@2c00128c0233:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/supabase/migrations# import { createSignal, Component } from 'solid-js';
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
import { SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY } from '@repo/config';
import { logger } from '@repo/logger';

const supabase = createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY);

export const Login: Component<{ onLogin: (token: string) => void }> = (props) => {
    const [email, setEmail] = createSignal('');
    const [password, setPassword] = createSignal('');
    const [loading, setLoading] = createSignal(false);
    const [error, setError] = createSignal('');

    const handleLogin = async (e: Event) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        setLoading(true);
        setError('');

        try {
            logger.info('Attempting login...');
            const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({
                email: email(),
                password: password(),
            });

            if (error) throw error;

            if (data.session) {
                logger.info('Login successful');
                props.onLogin(data.session.access_token);
            }
        } catch (err: any) {
            logger.error({ err }, 'Login failed');
            setError(err.message);
        } finally {
            setLoading(false);
        }
    };

    return (
        <div style={{ "max-width": "400px", margin: "50px auto", padding: "20px", border: "1px solid #ccc", "border-radius": "8px" }}>
            <h2>Login</h2>
            <form onSubmit={handleLogin}>
                <div style={{ "margin-bottom": "10px" }}>
                    <label style={{ display: "block", "margin-bottom": "5px" }}>Email:</label>
                    <input
                        type="email"
                        value={email()}
                        onInput={(e) => setEmail(e.currentTarget.value)}
                        required
                        style={{ width: "100%", padding: "8px" }}
                    />
                </div>
                <div style={{ "margin-bottom": "20px" }}>
                    <label style={{ display: "block", "margin-bottom": "5px" }}>Password:</label>
                    <input
                        type="password"
                        value={password()}
                        onInput={(e) => setPassword(e.currentTarget.value)}
                        required
                        style={{ width: "100%", padding: "8px" }}
                    />
                </div>
                {error() && <p style={{ color: "red", "margin-bottom": "10px" }}>{error()}</p>}
                <button type="submit" disabled={loading()} style={{ width: "100%", padding: "10px", "background-color": "#007bff", color: "white", border: "none", "border-radius": "4px", cursor: "pointer" }}>
                    {loading() ? 'Logging in...' : 'Login'}
                </button>
            </form>
        </div>
    );
};

createSignal, onCleanup } from 'solid-js';\n\nexport interface Route {\n  path: string;\n  params: Record<string, string>;\n}\n\nfunction parseHash(): Route {\n  const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) || '/dashboard';\n  const parts = hash.split('/');\n\n  // Match /table/:tableName\n  if (parts[1] === 'table' && parts[2]) {\n    return { path: '/table/:tableName', params: { tableName: decodeURIComponent(parts[2]) } };\n  }\n\n  return { path: hash, params: {} };\n}\n\nexport function createRouter() {\n  const [route, setRoute] = 'cursor-pointer hover:bg-primary-50 group relative'\n            : ''\n        }`}\n        onClick={startEdit}\n      >\n        <span class=\"break-words\">{displayValue() || <span class=\"text-gray-300\">\\u2014</span>}</span>\n        <Show when={props.editable}>\n          <span class=\"absolute right-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 text-gray-400 text-xs\">\n            \\u270E\n          </span>\n        </Show>\n      </div>\n    }>\n      <div class=\"flex items-center\">\n        <input\n          class=\"input text-sm py-1\"\n          value={editValue()}\n          onInput={(e) => setEditValue(e.currentTarget.value)}\n          onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n          autofocus\n          onBlur={cancelEdit}\n        />\n      </div>\n    </Show>\n  );\n};\n''')\n\n# src/components/DataGrid.tsx\nwith open(f'{PORTAL}/src/components/DataGrid.tsx', 'w') as f:\n    f.write('''import { Component, For, Show, createSignal } from 'solid-js';\nimport { EditableCell } from './EditableCell';\nimport { Pagination } from './Pagination';\nimport { supabase } from '../lib/supabase';\nimport type { SortDirection } from '../lib/types';\n\ninterface DataGridProps {\n  tableName: string;\n  data: Record<string, unknown>[];\n  columns: string[];\n  editableColumns: string[];\n  isEditable: boolean;\n  primaryKey: string;\n  totalCount: number;\n  page: number;\n  pageSize: number;\n  sortColumn: string | null;\n  sortDirection: SortDirection;\n  onSort: (column: string) => void;\n  onPageChange: (page: number) => void;\n  onDataChange: () => void;\n}\n\nexport const DataGrid: Component<DataGridProps> = (props) => {\n  const [saving, setSaving] = createSignal<string | null>(null);\n  const [saveError, setSaveError] = createSignal<string | null>(null);\n\n  const isColumnEditable = (col: string) => {\n    if (!props.isEditable) return false;\n    if (col === props.primaryKey) return false;\n    if (props.editableColumns.length === 0) return true; // all editable if none specified\n    return props.editableColumns.includes(col);\n  };\n\n  const handleCellSave = async (rowId: string, column: string, newValue: unknown) => {\n    setSaving(rowId + column);\n    setSaveError(null);\n\n    const { error } = await supabase\n      .from(props.tableName)\n      .update({ [column]: newValue })\n      .eq(props.primaryKey, rowId);\n\n    if (error) {\n      setSaveError(`Failed to save: ${error.message}`);\n    } else {\n      props.onDataChange();\n    }\n    setSaving(null);\n  };\n\n  const sortIcon = (col: string) => {\n    if (props.sortColumn !== col) return ' \\u2195';\n    return props.sortDirection === 'asc' ? ' \\u2191' : ' \\u2193';\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <div class=\"bg-white rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 overflow-hidden\">\n      <Show when={saveError()}>\n        <div class=\"bg-red-50 border-b border-red-200 px-4 py-2 text-sm text-red-700\">\n          {saveError()}\n          <button class=\"ml-2 underline\" onClick={() => setSaveError(null)}>dismiss</button>\n        </div>\n      </Show>\n\n      <div class=\"overflow-x-auto\">\n        <table class=\"min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200\">\n          <thead class=\"bg-gray-50\">\n            <tr>\n              <For each={props.columns}>\n                {(col) => (\n                  <th\n                    class=\"px-3 py-3 text-left text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider cursor-pointer hover:bg-gray-100 select-none whitespace-nowrap\"\n                    onClick={() => props.onSort(col)}\n                  >\n                    {col}\n                    <span class=\"text-gray-400\">{sortIcon(col)}</span>\n                  </th>\n                )}\n              </For>\n            </tr>\n          </thead>\n          <tbody class=\"bg-white divide-y divide-gray-200\">\n            <Show when={props.data.length > 0} fallback={\n              <tr>\n                <td colspan={props.columns.length} class=\"px-4 py-8 text-center text-gray-400\">\n                  No data found\n                </td>\n              </tr>\n            }>\n              <For each={props.data}>\n                {(row) => {\n                  const rowId = String(row[props.primaryKey] ?? '');\n                  return (\n                    <tr class=\"hover:bg-gray-50\">\n                      <For each={props.columns}>\n                        {(col) => (\n                          <td class=\"text-sm text-gray-900 max-w-xs\">\n                            <EditableCell\n                              value={row[col]}\n                              editable={isColumnEditable(col)}\n                              onSave={(v) => handleCellSave(rowId, col, v)}\n                            />\n                          </td>\n                        )}\n                      </For>\n                    </tr>\n                  );\n                }}\n              </For>\n            </Show>\n          </tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      <Pagination\n        page={props.page}\n        pageSize={props.pageSize}\n        totalCount={props.totalCount}\n        onPageChange={props.onPageChange}\n      />\n    </div>\n  );\n};\n''')\n\nprint('DataGrid and EditableCell created')\n"
    }
}

> **Generated**: 2026-02-17 | **Author**: Agent Zero (Developer)
> **Scope**: Full monorepo architecture, data model, services, current state, and strategic direction

---

## 1. What You're Building

A **construction renovation management platform** — a suite of interconnected applications that digitize the entire lifecycle of multi-unit renovation projects, from initial scope definition through work execution, inspection, and closeout.

The platform solves a specific industry pain point: construction teams juggle spreadsheets, paper checklists, voice memos, and disconnected tools. This system unifies everything into a single data model where:

- **Office staff** define project scopes, manage templates, upload architectural drawings, and track progress from dashboards
- **Field workers** execute work orders room-by-room using mobile-optimized checklists, log time via voice, and capture site conditions
- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol
- **NocoDB** provides a spreadsheet-like interface for bulk data management and ad-hoc queries without custom UI development

The platform is **multi-tenant** (organizations), **multi-project** (wo_projects), and **multi-trade** (painting, plumbing, carpentry, etc.).

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

### 2.1 Philosophy

Three core principles drive every technical decision:

1. **Schema-First**: Zod schemas in `packages/schemas` are the single source of truth. If it's not in a schema, it doesn't exist. This guarantees type safety from database to UI.
2. **Monorepo with Shared DNA**: Multiple apps share the same data model, validation logic, and configuration. A change to a schema propagates instantly to all consumers.
3. **Hybrid Relational + JSONB**: Relational columns for indexing, foreign keys, and permissions. JSONB columns for deeply nested hierarchical data (template definitions, scope trees, overrides).

### 2.2 Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm | Fast parallel builds, workspace linking, dependency deduplication |
| **Frontend** | SolidJS + Vite | True reactivity via signals (no virtual DOM), sub-second HMR |
| **Backend** | Hono (OpenAPIHono) | Lightweight, web-standard, runs on Node/Edge/Deno. Built-in OpenAPI generation |
| **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) | Managed Postgres with Auth, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Row-Level Security |
| **Validation** | Zod | Runtime validation + static TypeScript inference from a single definition |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first, consistent design system across apps |
| **Voice/AI** | Anthropic Claude + OpenAI Whisper | Voice transcription → LLM structured extraction |
| **Document Parsing** | FastAPI + Detectron2 + LayoutParser | GPU-accelerated PDF parsing, floor plan detection, image extraction |
| **Spreadsheet UI** | NocoDB | No-code database interface for bulk editing relational data |
| **Agent Protocol** | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) | JSON-RPC interoperability between AI agents |

### 2.3 Service Topology

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        USER DEVICES                             │
├──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────────────────┤

│ Orders   │ Admin    │ Companion│ Dashboard│ Spreadsheet         │

│ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ Docker              │
│ Tailwind │ Tailwind │ PWA      │ Legacy   │                     │
└────┬──��──┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴──────────┬──────────┘

     └──────────┴──────────┴──────────┘                │
                      │                                │

              │   Hono API     │                       │

## 3. Data Model

### 3.1 Entity Relationship Summary

```
Organization (tenant)
  ├── Units (physical spaces: apartments, condos)
  │     ├── Unit Rooms (rooms within a unit + measurements)
  │     ├── Unit Documents (linked architectural drawings)
  │     └── Work Orders (one per trade per unit)
  │           ├── Work Order Tasks (room × template checklist)
  │           └── Work Order Notes (comments, voice logs)
  │
  ├── Rooms (master list: Kitchen, Bathroom, Bedroom...)
  │
  ├── Work Order Templates (trade checklists)
  │     └── Template Definition (JSONB: sections → columns → steps)
  │
  ├── WO Projects (properties/job sites)
  │     ├── Project Documents (uploaded PDFs, drawings)
  │     │     └── Document Images (extracted pages/floor plans)
  │     └── Project-scoped Template overrides
  │
  └── Template Libraries (importable template collections)
```

### 3.2 The Template Hierarchy (Three-Tier)

This is the most architecturally significant design decision in the system:

| Tier | Scope | Purpose | Example |
|------|-------|---------|--------|
| **App** | System-wide | Factory defaults, read-only | "Standard Painting Checklist v1" |
| **Company** | Per organization | Tenant customizations | "Acme Painting Checklist" (adds extra steps) |
| **Project** | Per wo_project | Job-specific overrides | "123 Main St Painting" (removes N/A rooms) |

Templates cascade: a project template inherits from its company parent, which inherits from the app default. The `parent_template_id` and `copied_from_template_id` columns track lineage.

### 3.3 The JSONB Strategy

Relational tables handle entities that need indexing, joins, and permissions. JSONB handles deeply nested hierarchical data:

| Table | JSONB Column | Contains |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| `work_order_templates` | `definition` | `{ sections: { "Prep": { columns: [...] }, "Finish": { columns: [...] } }, logic: { rules: [...] } }` |
| `work_order_tasks` | `task_data` | `{ steps: [{ key, label, status, completed_by, completed_at, not_applicable }] }` |
| `units` | `metadata` | Flexible unit-specific notes, special instructions |
| `project_definitions` | `floorplan_definitions` | Nested room/item trees per floor plan type |
| `products` | `attributes` | Spec matching data (dimensions, colors, materials) |

A SQL view (`view_project_bom`) flattens the JSONB trees into rows for NocoDB consumption.

### 3.4 Key Database Tables (Public Schema)

**Core Business:**
- `organizations` — Multi-tenant root
- `units` — Physical spaces (apartments)
- `rooms` — Room type catalog
- `wo_projects` — Job sites/properties
- `work_order_templates` — Trade checklist definitions
- `work_orders` — Template instances per unit
- `work_order_tasks` — Room-level checklist execution
- `work_order_notes` — Comments and voice log entries
- `template_libraries` — Importable template collections

**Documents & Media:**
- `project_documents` — Uploaded PDFs, drawings
- `document_images` — Extracted page images
- `unit_documents` — Links documents to units
- `image_links` — Links images to any entity
- `unit_rooms` — Room instances with measurements
- `unit_room_measurements` — Detailed dimension data
- `unit_room_calculations` — Computed areas/volumes

**Materials (Planned/Partial):**
- `metric_definitions` — Measurement metrics

**NocoDB (~90 tables):**
- `nc_*` — NocoDB's internal metadata for its spreadsheet interface

## 4. Shared Packages

### 4.1 `packages/schemas` — The Contract

The most critical package. Every data shape is defined here as Zod schemas:

| File | Exports | Used By |
|------|---------|--------|
| `work-orders.ts` | Organization, Unit, Room, Template, WorkOrder, Task, Note schemas + API request/response types | API routes, Work Orders app |
| `materials.ts` | Supplier, Product, ProjectSupply schemas | Material Admin, API |
| `renovation.ts` | Unit, UnitScopeItem schemas (legacy?) | Site Companion |
| `taxonomy.ts` | Taxonomy categories (actions, room types) | Material Admin, API |
| `delivery.ts` | Delivery tracking schemas | API |
| `todo.ts` | Simple todo schemas | Web Dashboard |
| `zod.ts` | Shared Zod utilities | All |

### 4.2 `packages/config` — Centralized Configuration

Hardcoded Supabase URLs, API keys, port assignments, and CORS origins. All apps import from here for consistency.

### 4.3 `packages/db` — Database Types

Auto-generated TypeScript types from Supabase schema via `pnpm db:types`. Provides compile-time safety for direct database queries.

### 4.4 `packages/ui` — Shared Components

Reusable SolidJS components (buttons, layouts) for consistent design across apps.

### 4.5 `packages/logger` — Structured Logging

Shared logging utility for consistent output formatting.

### 4.6 `packages/tsconfig` — TypeScript Configuration

Base TypeScript configuration inherited by all packages and apps via `workspace:*` symlinks.

---

## 5. Applications

### 5.1 API (`apps/api`) — The Brain

**Framework**: Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js
**Port**: 3000
**Key Dependencies**: `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `@supabase/supabase-js`, `pdfjs-dist`, `@napi-rs/canvas`

**Routes:**

| Route | Purpose | Key Operations |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| `/work-orders` | Work order CRUD | Create from template, update task steps, manage lifecycle |
| `/work-orders` (documents) | Document management | Upload PDFs, link to projects/units |
| `/materials` | Supplier/product catalog | CRUD for suppliers, products, project supplies |
| `/taxonomy` | Category management | Actions, room types, item categories |
| `/projects` | Project definitions | JSONB scope trees, floorplan definitions |
| `/units` | Unit management | CRUD with metadata, scope items |
| `/voice` | Voice processing | Audio → Whisper transcription → LLM structured extraction |
| `/delivery` | Delivery tracking | Delivery scheduling and status |
| `/todos` | Simple todos | Basic CRUD (reference/demo) |
| `/a2a` | Agent-to-Agent protocol | JSON-RPC endpoint for AI agent interop |
| `/ui` | Swagger UI | Interactive API documentation |
| `/doc` | OpenAPI JSON | Machine-readable API spec |

**Services Layer** (`src/services/`): Business logic separated from route handlers.
**Middleware** (`src/middleware/`): Auth, validation, error handling.
**Scripts** (`scripts/`): 50+ utility scripts for seeding, migration, testing, and verification.

### 5.2 Work Orders App (`apps/work-orders`)

**Port**: 3004

- `NewProject` — Project creation wizard

### 5.3 Site Companion (`apps/site-companion`)

**Framework**: SolidJS PWA
**Port**: 3003
**Purpose**: Mobile-optimized field worker interface

**Pages:**
- `UnitList` — Browse assigned units
- `UnitDetail` — View unit scope, execute work items
- `VoiceTest` — Voice recording → transcription → structured data extraction

**Key Feature**: Designed as a Progressive Web App for offline-capable field use. Workers can record voice memos that get transcribed and parsed into structured work log entries.

### 5.4 Material Admin (`apps/material-admin`)

**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS
**Port**: 3002
**Purpose**: Supplier catalog, product management, taxonomy editing

**Features:**

- Product catalog with JSONB attributes for spec matching

- JSON tree editor for complex nested definitions

### 5.5 Web Dashboard (`apps/web-dashboard`)

**Framework**: SolidJS (minimal)
**Port**: 3001

## 6. Supporting Services

### 6.1 Doc Parser (`doc-parser/`)

**Framework**: FastAPI (Python)
**Port**: 5000
**Infrastructure**: Docker with GPU support (Detectron2, LayoutParser)

**Capabilities:**
- PDF upload and page rendering
- Layout analysis (text blocks, tables, figures)
- Floor plan detection and extraction
- Image upload to Supabase Storage
- Text extraction via PyMuPDF

**Integration**: Called via HTTP from the Hono API or directly from Agent Zero. Uploads extracted images to Supabase Storage and returns structured metadata.

### 6.2 NocoDB (`nocodb/`)

**Port**: 8082
**Purpose**: Spreadsheet interface directly connected to the Supabase PostgreSQL database

**Use Cases:**
- Bulk editing relational data (units, rooms, taxonomy)
- Viewing flattened SQL views of JSONB data (`view_project_bom`)
- Ad-hoc queries without building custom UI
- Deep linking to the Material Admin app for complex JSONB editing

### 6.3 A2A Protocol

The API exposes an Agent-to-Agent JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a` with an agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`. This enables:
- External AI agents (like Agent Zero) to discover and interact with the platform
- Structured task delegation (e.g., "create a work order for unit 101")
- Multi-agent workflows across different AI systems

## 8. Data Flow Patterns

### 8.1 Work Order Lifecycle

```
1. Admin creates Template (definition JSONB with sections/columns)
         ↓
2. Admin creates Work Order for Unit + Trade
         ↓
3. System "snapshots" template → creates WorkOrderTasks (one per room)
         ↓
4. Field worker opens Site Companion → sees room checklist
         ↓
5. Worker completes steps (task_data JSONB updated)
         ↓
6. Inspector reviews → signs off → work order marked complete
```

### 8.2 Voice Processing Pipeline

```
1. Field worker records voice memo in Site Companion
         ↓
2. Audio sent to API /voice endpoint
         ↓
3. Whisper transcribes audio → text
         ↓
4. Anthropic Claude extracts structured data:
   - Hours worked
   - Items completed
   - Issues/notes
         ↓
5. Structured data creates/updates work_logs, work_order_tasks
```

### 8.3 Document Processing Pipeline

```
1. Admin uploads PDF (architectural drawings) via Work Orders app
         ↓
2. File stored in Supabase Storage, metadata in project_documents
         ↓
3. Doc Parser service called:
   - Renders pages to images
   - Detects layout (tables, figures, floor plans)
   - Extracts text
   - Uploads images to Supabase Storage
         ↓
4. Extracted images stored in document_images table
         ↓
5. Images linked to units/rooms via image_links table
```

### 8.4 NocoDB ↔ Custom App Integration

```
1. NocoDB reads relational data directly from PostgreSQL
         ↓
2. Complex JSONB data exposed via SQL views (view_project_bom)
         ↓
3. "Edit in App" deep links open Material Admin with URL params
         ↓
4. Material Admin's JsonTreeEditor modifies JSONB directly
         ↓
5. Changes visible in NocoDB immediately (same database)
```

---

## 9. Strategic Direction & Recommendations

### 9.1 Immediate Next Steps

1. **Implement Timesheet Tables**: The schema is designed (`docs/timesheet/schema_design.md`). Create the migration for `timesheets`, `work_logs`, and `site_items` tables.

2. **Voice-to-Timesheet Pipeline**: Connect the existing voice transcription to the timesheet data model. This is the platform's killer feature — workers speak their hours and the system creates structured time entries.

3. **Site Companion Buildout**: The PWA scaffold exists but needs the full work order execution UI (room checklists, step completion, photo capture).

4. **RLS Policy Hardening**: Current policies are permissive for development. Production requires proper `organization_id` scoping and role-based access.

### 9.2 Architecture Strengths

- **Schema-first approach** prevents data model drift across 5+ applications
- **JSONB hybrid** elegantly handles the tension between structured queries and deeply nested domain data
- **Template hierarchy** is a sophisticated solution to the "every project is slightly different" problem
- **Monorepo with Turborepo** enables rapid iteration without dependency hell
- **A2A protocol** future-proofs for AI agent integration

### 9.3 Architecture Risks

- **JSONB complexity**: The flattened SQL views are read-only. Any write path for nested data requires the custom app, creating a dependency on the Material Admin for certain operations.
- **NocoDB coupling**: ~90 NocoDB tables in the same database adds metadata overhead and potential migration conflicts.
- **Config package hardcoding**: Supabase URLs and keys are hardcoded in `packages/config`. Should migrate to environment variables for deployment flexibility.
- **Single API server**: All routes in one Hono instance. As the platform grows, consider splitting into domain-specific services.

-- ========================================
-- PROJECTS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE projects (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  address TEXT,
  client_name TEXT,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK (status IN ('draft', 'active', 'completed', 'archived')),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- PROJECT DOCUMENTS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE project_documents (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  file_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_url TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_size INTEGER,
  uploaded_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  uploaded_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE parsed_document_data (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES project_documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  parsed_content JSONB NOT NULL,
  confidence_score DECIMAL(3,2),
  requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  reviewed_by UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  reviewed_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- UNITS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE units (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  unit_number TEXT NOT NULL,
  type TEXT, -- A, B, C, D, E, F
  bedrooms INTEGER,
  area_sf INTEGER,
  floor INTEGER,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_started' CHECK (status IN ('not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed')),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(project_id, unit_number)
);

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDERS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  unit_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES units(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  trade TEXT NOT NULL,
  contractor_name TEXT,
  contractor_id UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  start_date DATE,
  completion_date DATE,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_started' CHECK (status IN ('not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed')),
  inspector_signoff_by UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  inspector_signoff_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDER TASKS (Checkboxes)
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_tasks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  room TEXT NOT NULL,
  task_column TEXT NOT NULL,
  task_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  checked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  not_applicable BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  checked_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  checked_by UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDER TASK HISTORY (For analytics)
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_task_history (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_order_tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  checked BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  changed_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  changed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Trigger to auto-populate history
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION log_task_change()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  IF (TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.checked != NEW.checked) THEN
    INSERT INTO work_order_task_history (task_id, checked, changed_by, changed_at)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.checked, NEW.checked_by, NEW.checked_at);
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER task_change_logger
  AFTER UPDATE ON work_order_tasks
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION log_task_change();

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDER NOTES
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_notes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  note_text TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- TIME ESTIMATES
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_time_estimates (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  estimated_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
  estimated_start_date DATE,
  estimated_completion_date DATE,
  notes TEXT,
  created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- TIME ENTRIES (Future timesheet integration)
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_time_entries (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  task_id UUID NULL REFERENCES work_order_tasks(id),
  hours DECIMAL(5,2) NOT NULL,
  entry_date DATE NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- PROGRESS SNAPSHOTS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_progress_snapshots (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  snapshot_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  total_tasks INTEGER NOT NULL,
  completed_tasks INTEGER NOT NULL,
  completion_percentage DECIMAL(5,2) NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Function to take snapshot
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION take_progress_snapshot(wo_id UUID)
RETURNS void AS $$
DECLARE
  total INTEGER;
  completed INTEGER;
  percentage DECIMAL(5,2);
BEGIN
  SELECT
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT not_applicable),
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE checked AND NOT not_applicable)
  INTO total, completed
  FROM work_order_tasks
  WHERE work_order_id = wo_id;
  
  percentage := CASE WHEN total > 0 THEN (completed::DECIMAL / total * 100) ELSE 0 END;
  
  INSERT INTO work_order_progress_snapshots
    (work_order_id, total_tasks, completed_tasks, completion_percentage)
  VALUES
    (wo_id, total, completed, percentage);
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- ========================================
-- INDEXES
-- ========================================
CREATE INDEX idx_projects_org ON projects(organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_units_project ON units(project_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_units_org ON units(organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_units_project_org ON units(project_id, organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_unit ON work_orders(unit_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_org ON work_orders(organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_orders_unit_trade ON work_orders(unit_id, trade);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_work_order ON work_order_tasks(work_order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_org ON work_order_tasks(organization_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_history_task ON work_order_task_history(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_notes_work_order ON work_order_notes(work_order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_time_estimates_wo ON work_order_time_estimates(work_order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_time_entries_wo ON work_order_time_entries(work_order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_time_entries_user ON work_order_time_entries(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_progress_snapshots_wo ON work_order_progress_snapshots(work_order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_documents_project ON project_documents(project_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_parsed_data_document ON parsed_document_data(document_id);

-- ========================================
-- VIEWS (Helper views for common queries)
-- ========================================
CREATE VIEW work_order_progress AS
SELECT
  wo.id AS work_order_id,
  wo.unit_id,
  wo.trade,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT t.not_applicable) AS total_tasks,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE t.checked AND NOT t.not_applicable) AS completed_tasks,
  ROUND(
    CASE
      WHEN COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT t.not_applicable) > 0
      THEN (COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE t.checked AND NOT t.not_applicable)::DECIMAL /
            COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT t.not_applicable) * 100)
      ELSE 0
    END,
    2
  ) AS completion_percentage
FROM work_orders wo
LEFT JOIN work_order_tasks t ON t.work_order_id = wo.id
GROUP BY wo.id, wo.unit_id, wo.trade;

CREATE VIEW unit_progress AS
SELECT
  u.id AS unit_id,
  u.unit_number,
  u.project_id,
  COUNT(DISTINCT wo.id) AS total_work_orders,
  COUNT(DISTINCT wo.id) FILTER (WHERE wo.status = 'completed') AS completed_work_orders,
  ROUND(AVG(wop.completion_percentage), 2) AS avg_completion_percentage
FROM units u
LEFT JOIN work_orders wo ON wo.unit_id = u.id
LEFT JOIN work_order_progress wop ON wop.work_order_id = wo.id
GROUP BY u.id, u.unit_number, u.project_id;

# 📖 Measurements & Calculations System — Reference Guide

---

## Understanding Cost Types

You're right — not everything relates to square footage. The system handles **three distinct cost models**:

| Cost Model | Measured By | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **Area-based** | $/SF or $/m² | Paint, flooring, drywall, ceiling tiles |
| **Linear-based** | $/LF | Baseboards, crown molding, caulking, countertop edges |
| **Per-item (count)** | $/each | Toilets, sinks, faucets, appliances, doors, light fixtures, outlets |

The `cost_per_sf` column in the views only applies to area-based costs. For a toilet at $350, that number stands alone — dividing it by room area would be meaningless.

---

## 🗄️ Table 1: `unit_room_measurements`

**What it is:** The physical reality of each room — dimensions you'd get from a tape measure or floor plan.

**One row per room per unit** (1:1 with `unit_rooms`).

| Column | Meaning | Example | How It's Used |
|---|---|---|---|
| `unit_room_id` | Links to which unit + room | Kitchen in Unit 201 | Joins everything together |
| `area_sf` | Floor area in square feet | 49.5 | Calculate flooring material needed, cost per SF comparisons |
| `area_m2` | Same area in metric | 4.60 | For metric-system suppliers or international reporting |
| `wall_length_total_lf` | Total wall perimeter | 28.2 LF | Starting point for baseboard, chair rail, wainscoting estimates |
| `wall_length_cabinets_lf` | Wall hidden by cabinets | 8.5 LF | Subtracted from paintable wall — you don't paint behind cabinets |
| `wall_length_paintable_lf` | Exposed wall length | 19.7 LF | Multiply by ceiling height = paintable wall area |
| `baseboard_length_lf` | Baseboard run | 19.7 LF | Order this many linear feet of baseboard material |
| `ceiling_height_ft` | Floor to ceiling | 8.0 | Multiplied by wall length = wall area for paint/drywall |
| `wall_area_sf` | Total wall surface | 157.6 SF | Paint coverage calculation (÷ 350 = gallons needed) |
| `ceiling_area_sf` | Ceiling surface | 49.5 SF | Ceiling paint, tiles, or drywall |
| `window_count` | Number of windows | 1 | Window replacement quotes, blind/curtain ordering |
| `door_count` | Number of doors | 1 | Door replacement quotes, subtract from baseboard length |
| `width_ft` / `depth_ft` | Room dimensions | 7.5 × 6.6 | Layout planning, furniture fit, appliance sizing |
| `width_mm` / `depth_mm` | Same in millimeters | 2255 × 2010 | Precision ordering for countertops, custom cabinetry |
| `source` | Where data came from | `pdf_extraction` | Audit trail — was this measured on-site or from drawings? |
| `extraction_confidence` | AI confidence score | 0.85 | Flag low-confidence rooms for manual verification |
| `extras` | JSONB overflow | `{"has_soffit": true}` | Anything unusual that doesn't fit standard columns |

### 💡 Practical Uses
- **"How much flooring do I need for the whole project?"** → `SELECT SUM(area_sf) * 1.10 FROM unit_room_measurements` (10% waste)
- **"Which rooms have the most paintable wall?"** → Sort by `wall_area_sf` descending
- **"Flag rooms where AI wasn't confident"** → Filter `extraction_confidence < 0.75`

---

## 🗄️ Table 2: `unit_room_calculations`

**What it is:** Your extensible cost ledger. Every supply estimate, material cost, labor hour, appliance price, and invoice allocation lives here.

**Multiple rows per room** — one row per metric. This is what makes it infinitely extensible.

| Column | Meaning | Example | How It's Used |
|---|---|---|---|
| `unit_room_id` | Which room | Bathroom in Unit 201 | Links to measurements |
| `metric_key` | Machine identifier | `appliance_cost` | Used in formulas and view pivots |
| `metric_label` | Human-readable name | "Appliance Cost" | What you see in NocoDB column headers |
| `category` | Grouping | `cost`, `supply`, `invoice`, `labor`, `measurement` | Filter in NocoDB: show me only costs, only supplies |
| `trade_key` | Which trade | `plumbing`, `painting`, `appliances` | Filter by trade, roll up trade totals |
| `value_numeric` | The number | 350.00 | The actual value — dollars, gallons, hours, count |
| `value_text` | Text/notes | "Kohler K-3817 toilet" | Product names, vendor notes, descriptions |
| `unit_of_measure` | Units | `$`, `gal`, `lf`, `ea`, `hrs` | Tells you what value_numeric means |
| `is_calculated` | Auto-computed? | `true` | If true, recalculate when inputs change |
| `is_editable` | User can change? | `true` | All rows are editable in NocoDB by default |
| `source` | Origin | `user_input`, `formula`, `invoice` | Track where numbers came from |
| `formula` | How it was computed | `wall_area_sf / 350` | Human-readable — helps you understand and verify |

### 💡 Examples of What Goes Here

**Area-based metrics:**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | How calculated |
|---|---|---|---|
| `paint_wall_gal` | 0.45 | gal | wall_area_sf ÷ 350 (coverage rate) |
| `flooring_sf` | 54.5 | sf | area_sf × 1.10 (10% waste) |
| `flooring_cost` | 245.25 | $ | flooring_sf × cost_per_sf |

**Per-item metrics (NOT area-based):**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `appliance_cost` | 4,200 | $ | Fridge + stove + dishwasher — flat cost |
| `toilet_cost` | 350 | $ | Per unit, not per SF |
| `faucet_cost` | 185 | $ | Per item |
| `door_cost_per_unit` | 220 | $ | Per door × door_count |
| `light_fixture_cost` | 95 | ea/$ | Per fixture |

**Invoice proportioning:**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sub_invoice_allocated` | 877.22 | $ | This room's share of a $50K painting contract |
| `sub_invoice_share_pct` | 27.6 | % | Based on wall area ratio |

**Labor:**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `labor_hours_est` | 4.5 | hrs | Estimated time for this room |
| `labor_rate_hr` | 45.00 | $/hr | Crew rate |
| `labor_cost` | 202.50 | $ | hours × rate |

### 💡 Practical Uses
- **"What's the total appliance budget?"** → Filter `metric_key = 'appliance_cost'`, sum `value_numeric`
- **"Show me everything the plumber needs"** → Filter `trade_key = 'plumbing'`
- **"What did the user manually enter vs what was calculated?"** → Filter by `source`
- **Add a new metric anytime** — just insert a row with a new `metric_key`. No migration needed.

---

## 🗄️ Table 3: `metric_definitions`

**What it is:** A dictionary of known metric types. Think of it as a dropdown menu for NocoDB — when adding a new calculation, pick from this list for consistency.

| Column | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `metric_key` | Unique identifier | `paint_wall_gal` |
| `metric_label` | Display name | "Wall Paint (gal)" |
| `category` | Group | `supply` |
| `trade_key` | Trade | `painting` |
| `unit_of_measure` | Units | `gal` |
| `default_formula` | Suggested calculation | `wall_area_sf / 350` |
| `description` | Explanation | "Gallons for walls at 350sf/gal coverage" |
| `sort_order` | Display ordering | 100 |

### 💡 Practical Use
- **Consistency** — everyone uses `toilet_cost` not "toilet price" or "wc cost"
- **Onboarding** — new team members see what metrics exist and how they're calculated
- **You can add new definitions anytime** — e.g., `vanity_cost`, `tile_sf`, `grout_bags`

---

## 👁️ View 1: `v_unit_cost_sheet`

**What it is:** Your **main working spreadsheet**. Every room in every unit as a row, with measurements and the most common cost metrics pivoted into columns.

**144 rows** (one per unit-room across all 20 units)

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `unit_type`, `bedrooms` | Unit identity |
| `room_name` | Which room |
| `area_sf` through `door_count` | Physical measurements from the wide table |
| `paint_wall_gal` | Gallons of wall paint needed |
| `flooring_sf`, `flooring_cost` | Flooring material and cost |
| `baseboard_lf`, `baseboard_cost` | Baseboard linear feet and cost |
| `appliance_cost` | Per-item appliance total (not area-based) |
| `door_cost_total` | Per-item door total |
| `labor_cost` | Labor estimate |
| `sub_invoice_allocated` | This room's share of subcontractor invoices |
| `total_cost` | Everything summed |

### 💡 How to Use in NocoDB
- **Filter** by `floor = 3` to see just third-floor rooms
- **Sort** by `flooring_cost` descending to find the most expensive flooring jobs
- **Group** by `unit_number` to see per-unit breakdowns
- **Hide columns** you don't need for a specific meeting or report
- **NULL values** mean no data yet — rooms without calculations show blank, not zero

---

## 👁️ View 2: `v_project_cost_summary`

**What it is:** **One row per unit** — everything rolled up. Your executive dashboard.

**20 rows** (one per unit)

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `bedrooms` | Unit identity |
| `room_count` | How many rooms in this unit |
| `total_room_area_sf` | Combined floor area of all rooms |
| `total_wall_area_sf` | Combined wall surface |
| `total_flooring_cost` | Sum of all flooring costs |
| `total_paint_gal` | Total paint gallons (wall + ceiling) |
| `total_appliance_cost` | Sum of all appliance costs (per-item, not per-SF) |
| `total_invoice_allocated` | Sum of all subcontractor allocations |
| `total_cost` | Grand total for the unit |
| `cost_per_sf` | Total cost ÷ unit area — **only meaningful for area-based costs** |

### 💡 How to Use
- **"Which unit is most expensive?"** → Sort by `total_cost`
- **"Compare cost per SF across unit types"** → Group by `unit_type`, compare `cost_per_sf`
- **"How much paint for the whole project?"** → Sum the `total_paint_gal` column
- **"Budget report for the owner"** → Export this view as CSV

---

## 👁️ View 3: `v_trade_cost_comparison`

**What it is:** Every calculation row with its trade, unit, and room context. **Filter by trade** to see that trade's costs across the entire project.

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `trade_key` | Which trade (painting, plumbing, etc.) |
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `room_name` | Location |
| `metric_key`, `metric_label` | What's being measured |
| `value_numeric`, `unit_of_measure` | The value |
| `category` | cost, supply, invoice, labor |
| `cost_per_sf` | Value ÷ room area — **only shown for $ metrics, NULL for per-item costs** |

### 💡 How to Use
- **"Show me all painting costs"** → Filter `trade_key = 'painting'`
- **"Compare plumbing cost per SF across units"** → Filter trade, sort by `cost_per_sf`
- **"Which rooms have the highest cost per SF for flooring?"** → Filter + sort
- **Note:** `cost_per_sf` will be NULL for per-item costs like toilets — this is correct behavior

---

## 👁️ View 4: `v_all_calculations`

| `metric_key`, `metric_label` | What metric |
| `category`, `trade_key` | Classification |
| `value_numeric`, `value_text` | The values |
| `unit_of_measure` | Units |

| `source` | Where it came from |
| `formula` | How it was computed |

### 💡 How to Use

- **"What formulas are being used?"** → Filter `is_calculated = true`, read `formula` column
- **"Find all invoice allocations"** → Filter `category = 'invoice'`
- **"What text notes exist?"** → Filter `value_text IS NOT NULL`

tables\n\n---\n\n## Entity Relationship Overview\n\n```\norganizations (tenant root)\n ├── wo_projects (job sites / properties)\n │    ├── units (apartments / physical spaces)\n │    │    ├── (polymorphic)\n │    └── work_order_templates (project-scoped)\n ├── rooms (master room catalog)\n ├── template_libraries\n │    └── work_order_templates (library-scoped)\n ├── portal_table_config\n └── work_order_templates (company-scoped)\n\nagent_memories (standalone — Agent Zero)\nmetric_definitions (standalone — reference catalog)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core / Tenancy\n\n### `organizations`\n**The multi-tenant root.** Every business entity (contractor, property manager) is an organization. Nearly all other tables reference this directly or indirectly via foreign keys. RLS policies use `organization_id` to isolate tenant data.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | Tenant identifier |\n| `name` | text | Display name (e.g., \"Acme Renovations\") |\n| `slug` | text | URL-safe identifier, unique |\n| `settings` | jsonb | Tenant-specific configuration (feature flags, preferences) |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n🔒 RLS Enabled\n\n---\n\n### `wo_projects`\n**A job site or property.** Represents a physical location where renovation work happens (e.g., \"123 Main Street Condos\"). Contains multiple units. Linked to a template library that defines which trade checklists are available.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | Tenant owner |\n| `name` | text | Project name |\n| `description` | text | Notes, scope summary |\n| `status` | text | `draft` · `active` · `completed` · `archived` |\n| `project_type` | text | Classification (e.g., \"residential\", \"commercial\") |\n| `library_id` | uuid FK → template_libraries | Which template collection this project uses |\n| `metadata` | jsonb | Flexible project-level data |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n---\n\n### `rooms`\n**Master room type catalog.** A lookup table of room names (Kitchen, Bathroom, Bedroom, etc.) scoped per organization. Referenced by `unit_rooms` and `work_order_tasks` to maintain consistent naming.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | Tenant owner |\n| `name` | text | Room type name (e.g., \"Kitchen\") |\n| `display_order` | int4 | Sort position in UI dropdowns |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n🔒 RLS Enabled\n\n---\n\n### `template_libraries`\n**Importable collections of work order templates.** A library groups related templates (e.g., \"Standard Residential Painting\") that can be assigned to projects. Supports the three-tier template hierarchy: App → Company → Project.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `name` | text | Library name |\n| `description` | text | What this collection covers |\n| `project_type` | text | Which project types this library applies to |\n| `scope` | text | `app` (system-wide) · `company` (per-org) |\n| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | NULL for app-scope libraries |\n| `icon` | text | UI icon identifier |\n| `color` | text | UI color code |\n| `metadata` | jsonb | Additional library configuration |\n| `is_active` | bool | Whether available for new projects |\n| `is_default` | bool | Auto-assigned to new projects of matching type |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n---\n\n## 2. Work Orders\n\n### `work_order_templates`\n**Trade checklist definitions.** The blueprint for a work order. Contains a JSONB `definition` with sections, columns, and steps that define what work needs to be done for a specific trade (painting, plumbing, etc.). Supports a three-tier inheritance hierarchy via `parent_template_id`.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | NULL for app-scope templates |\n| `trade_name` | text | Trade identifier (e.g., \"painting\", \"plumbing\") |\n| `version` | int4 | Template version number |\n| `is_active` | bool | Whether available for new work orders |\n| `definition` | jsonb | **The checklist structure** — sections → columns → steps |\n| `scope` | text | `app` · `company` · `project` |\n| `project_id` | uuid FK → wo_projects | Set only for project-scoped templates |\n| `parent_template_id` | uuid FK → self | Inheritance: which template this overrides |\n| `copied_from_template_id` | uuid FK → self | Lineage: which template this was cloned from |\n| `library_id` | uuid FK → template_libraries | Which library this belongs to |\n| `created_by` | uuid | User who created |\n| `updated_by` | uuid | User who last modified |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n🔒 RLS Enabled · **Key JSONB**: `definition` contains `{ sections: { \"Prep\": { columns: [...] }, \"Finish\": { columns: [...] } } }`\n\n---\n\n### `work_orders`\n**A template instance for a specific unit.** When a work order is created, the system snapshots the template and generates tasks for each room. Tracks the full lifecycle from assignment through inspection signoff.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | Tenant owner |\n| `unit_id` | uuid FK → units | Which apartment/space |\n| `template_id` | uuid FK → work_order_templates | Which checklist definition |\n| `trade_name` | text | Denormalized trade name for quick filtering |\n| `status` | text | `not_started` · `in_progress` · `completed` |\n| `assigned_contractor_id` | uuid | Worker/crew assigned |\n| `inspector_id` | uuid | Inspector for signoff |\n| `start_date` | timestamptz | Scheduled start |\n| `completion_date` | timestamptz | Actual completion |\n| `inspector_signoff_date` | timestamptz | When inspector approved |\n| `metadata` | jsonb | Additional work order data |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n🔒 RLS Enabled\n\n---\n\n### `work_order_tasks`\n**Room-level checklist execution.** One row per room per work order. The `task_data` JSONB contains the actual step-by-step checklist state — which items are completed, skipped, or pending. This is what field workers interact with.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `work_order_id` | uuid FK → work_orders | Parent work order |\n| `room_id` | uuid FK → rooms | Which room this task covers |\n| `row_order` | int4 | Display order in the UI |\n| `task_data` | jsonb | **The checklist state** — steps with completion status |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n🔒 RLS Enabled · **Key JSONB**: `task_data` contains `{ steps: [{ key, label, status, completed_by, completed_at, not_applicable }] }`\n\n---\n\n### `work_order_notes`\n**Comments and voice log entries on work orders.** Free-text notes attached to a work order by any team member. Units & Rooms\n\n### `units`\n**Physical spaces — apartments, condos, suites.** Each unit belongs to a project and organization. Contains basic info like floor, type, and area. The `metadata` JSONB holds unit-specific notes and special instructions.\n\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | uuid PK | — |\n| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | Tenant owner |\n| `project_id` | uuid FK → wo_projects | Which property |\n| `unit_number` | text | Display identifier (e.g., \"201\", \"A-101\") |\n| `unit_type` | text | Floor plan type (A, B, C, D, E, F) |\n| `floor` | int4 | Building floor number |\n| `bedrooms` | int4 | Bedroom count |\n| `area_sf` | numeric | Total unit area in square feet |\n| `status` | text | `not_started` · `in_progress` · `completed` |\n| `floor_plan_image` | text | URL to floor plan image |\n| `dimensions` | jsonb | Detailed dimension data |\n| `metadata` | jsonb | Special instructions, notes |\n| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |\n| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |\n\n🔒 RLS Enabled · **Unique**: (`project_id`, `unit_number`)\n\n---\n\n### `unit_rooms`\n**Room instances within a unit.** Links a unit to rooms from the master catalog with unit-specific measurements. import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';

const projectDefinitionsRouter = new OpenAPIHono();

const supabaseUrl = process.env.SUPABASE_URL || 'http://localhost:54321';
const supabaseKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY || 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImV4cCI6MTk4MzgxMjk5Nn0.EGIM96RAZx35lJzdJsyH-qQwv8Hdp7fsn3W0YpN81IU';
const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey);

const ProjectDefinitionSchema = z.object({
  project_id: z.string().uuid(),
  taxonomy_version: z.string().nullable(),
  floorplan_definitions: z.record(z.any()), // JSONB
});

const UpdateDefinitionSchema = z.object({
  floorplan_definitions: z.record(z.any()),
});

// GET /projects (List)
projectDefinitionsRouter.openapi(
  createRoute({
    method: 'get',
    path: '/',
    responses: {
      200: {
        description: 'List Projects',
        content: {
          'application/json': {
            schema: z.array(z.object({
              id: z.string().uuid(),
              name: z.string(),
              code: z.string(),
            })),
          },
        },
      },
    },
  }),
  async (c) => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('projects')
      .select('id, name, code');

    if (error) {
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    }
    return c.json(data);
  }
);

// GET /projects/:id/definitions
projectDefinitionsRouter.openapi(
  createRoute({
    method: 'get',
    path: '/{id}/definitions',
    responses: {
      200: {
        description: 'Get Project Definitions',
        content: {
          'application/json': {
            schema: ProjectDefinitionSchema.nullable(),
          },
        },
      },
      404: { description: 'Project not found' }
    },
  }),
  async (c) => {
    const id = c.req.param('id');
    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('project_definitions')
      .select('*')
      .eq('project_id', id)
      .single();

    if (error && error.code !== 'PGRST116') { // PGRST116 is "The result contains 0 rows"
       return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    }
    
    // Return null if not found (frontend should handle it) or create default?
    return c.json(data || null);
  }
);

// PUT /projects/:id/definitions
projectDefinitionsRouter.openapi(
  createRoute({
    method: 'put',
    path: '/{id}/definitions',
    request: {
      body: {
        content: {
          'application/json': {
            schema: UpdateDefinitionSchema,
          },
        },
      },
    },
    responses: {
      200: {
        description: 'Updated Project Definition',
        content: {
            'application/json': {
              schema: ProjectDefinitionSchema,
            },
          },
      },
      500: { description: 'Error updating definitions' }
    },
  }),
  async (c) => {
    const id = c.req.param('id');
    const { floorplan_definitions } = c.req.valid('json');

    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('project_definitions')
      .upsert({
        project_id: id,
        floorplan_definitions
      }, { onConflict: 'project_id' })
      .select()
      .single();

    if (error) {
        console.error('Error updating project definitions:', error);
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    }

    return c.json(data);
  }
);

export default projectDefinitionsRouter;
