```
2. Enable Row Level Security (RLS) on the config table to ensure tenants can only see their own configuration: `ALTER TABLE portal_table_config ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;`.
3. Implement a frontend application (e.g., using SolidJS) that authenticates via Supabase Auth.
4. Use the Supabase client to fetch the `portal_table_config` for the logged-in user's organization.
5. Dynamically render data grids that toggle between read-only and editable modes based on the `access_level` and `editable_columns` defined in the configuration for each table."}

{'problem': 'Database schema and data need to be extracted from a live Supabase instance and prepared for a clean restoration/migration into a local environment.', 'solution': "1. Use `pg_dump` to create a full schema and data backup: `pg_dump -h host -p port -U user -d dbname > backup.sql`.
2. Use a script (e.g., Python) to parse the SQL dump and separate it into individual migration files (e.g., `00001_extensions.sql`, `00002_tables.sql`, etc.) to maintain dependency order.
3. For seed data, extract table data into `INSERT` statements, ensuring they are ordered by foreign key dependencies to avoid constraint violations.
4. Wrap the seed data in `SET session_replication_role = 'replica';` and `SET session_replication_role = 'origin';` to temporarily bypass circular reference checks during the import.
5. Place migration files in `supabase/migrations/` and the seed file at `supabase/seed.sql` to enable restoration via `supabase db reset`."}

Local Supabase development environment configuration for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Connection Details:
- API URL: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL Port: 54322 (Host: host.docker.internal, User: postgres, Password: postgres, DB: postgres)
- Studio Port: 54323

Authentication Keys:
- Anon Key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJleHAiOjE5ODM4MTI5OTZ9.CRXP1A7WOeoJeXxjNni43kdQwgnWNReilDMblYTn_I0
- Service Role Key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImV4cCI6MTk4MzgxMjk5Nn0.EGIM96RAZx35lJzdJsyH-qQwv8Hdp7fsn3W0YpN81IU

Features & Integration:
- Includes pgvector support for agent memories.
- Serves as the external data source for NocoDB (connected via host.docker.internal:54322).

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform for multi-unit projects, often referred to as the 'timesheet_app'. It utilizes a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo architecture organized into 'apps/' (web-dashboard, material-admin, work-orders, site-companion, tenant-portal), 'packages/' (@repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils), and 'supabase' directories, following a 'schema-first' philosophy. The project architecture follows a three-tier template hierarchy (App, Company, and Project). Tech stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Hono (API), Supabase (PostgreSQL, ^2.90.1), NocoDB, Zod, and Tailwind CSS. The database employs a hybrid relational-JSONB data model. Apps and ports: API (:3000), Web Dashboard (:3001), Material Admin (:3002), Site Companion PWA (:3003), Work Orders (:3004), and Tenant Portal. Documentation: docs/general/platform_analysis.md.

kvps.memories:
Routing is hash-based using @solidjs/router with paths: #/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, and #/admin.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface (Option 1, replacing NocoDB for tenants), designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting both Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Dynamic table viewing and editing driven by `portal_table_config`. Configurable visibility/editability per organization.
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using existing Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions (read-only vs. editable).

kvps.memories:
# `Agent Zero`

[![Agent Zero Website](https://img.shields.io/badge/Website-agent--zero.ai-0A192F?style=for-the-badge&logo=vercel&logoColor=white)](https://agent-zero.ai) [![Thanks to Sponsors](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub%20Sponsors-Thanks%20to%20Sponsors-FF69B4?style=for-the-badge&logo=githubsponsors&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/sponsors/agent0ai) [![Follow on X](https://img.shields.io/badge/X-Follow-000000?style=for-the-badge&logo=x&logoColor=white)](https://x.com/Agent0ai) [![Join our Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-Join%20our%20server-5865F2?style=for-the-badge&logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/B8KZKNsPpj) [![Subscribe on YouTube](https://img.shields.io/badge/YouTube-Subscribe-red?style=for-the-badge&logo=youtube&logoColor=white)](https://www.youtube.com/@AgentZeroFW) [![Connect on LinkedIn](https://img.shields.io/badge/LinkedIn-Connect-blue?style=for-the-badge&logo=linkedin&logoColor=white)](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jan-tomasek/) [![Follow on Warpcast](https://img.shields.io/badge/Warpcast-Follow-5A32F3?style=for-the-badge)](https://warpcast.com/agent-zero) ## Documentation: [Introduction](#a-personal-organic-agentic-framework-that-grows-and-learns-with-you) • [Installation](./docs/setup/installation.md) • [How to update](./docs/setup/installation.md#how-to-update-agent-zero)
[Development Setup](./docs/setup/dev-setup.md) • [Usage](./docs/guides/usage.md) Or see DeepWiki generated documentation: [![Ask DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/badge.svg)](https://deepwiki.com/agent0ai/agent-zero)
> ### 🚨 **AGENT ZERO SKILLS** 🚨 > **Skills System** - portable, structured agent capabilities using the open `SKILL.md` standard (compatible with Claude Code, Codex and more). > > **Plus:** Git-based Projects with authentication for public/private repositories - clone codebases directly into isolated workspaces. > > See [Usage Guide](./docs/guides/usage.md) and [Projects Tutorial](./docs/guides/projects.md) to get started.
[![Showcase](/docs/res/showcase-thumb.png)](https://youtu.be/lazLNcEYsiQ) ## A personal, organic agentic framework that grows and learns with you - Agent Zero is not a predefined agentic framework. It is designed to be dynamic, organically growing, and learning as you use it. - Agent Zero is fully transparent, readable, comprehensible, customizable, and interactive. - Agent Zero uses the computer as a tool to accomplish its (your) tasks. # ⚙️ Installation Click to open a video to learn how to install Agent Zero: [![Easy Installation guide](/docs/res/easy_ins_vid.png)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w5v5Kjx51hs) A detailed setup guide for Windows, macOS, and Linux with a video can be found in the Agent Zero Documentation at [this page](./docs/setup/installation.md). ### ⚡ Quick Start ```bash # Pull and run with Docker docker pull agent0ai/agent-zero docker run -p 50001:80 agent0ai/agent-zero # Visit http://localhost:50001 to start ``` # 💡 Key Features 1. **General-purpose Assistant** - Agent Zero is not pre-programmed for specific tasks (but can be). It is meant to be a general-purpose personal assistant. Give it a task, and it will gather information, execute commands and code, cooperate with other agent instances, and do its best to accomplish it. - It has a persistent memory, allowing it to memorize previous solutions, code, facts, instructions, etc., to solve tasks faster and more reliably in the future. ![Agent 0 Working](/docs/res/ui_screen2.png) 2. **Computer as a Tool**

    content = content.replace(

    print('Config updated with tenant portal port')
else:

print('\nUpdated config:')
with open(config_path, 'r') as f:
    print(f.read())

kvps.memories:
User is building a construction renovation management platform monorepo named 'voicelog-scaffold' using SolidJS, Hono, Turborepo, and Supabase.

The database contains 21 base tables (e.g., work_orders, units, organizations) and 5 views (e.g., v_budget_tracker, v_unit_cost_sheet).

A 'Tenant Portal' app was created at 'apps/tenant-portal/' running on port 3005, supporting Supabase Auth (email/password and magic links).

The Tenant Portal uses a configuration-driven UI where table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.

The 'portal_table_config' table stores settings for access_level (hidden, readonly, editable), visible_columns, editable_columns, and display preferences.

Row Level Security (RLS) is implemented across the database to ensure tenant isolation based on 'organization_id'.

The project configuration in 'packages/config/src/index.ts' includes ports and CORS origins for web-dashboard (3001), material-admin (3002), mobile-app (3003), work-orders (3004), and tenant-portal (3005).

logs.heading: Solution memorization completed: 2 solutions processed, 2 intelligently consolidated
logs.content:
```json
[
  {
    "problem": "The user wants to build a tenant-facing web portal for a Supabase-backed construction management platform that allows specific database tables and views to be visible or editable based on per-tenant configuration, with support for Supabase Auth (email/password and magic links).",
    "solution": "1. **Database Schema**: Create a `portal_table_config` table to store per-organization settings (access_level: hidden/readonly/editable, visible_columns, editable_columns, display_order, etc.). Implement Row Level Security (RLS) to ensure tenants only see their own configuration.
2. **App Scaffolding**: Create a new SolidJS application in the monorepo (`apps/tenant-portal/`) using Vite and Tailwind CSS. Configure the dev server to run on a unique port (e.g., 3005).
3. **Authentication**: Implement a `LoginPage` using `@supabase/supabase-js` supporting both `signInWithPassword` and `signInWithOtp` (magic links). Use an `AuthGuard` component to protect routes.
4. **Dynamic Routing**: Use a hash-based router to handle navigation within iframes. Define routes for `#/login`, `#/dashboard`, `#/table/:tableName`, and `#/admin`.
5. **Dynamic Data Grid**: Build a `DataGrid` component that:
    - Fetches data from Supabase using `supabase.from(tableName).select('*', { count: 'exact' })`.
    - Discovers columns dynamically from the first row of data.
    - Filters columns based on `visible_columns` and `hidden_columns` from the config.
    - Implements inline editing for tables marked as `editable` using a `supabase.update()` call on cell blur or Enter key.
    - Supports server-side pagination and sorting.
6. **Admin Configuration Interface**: Create an `AdminConfigPage` accessible to super-admins that allows selecting an organization and toggling access levels and column permissions for all available tables and views using the Supabase `service_role` key to bypass RLS.
7. **Shared Configuration**: Update the monorepo's shared config package to include the new application's port and add its URL to the `CORS_ORIGINS` list."
  },
  {
    "problem": "NocoDB (v0.300+) encountered 'Forbidden - Unauthorized access' or 'Base not accessible' errors due to JWT verification failures or broken workspace permissions after container rebuilds or metadata persistence in the primary database.",
    "solution": "1. **Isolate Metadata**: Reconfigure NocoDB to use a separate SQLite file for its internal metadata instead of the primary PostgreSQL database to avoid stale permission records. Update `docker-compose.yml` to remove database environment variables for NocoDB.
2. **Clean Volumes**: Stop the NocoDB container and force-remove the persistent data volume using `docker compose down -v` or `docker volume rm <volume_name>` to regenerate valid authentication secrets and workspace IDs.
3. **Database Cleanup**: If metadata was previously stored in Postgres, manually drop the NocoDB-specific tables (prefixed with `nc_` and `xc_`) to ensure a clean state for the next connection attempt.
4. **Version Downgrade**: If permission issues persist in v0.300+, downgrade the NocoDB image to a stable version like `v0.252.0` where the workspace permission system is more reliable."
  }
]
```

# Voicelog Scaffold — Comprehensive Platform Analysis

> **Generated**: 2026-02-17 | **Author**: Agent Zero (Developer)
> **Scope**: Full monorepo architecture, data model, services, current state, and strategic direction

---

## 1. What You're Building

A **construction renovation management platform** — a suite of interconnected applications that digitize the entire lifecycle of multi-unit renovation projects, from initial scope definition through work execution, inspection, and closeout.

The platform solves a specific industry pain point: construction teams juggle spreadsheets, paper checklists, voice memos, and disconnected tools. This system unifies everything into a single data model where:

- **Office staff** define project scopes, manage templates, upload architectural drawings, and track progress from dashboards
- **Field workers** execute work orders room-by-room using mobile-optimized checklists, log time via voice, and capture site conditions
- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol
- **NocoDB** provides a spreadsheet-like interface for bulk data management and ad-hoc queries without custom UI development

The platform is **multi-tenant** (organizations), **multi-project** (wo_projects), and **multi-trade** (painting, plumbing, carpentry, etc.).

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

### 2.1 Philosophy

Three core principles drive every technical decision:

1. **Schema-First**: Zod schemas in `packages/schemas` are the single source of truth. If it's not in a schema, it doesn't exist. This guarantees type safety from database to UI.
2. **Monorepo with Shared DNA**: Multiple apps share the same data model, validation logic, and configuration. A change to a schema propagates instantly to all consumers.
3. **Hybrid Relational + JSONB**: Relational columns for indexing, foreign keys, and permissions. JSONB columns for deeply nested hierarchical data (template definitions, scope trees, overrides).

### 2.2 Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm | Fast parallel builds, workspace linking, dependency deduplication |
| **Frontend** | SolidJS + Vite | True reactivity via signals (no virtual DOM), sub-second HMR |
| **Backend** | Hono (OpenAPIHono) | Lightweight, web-standard, runs on Node/Edge/Deno. Built-in OpenAPI generation |
| **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) | Managed Postgres with Auth, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Row-Level Security |
| **Validation** | Zod | Runtime validation + static TypeScript inference from a single definition |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first, consistent design system across apps |
| **Voice/AI** | Anthropic Claude + OpenAI Whisper | Voice transcription → LLM structured extraction |
| **Document Parsing** | FastAPI + Detectron2 + LayoutParser | GPU-accelerated PDF parsing, floor plan detection, image extraction |
| **Spreadsheet UI** | NocoDB | No-code database interface for bulk editing relational data |
| **Agent Protocol** | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) | JSON-RPC interoperability between AI agents |

### 2.3 Service Topology

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        USER DEVICES                             │
├──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────────────────┤
│ Work     │ Material │ Site     │ Web      │ NocoDB              │
│ Orders   │ Admin    │ Companion│ Dashboard│ Spreadsheet         │
│ :3004    │ :3002    │ :3003    │ :3001    │ :8082               │
│ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ Docker              │
│ Tailwind │ Tailwind │ PWA      │ Legacy   │                     │
└────┬──��──┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴──────────┬──────────┘
     │          │          │          │                │
     └──────────┴──────────┴──────────┘                │
                      │                                │
              ┌───────▼────────┐                       │
              │   Hono API     │                       │
              │   :3000        │                       │
              │                │                       │
              │ /work-orders   │                       │
              │ /materials     │                       │
              │ /voice         │                       │
              │ /projects 

<< 8378 Characters hidden >>

 Deep dive into a single template's sections and columns
- `WorkOrderDetail` — Execute a work order: room-by-room checklist with step completion
- `Projects` — Manage wo_projects (job sites)
- `ProjectDetail` — Single project view with units, work orders, documents
- `NewProject` — Project creation wizard
- `ProjectDocuments` — Upload and manage architectural drawings/PDFs

### 5.3 Site Companion (`apps/site-companion`)

**Framework**: SolidJS PWA
**Port**: 3003
**Purpose**: Mobile-optimized field worker interface

**Pages:**
- `UnitList` — Browse assigned units
- `UnitDetail` — View unit scope, execute work items
- `VoiceTest` — Voice recording → transcription → structured data extraction

**Key Feature**: Designed as a Progressive Web App for offline-capable field use. Workers can record voice memos that get transcribed and parsed into structured work log entries.

### 5.4 Material Admin (`apps/material-admin`)

**Framework**: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS
**Port**: 3002
**Purpose**: Supplier catalog, product management, taxonomy editing

**Features:**
- Supplier CRUD with contact info
- Product catalog with JSONB attributes for spec matching
- Project supplies (consumables) management
- Taxonomy editing (room types, actions, categories)
- JSON tree editor for complex nested definitions
- BOM (Bill of Materials) flattened view

### 5.5 Web Dashboard (`apps/web-dashboard`)

**Framework**: SolidJS (minimal)
**Port**: 3001
**Purpose**: Legacy/reference admin view

**Features:**
- Login via Supabase Auth
- Basic todo management (reference implementation)
- Hono RPC client integration example

---

## 6. Supporting Services

### 6.1 Doc Parser (`doc-parser/`)

**Framework**: FastAPI (Python)
**Port**: 5000
**Infrastructure**: Docker with GPU support (Detectron2, LayoutParser)

**Capabilities:**
- PDF upload and page rendering
- Layout analysis (text blocks, tables, figures)
- Floor plan detection and extraction
- Image upload to Supabase Storage
- Text extraction via PyMuPDF

**Integration**: Called via HTTP from the Hono API or directly from Agent Zero. Uploads extracted images to Supabase Storage and returns structured metadata.

### 6.2 NocoDB (`nocodb/`)

**Port**: 8082
**Purpose**: Spreadsheet interface directly connected to the Supabase PostgreSQL database

**Use Cases:**
- Bulk editing relational data (units, rooms, taxonomy)
- Viewing flattened SQL views of JSONB data (`view_project_bom`)
- Ad-hoc queries without building custom UI
- Deep linking to the Material Admin app for complex JSONB editing

### 6.3 A2A Protocol

The API exposes an Agent-to-Agent JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a` with an agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`. This enables:
- External AI agents (like Agent Zero) to discover and interact with the platform
- Structured task delegation (e.g., "create a work order for unit 101")
- Multi-agent workflows across different AI systems

---

## 7. Current State Assessment

### 7.1 What's Built and Working

| Component | Status | Maturity |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Monorepo infrastructure | ✅ Complete | Production-ready |
| Supabase schema + migrations | ✅ Complete | 6 migrations, well-structured |
| Shared schemas (Zod) | ✅ Complete | Comprehensive type coverage |
| API routes (Hono) | ✅ Complete | 10 route modules, OpenAPI docs |
| Work Orders app | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD, template hierarchy, documents |
| Material Admin app | ✅ Complete | Suppliers, products, taxonomy, JSON editor |
| Site Companion app | ⚠️ Scaffold | Basic unit list, voice test page |
| Web Dashboard | ⚠️ Legacy | Login + todos only |
| Doc Parser | ✅ Complete | GPU pipeline, Supabase integration |
| NocoDB integration | ✅ Complete | Connected to Supabase DB |
| A2A Protocol | ✅ Complete | Agent card + JSON-RPC endpoint |
| Template hierarchy (3-tier) | ✅ Complete | App/Company/Project scoping |
| Document storage | ✅ Complete | Upload, extraction, linking |
| Voice processing | ⚠️ Partial | Transcription works, structured extraction in progress |
| Timesheet system | 📋 Designed | Schema designed, not yet implemented |
| Auth / RLS | ⚠️ Partial | Supabase Auth configured, RLS policies need hardening |
| Realtime subscriptions | ⚠️ Partial | Infrastructure ready, limited app integration |

### 7.2 What's Designed but Not Yet Implemented

**Timesheet System** (documented in `docs/timesheet/`):
- `timesheets` table — daily time containers per user
- `work_logs` table — links hours to specific BOM items (`site_item_id`)
- Milestone tracking (e.g., "Prime 30%", "Finish 100%")
- Earned value calculations
- Voice-to-timesheet pipeline (speak → transcribe → extract hours + items → create work_log)

**Materials/BOM System** (partially documented):
- `site_items` table — exploded BOM from JSONB definitions
- Product-to-scope matching via JSONB attributes
- Supplier order management
- Project supplies tracking

---

## 8. Data Flow Patterns

### 8.1 Work Order Lifecycle

```
1. Admin creates Template (definition JSONB with sections/columns)
         ↓
2. Admin creates Work Order for Unit + Trade
         ↓
3. System "snapshots" template → creates WorkOrderTasks (one per room)
         ↓
4. Field worker opens Site Companion → sees room checklist
         ↓
5. Worker completes steps (task_data JSONB updated)
         ↓
6. Inspector reviews → signs off → work order marked complete
```

### 8.2 Voice Processing Pipeline

```
1. Field worker records voice memo in Site Companion
         ↓
2. Audio sent to API /voice endpoint
         ↓
3. Whisper transcribes audio → text
         ↓
4. Anthropic Claude extracts structured data:
   - Hours worked
   - Items completed
   - Issues/notes
         ↓
5. Structured data creates/updates work_logs, work_order_tasks
```

### 8.3 Document Processing Pipeline

```
1. Admin uploads PDF (architectural drawings) via Work Orders app
         ↓
2. File stored in Supabase Storage, metadata in project_documents
         ↓
3. Doc Parser service called:
   - Renders pages to images
   - Detects layout (tables, figures, floor plans)
   - Extracts text
   - Uploads images to Supabase Storage
         ↓
4. Extracted images stored in document_images table
         ↓
5. Images linked to units/rooms via image_links table
```

### 8.4 NocoDB ↔ Custom App Integration

```
1. NocoDB reads relational data directly from PostgreSQL
         ↓
2. Complex JSONB data exposed via SQL views (view_project_bom)
         ↓
3. "Edit in App" deep links open Material Admin with URL params
         ↓
4. Material Admin's JsonTreeEditor modifies JSONB directly
         ↓
5. Changes visible in NocoDB immediately (same database)
```

---

## 9. Strategic Direction & Recommendations

### 9.1 Immediate Next Steps

1. **Implement Timesheet Tables**: The schema is designed (`docs/timesheet/schema_design.md`). Create the migration for `timesheets`, `work_logs`, and `site_items` tables.

2. **Voice-to-Timesheet Pipeline**: Connect the existing voice transcription to the timesheet data model. This is the platform's killer feature — workers speak their hours and the system creates structured time entries.

3. **Site Companion Buildout**: The PWA scaffold exists but needs the full work order execution UI (room checklists, step completion, photo capture).

4. **RLS Policy Hardening**: Current policies are permissive for development. Production requires proper `organization_id` scoping and role-based access.

### 9.2 Architecture Strengths

- **Schema-first approach** prevents data model drift across 5+ applications
- **JSONB hybrid** elegantly handles the tension between structured queries and deeply nested domain data
- **Template hierarchy** is a sophisticated solution to the "every project is slightly different" problem
- **Monorepo with Turborepo** enables rapid iteration without dependency hell
- **A2A protocol** future-proofs for AI agent integration

### 9.3 Architecture Risks

- **JSONB complexity**: The flattened SQL views are read-only. Any write path for nested data requires the custom app, creating a dependency on the Material Admin for certain operations.
- **NocoDB coupling**: ~90 NocoDB tables in the same database adds metadata overhead and potential migration conflicts.
- **Config package hardcoding**: Supabase URLs and keys are hardcoded in `packages/config`. Should migrate to environment variables for deployment flexibility.
- **Single API server**: All routes in one Hono instance. As the platform grows, consider splitting into domain-specific services.

---

## 10. File Structure Reference

```
voicelog-scaffold/
├── apps/
│   ├── api/                  # Hono backend (The Brain)
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── routes/       # 10 route modules
│   │   │   ├── services/     # Business logic
│   │   │   ├── middleware/   # Auth, validation
│   │   │   └── index.ts      # App entry + route mounting
│   │   └── scripts/          # 50+ utility scripts
│   ├── work-orders/          # Work order management UI
│   ├── material-admin/       # Supplier/product/taxonomy UI
│   ├── site-companion/       # Mobile PWA for field workers
│   └── web-dashboard/        # Legacy admin reference
├── packages/
│   ├── schemas/              # Zod schemas (THE CONTRACT)
│   ├── db/                   # Auto-generated Supabase types
│   ├── config/               # Centralized configuration
│   ├── ui/                   # Shared SolidJS components
│   ├── logger/               # Structured logging
│   ├── utils/                # Shared utilities
│   └── tsconfig/             # Base TypeScript config
├── supabase/
│   ├── config.toml           # Supabase local config
│   └── migrations/           # 6 SQL migrations
├── doc-parser/               # FastAPI document parsing service
├── nocodb/                   # NocoDB Docker config
├── docs/                     # Comprehensive documentation
│   ├── general/              # This analysis
│   ├── workorders/           # Work order implementation docs
│   ├── timesheet/            # Timesheet design docs
│   └── a2a/                  # Agent protocol docs
├── turbo.json                # Turborepo build config
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml       # Workspace definition
└── package.json              # Root dependencies
```

---

*This document reflects the state of the codebase as of 2026-02-17. It should be updated as major architectural decisions are made or new subsystems are implemented.*

    '''│   ├── work-orders/          # Work order management UI
│   ├── material-admin/       # Supplier/product/taxonomy UI
│   ├── site-companion/       # Mobile PWA for field workers
│   └── web-dashboard/        # Legacy admin reference''',
    '''│   ├── work-orders/          # Work order management UI
│   ├── material-admin/       # Supplier/product/taxonomy UI
│   ├── site-companion/       # Mobile PWA for field workers
│   ├── web-dashboard/        # Legacy admin reference
│   └── tenant-portal/        # Configurable tenant data portal
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── pages/        # Login, Dashboard, TableView, AdminConfig
│       │   ├── components/   # Layout, DataGrid, EditableCell, Pagination
│       │   └── lib/          # Supabase clients, router, types
│       └── [config files]    # Vite, Tailwind, TypeScript'''
)

# 8. Add Tenant Portal to the intro list
content = content.replace(
    '- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol',
    '- **Tenants** access their project data through a configurable portal with read-only or editable views\n- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol'
)

# 9. Update the footer date
content = content.replace(
    '*This document reflects the state of the codebase as of 2026-02-17.',
    '*This document reflects the state of the codebase as of 2026-02-18.'
)

# 10. Add a data flow pattern for the Tenant Portal
tenant_data_flow = '''### 8.5 Tenant Portal Access Flow

```
1. Admin configures portal_table_config for an organization
   (sets tables as hidden/readonly/editable, column visibility)
         ↓
2. Tenant logs in via Supabase Auth (email/password or magic link)
         ↓
3. Dashboard fetches portal_table_config (RLS filters by org)
         ↓
4. Tenant clicks a table → TableViewPage queries supabase.from(tableName)
   (RLS automatically filters to tenant's organization data)
         ↓
5. For editable tables: click cell → inline edit → supabase.update()
   (RLS prevents cross-tenant writes)
```

'''

content = content.replace(
    '---\n\n## 9. Strategic Direction',
    tenant_data_flow + '---\n\n## 9. Strategic Direction'
)

with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print('platform_analysis.md updated successfully')
print(f'File size: {len(content)} bytes')

kvps.memories:
The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (located at /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform utilizing a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo architecture. 

Architecture & Structure:
- Apps (in `apps/`): web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app/site-companion (:3003), work-orders (:3004), and tenant-portal (:3005). API runs on :3000.
- Shared Packages (in `packages/`): @repo/config (contains central port/CORS config in `packages/config/src/index.ts`), @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.
- Hierarchy: Three-tier template hierarchy (App, Company, Project).
- Philosophy: Schema-first with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend/Data: Hono (API), Supabase (PostgreSQL, ^2.90.1), NocoDB, Zod.

Documentation: docs/general/platform_analysis.md.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting both Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI where table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration, while the service role handles admin updates.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.

Local Supabase development environment configuration for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Connection Details:

- PostgreSQL Port: 54322 (Host: host.docker.internal, User: postgres, Password: postgres, DB: postgres)

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Build a tenant-facing web portal for a Supabase-backed construction management platform within a Turborepo monorepo. The portal must allow specific database tables and views to be visible or editable based on per-tenant configuration, supporting Supabase Auth (email/password and magic links) and iframe-compatible routing.

# Solution
1. **Database Schema & RLS**: Create a `portal_table_config` table to manage per-organization access:
```sql
CREATE TABLE portal_table_config (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    table_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name TEXT,
    access_level TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'hidden' CHECK (access_level IN ('hidden', 'readonly', 'editable')),
    visible_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    editable_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    display_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    UNIQUE(organization_id, table_name)
);
ALTER TABLE portal_table_config ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```
2. **App Scaffolding**: Create a SolidJS application in `apps/tenant-portal/` using Vite and Tailwind CSS. Configure `vite.config.ts` to run on port 3005. Update the monorepo shared config and `CORS_ORIGINS` to include this new app.
3. **Authentication**: Implement a `LoginPage` using `@supabase/supabase-js` supporting `signInWithPassword` and `signInWithOtp` (magic links). Use an `AuthGuard` component for route protection.
4. **Routing**: Use `<Router source={hashIntegration()}>` from `@solidjs/router` to enable hash-based routing (`#/dashboard`), ensuring compatibility when the portal is embedded in iframes.
5. **Dynamic Data Grid**: Build a `DataGrid` component using SolidJS `createResource` that:
    - Fetches data: `supabase.from(tableName).select('*', { count: 'exact' })`.
    - Dynamically discovers columns and filters them based on `visible_columns` from the config.
    - Implements inline editing for `editable` tables via `supabase.update()` on cell blur/Enter.
    - Supports server-side pagination and sorting.
6. **Admin Configuration**: Create an `AdminConfigPage` for super-admins to toggle access levels and permissions. Use a Supabase client with the `service_role` key to bypass RLS for administrative setup.

kvps.memories:
The project 'voicelog-scaffold' is a construction renovation monorepo using SolidJS, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Hono, and Supabase.

A Tenant Portal application was built and integrated into the monorepo at 'apps/tenant-portal' (Port 3005), featuring Supabase Auth, dynamic table viewing, and inline editing.

The 'portal_table_config' database table manages per-organization table access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility for the Tenant Portal.

The platform architecture follows a 'schema-first' philosophy using Zod schemas in 'packages/schemas' as the single source of truth.

The database schema includes 150 tables, with core application tables for work orders, units, projects, and a significant NocoDB installation.

A comprehensive database restoration plan was implemented with 12 migration files in 'supabase/migrations/', including RLS policies for tenant isolation.

The project includes a 'doc-parser' service (FastAPI, Port 5000) for PDF layout analysis and floor plan detection.

NocoDB is installed (Port 8082) but was reconfigured to use an internal SQLite instance for metadata to separate it from the primary Supabase PostgreSQL database.

The 'platform_analysis.md' document was updated on 2026-02-18 to reflect the current architectural state, including the new Tenant Portal and service topology.

[
  {
    "problem": "The user needs to update a comprehensive platform analysis Markdown document to reflect the addition of a new 'Tenant Portal' application, including architectural diagrams, port allocations, database tables, and data flow patterns.",
    "solution": "1. Read the existing Markdown file to identify target sections for modification.
2. Use a Python script to perform targeted string replacements and insertions to maintain document structure:
    - Update the generation date and versioning info.
    - Modify ASCII service topology diagrams to include the new service and its port (e.g., port 3005).

    - Append new data flow diagrams using Mermaid or ASCII blocks to illustrate the interaction between the portal, RLS, and the database.
    - Update the file structure reference section to include the new application directory and its subfolders (src/pages, src/components, etc.).
3. Verify the updates using 'grep' or by reading the file to ensure all 15+ expected changes across multiple sections were applied correctly."
  },
  {
    "problem": "The user wants to implement a multi-tenant portal where specific database tables can be made visible or editable for external tenants based on configuration.",
    "solution": "1. Create a configuration table `portal_table_config` in the database with columns: `organization_id`, `table_name`, `access_level` (hidden, readonly, editable), and `column_config` (JSONB for visibility/editability per column).
2. Build a frontend using SolidJS and Tailwind CSS featuring:
    - A dynamic DataGrid component that renders rows based on the selected table.
    - Inline editing capabilities for cells when the configuration marks a column as editable.
    - A hash-based router to support deep linking and iframe embedding.
3. Implement authentication using Supabase Auth (supporting email/password and magic links).
4. Secure data access using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies to ensure tenants only see data belonging to their `organization_id` across all application tables.
5. Create an Admin Configuration page that uses the Supabase service role key to manage the `portal_table_config` for different organizations."
  },
  {
    "problem": "NocoDB access errors ('Forbidden' or 'Base not accessible') persisting even after Docker volume resets due to stale metadata in the primary database.",
    "solution": "1. Identify if NocoDB is using an external PostgreSQL database for its own metadata (look for `NC_DB` environment variables).
2. If metadata is stored in the primary DB, either:
    - Truncate/Drop all NocoDB-specific tables (prefixed with `nc_` or `xc_`) to force a clean re-initialization.
    - Or, reconfigure NocoDB to use a local SQLite file for metadata by removing the database connection environment variables from the `docker-compose.yml` file.
3. Stop the NocoDB container and remove its persistent volume using `docker compose down -v`.
4. Restart the service and perform a fresh setup via the NocoDB UI, then connect the primary database as an 'External Data Source' rather than the metadata store."
  },
  {
    "problem": "The user needs to migrate a live Supabase database schema and data to a local development environment while excluding internal metadata (like NocoDB tables).",
    "solution": "1. Install PostgreSQL client tools (`pg_dump`) in the environment.
2. Perform a full schema dump from the source database.
3. Use a Python script to parse the SQL dump and filter out system schemas or specific prefixes (e.g., `nc_` for NocoDB).
4. Organize the extracted SQL into numbered migration files (e.g., `00001_extensions.sql`, `00002_tables.sql`) in the `supabase/migrations/` directory, ensuring correct dependency order (Extensions -> Tables -> Functions -> Views -> RLS Policies).
5. For data seeding, use a script to extract data into `seed.sql`, reordering INSERT statements based on foreign key dependencies and wrapping them in `SET session_replication_role = 'replica';` to bypass circular reference constraints during import."
  }
]
```

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Build a tenant-facing web portal for a Supabase-backed construction management platform within a Turborepo monorepo. The portal must allow specific database tables and views to be visible or editable based on per-tenant configuration, supporting Supabase Auth (email/password and magic links) and iframe-compatible routing.

# Solution
1. **Database Schema & RLS**: Create a `portal_table_config` table to manage per-organization access:
```sql
CREATE TABLE portal_table_config (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    organization_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    table_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name TEXT,
    access_level TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'hidden' CHECK (access_level IN ('hidden', 'readonly', 'editable')),
    column_config JSONB, -- Stores visibility/editability per column
    visible_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    editable_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    display_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    UNIQUE(organization_id, table_name)
);
ALTER TABLE portal_table_config ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```
2. **App Scaffolding**: Create a SolidJS application in `apps/tenant-portal/` using Vite and Tailwind CSS. Configure `vite.config.ts` to run on port 3005. Update the monorepo shared config and `CORS_ORIGINS` to include this new app.
3. **Authentication**: Implement a `LoginPage` using `@supabase/supabase-js` supporting `signInWithPassword` and `signInWithOtp` (magic links). Use an `AuthGuard` component for route protection. Secure data access using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies to ensure tenants only see data belonging to their `organization_id` across all application tables.
4. **Routing**: Use `<Router source={hashIntegration()}>` from `@solidjs/router` to enable hash-based routing (`#/dashboard`), ensuring compatibility when the portal is embedded in iframes.
5. **Dynamic Data Grid**: Build a `DataGrid` component using SolidJS `createResource` that:
    - Fetches data: `supabase.from(tableName).select('*', { count: 'exact' })`.
    - Dynamically discovers columns and filters them based on `column_config` or `visible_columns` from the config.
    - Implements inline editing for `editable` tables via `supabase.update()` on cell blur/Enter.
    - Supports server-side pagination and sorting.
6. **Admin Configuration**: Create an `AdminConfigPage` for super-admins to manage the `portal_table_config` for different organizations. Use a Supabase client with the `service_role` key to bypass RLS for administrative setup.

# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations (Organizations, Units, Work Orders) to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file (e.g., `docs/general/platform_analysis.md`) covering architecture, data models, service topology, and strategic recommendations.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning, generation dates, and ASCII service topology diagrams.
    - Insert new rows into port allocation and service status tables.
    - Add detailed subsections for new apps (e.g., SolidJS framework, Supabase Auth, Dynamic DataGrid features).
    - Append Mermaid or ASCII data flow diagrams illustrating interactions between the app, RLS, and the database.
    - Update file structure references to include new application directories.
4. **Verification**: Use 'grep' or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly.

NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()

-- ========================================
-- PROJECTS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE projects (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  address TEXT,
  client_name TEXT,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK (status IN ('draft', 'active', 'completed', 'archived')),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- PROJECT DOCUMENTS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE project_documents (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  file_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_url TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_size INTEGER,
  uploaded_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  uploaded_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE parsed_document_data (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES project_documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  parsed_content JSONB NOT NULL,
  confidence_score DECIMAL(3,2),
  requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  reviewed_by UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  reviewed_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- UNITS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE units (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  unit_number TEXT NOT NULL,
  type TEXT, -- A, B, C, D, E, F
  bedrooms INTEGER,
  area_sf INTEGER,
  floor INTEGER,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_started' CHECK (status IN ('not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed')),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(project_id, unit_number)
);

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDERS
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  unit_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES units(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  trade TEXT NOT NULL,
  contractor_name TEXT,
  contractor_id UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  start_date DATE,
  completion_date DATE,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_started' CHECK (status IN ('not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed')),
  inspector_signoff_by UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  inspector_signoff_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDER TASKS (Checkboxes)
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_tasks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  organization_id UUID NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  room TEXT NOT NULL,
  task_column TEXT NOT NULL,
  task_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  checked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  not_applicable BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  checked_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  checked_by UUID NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ========================================
-- WORK ORDER TASK HISTORY (For analytics)
-- ========================================
CREATE TABLE work_order_task_history (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES work_order_tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  checked BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  changed_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  changed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Trigger to auto-populate history
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION log_task_change()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  IF (TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.checked != NEW.checked) THEN
    INSERT INTO work_order_task_history (task_id, checked, changed_by, changed_at)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.checked, NEW.checked_by, NEW.checked_at);
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER task_change_logger
  AFTER UPDATE ON work_order_tasks
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION log_task_change();

`area_m2` | Same area in metric | 4.60 | For metric-system suppliers or international reporting |

| `wall_length_paintable_lf` | Exposed wall length | 19.7 LF | Multiply by ceiling height = paintable wall area |
| `baseboard_length_lf` | Baseboard run | 19.7 LF | Order this many linear feet of baseboard material |
| `ceiling_height_ft` | Floor to ceiling | 8.0 | Multiplied by wall length = wall area for paint/drywall |
| `wall_area_sf` | Total wall surface | 157.6 SF | Paint coverage calculation (÷ 350 = gallons needed) |
| `ceiling_area_sf` | Ceiling surface | 49.5 SF | Ceiling paint, tiles, or drywall |
| `window_count` | Number of windows | 1 | Window replacement quotes, blind/curtain ordering |
| `door_count` | Number of doors | 1 | Door replacement quotes, subtract from baseboard length |
| `width_ft` / `depth_ft` | Room dimensions | 7.5 × 6.6 | Layout planning, furniture fit, appliance sizing |
| `width_mm` / `depth_mm` | Same in millimeters | 2255 × 2010 | Precision ordering for countertops, custom cabinetry |
| `source` | Where data came from | `pdf_extraction` | Audit trail — was this measured on-site or from drawings? |
| `extraction_confidence` | AI confidence score | 0.85 | Flag low-confidence rooms for manual verification |
| `extras` | JSONB overflow | `{"has_soffit": true}` | Anything unusual that doesn't fit standard columns |

### 💡 Practical Uses
- **"How much flooring do I need for the whole project?"** → `SELECT SUM(area_sf) * 1.10 FROM unit_room_measurements` (10% waste)
- **"Which rooms have the most paintable wall?"** → Sort by `wall_area_sf` descending
- **"Flag rooms where AI wasn't confident"** → Filter `extraction_confidence < 0.75`

---

## 🗄️ Table 2: `unit_room_calculations`

**What it is:** Your extensible cost ledger. Every supply estimate, material cost, labor hour, appliance price, and invoice allocation lives here.

**Multiple rows per room** — one row per metric. This is what makes it infinitely extensible.

| Column | Meaning | Example | How It's Used |
|---|---|---|---|
| `unit_room_id` | Which room | Bathroom in Unit 201 | Links to measurements |
| `metric_key` | Machine identifier | `appliance_cost` | Used in formulas and view pivots |
| `metric_label` | Human-readable name | "Appliance Cost" | What you see in NocoDB column headers |
| `category` | Grouping | `cost`, `supply`, `invoice`, `labor`, `measurement` | Filter in NocoDB: show me only costs, only supplies |
| `trade_key` | Which trade | `plumbing`, `painting`, `appliances` | Filter by trade, roll up trade totals |
| `value_numeric` | The number | 350.00 | The actual value — dollars, gallons, hours, count |
| `value_text` | Text/notes | "Kohler K-3817 toilet" | Product names, vendor notes, descriptions |
| `unit_of_measure` | Units | `$`, `gal`, `lf`, `ea`, `hrs` | Tells you what value_numeric means |
| `is_calculated` | Auto-computed? | `true` | If true, recalculate when inputs change |
| `is_editable` | User can change? | `true` | All rows are editable in NocoDB by default |
| `source` | Origin | `user_input`, `formula`, `invoice` | Track where numbers came from |
| `formula` | How it was computed | `wall_area_sf / 350` | Human-readable — helps you understand and verify |

### 💡 Examples of What Goes Here

**Area-based metrics:**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | How calculated |
|---|---|---|---|
| `paint_wall_gal` | 0.45 | gal | wall_area_sf ÷ 350 (coverage rate) |
| `flooring_sf` | 54.5 | sf | area_sf × 1.10 (10% waste) |
| `flooring_cost` | 245.25 | $ | flooring_sf × cost_per_sf |

**Per-item metrics (NOT area-based):**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `appliance_cost` | 4,200 | $ | Fridge + stove + dishwasher — flat cost |
| `toilet_cost` | 350 | $ | Per unit, not per SF |
| `faucet_cost` | 185 | $ | Per item |
| `door_cost_per_unit` | 220 | $ | Per door × door_count |
| `light_fixture_cost` | 95 | ea/$ | Per fixture |

**Invoice proportioning:**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sub_invoice_allocated` | 877.22 | $ | This room's share of a $50K painting contract |
| `sub_invoice_share_pct` | 27.6 | % | Based on wall area ratio |

**Labor:**
| metric_key | value_numeric | unit_of_measure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `labor_hours_est` | 4.5 | hrs | Estimated time for this room |
| `labor_rate_hr` | 45.00 | $/hr | Crew rate |
| `labor_cost` | 202.50 | $ | hours × rate |

### 💡 Practical Uses
- **"What's the total appliance budget?"** → Filter `metric_key = 'appliance_cost'`, sum `value_numeric`
- **"Show me everything the plumber needs"** → Filter `trade_key = 'plumbing'`
- **"What did the user manually enter vs what was calculated?"** → Filter by `source`
- **Add a new metric anytime** — just insert a row with a new `metric_key`. No migration needed.

---

## 🗄️ Table 3: `metric_definitions`

**What it is:** A dictionary of known metric types. Think of it as a dropdown menu for NocoDB — when adding a new calculation, pick from this list for consistency.

| Column | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `metric_key` | Unique identifier | `paint_wall_gal` |
| `metric_label` | Display name | "Wall Paint (gal)" |
| `category` | Group | `supply` |
| `trade_key` | Trade | `painting` |
| `unit_of_measure` | Units | `gal` |
| `default_formula` | Suggested calculation | `wall_area_sf / 350` |
| `description` | Explanation | "Gallons for walls at 350sf/gal coverage" |
| `sort_order` | Display ordering | 100 |

### 💡 Practical Use
- **Consistency** — everyone uses `toilet_cost` not "toilet price" or "wc cost"
- **Onboarding** — new team members see what metrics exist and how they're calculated
- **You can add new definitions anytime** — e.g., `vanity_cost`, `tile_sf`, `grout_bags`

---

## 👁️ View 1: `v_unit_cost_sheet`

**What it is:** Your **main working spreadsheet**. Every room in every unit as a row, with measurements and the most common cost metrics pivoted into columns.

**144 rows** (one per unit-room across all 20 units)

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `unit_type`, `bedrooms` | Unit identity |
| `room_name` | Which room |
| `area_sf` through `door_count` | Physical measurements from the wide table |
| `paint_wall_gal` | Gallons of wall paint needed |
| `flooring_sf`, `flooring_cost` | Flooring material and cost |
| `baseboard_lf`, `baseboard_cost` | Baseboard linear feet and cost |
| `appliance_cost` | Per-item appliance total (not area-based) |
| `door_cost_total` | Per-item door total |
| `labor_cost` | Labor estimate |
| `sub_invoice_allocated` | This room's share of subcontractor invoices |
| `total_cost` | Everything summed |

### 💡 How to Use in NocoDB
- **Filter** by `floor = 3` to see just third-floor rooms
- **Sort** by `flooring_cost` descending to find the most expensive flooring jobs
- **Group** by `unit_number` to see per-unit breakdowns
- **Hide columns** you don't need for a specific meeting or report
- **NULL values** mean no data yet — rooms without calculations show blank, not zero

---

## 👁️ View 2: `v_project_cost_summary`

**What it is:** **One row per unit** — everything rolled up. Your executive dashboard.

**20 rows** (one per unit)

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `bedrooms` | Unit identity |
| `room_count` | How many rooms in this unit |
| `total_room_area_sf` | Combined floor area of all rooms |
| `total_wall_area_sf` | Combined wall surface |
| `total_flooring_cost` | Sum of all flooring costs |
| `total_paint_gal` | Total paint gallons (wall + ceiling) |
| `total_appliance_cost` | Sum of all appliance costs (per-item, not per-SF) |
| `total_invoice_allocated` | Sum of all subcontractor allocations |
| `total_cost` | Grand total for the unit |
| `cost_per_sf` | Total cost ÷ unit area — **only meaningful for area-based costs** |

### 💡 How to Use
- **"Which unit is most expensive?"** → Sort by `total_cost`
- **"Compare cost per SF across unit types"** → Group by `unit_type`, compare `cost_per_sf`
- **"How much paint for the whole project?"** → Sum the `total_paint_gal` column
- **"Budget report for the owner"** → Export this view as CSV

---

## 👁️ View 3: `v_trade_cost_comparison`

**What it is:** Every calculation row with its trade, unit, and room context. **Filter by trade** to see that trade's costs across the entire project.

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `trade_key` | Which trade (painting, plumbing, etc.) |
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `room_name` | Location |
| `metric_key`, `metric_label` | What's being measured |
| `value_numeric`, `unit_of_measure` | The value |
| `category` | cost, supply, invoice, labor |
| `cost_per_sf` | Value ÷ room area — **only shown for $ metrics, NULL for per-item costs** |

### 💡 How to Use
- **"Show me all painting costs"** → Filter `trade_key = 'painting'`
- **"Compare plumbing cost per SF across units"** → Filter trade, sort by `cost_per_sf`
- **"Which rooms have the highest cost per SF for flooring?"** → Filter + sort
- **Note:** `cost_per_sf` will be NULL for per-item costs like toilets — this is correct behavior

---

## 👁️ View 4: `v_all_calculations`

**What it is:** A flat, unprocessed list of **every single calculation record**. The raw EAV data with unit/room names joined in.

| Column | What You See |
|---|---|
| `unit_number`, `floor`, `room_name` | Location |
| `metric_key`, `metric_label` | What metric |
| `category`, `trade_key` | Classification |
| `value_numeric`, `value_text` | The values |
| `unit_of_measure` | Units |
| `is_calculated`, `is_editable` | Flags |
| `source` | Where it came from |
| `formula` | How it was computed |

### 💡 How to Use
- **"Show me everything that was manually entered"** → Filter `source = 'user_input'`
- **"What formulas are being used?"** → Filter `is_calculated = true`, read `formula` column
- **"Find all invoice allocations"** → Filter `category = 'invoice'`
- **"What text notes exist?"** → Filter `value_text IS NOT NULL`

{

        "The psql session seems stuck. Let me reset and use a fresh Python session to query the database properly."

    "headline": "Querying database schema with fresh session",
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "python",
        "session": 1,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "import subprocess, os

env = {**os.environ, 'PGPASSWORD': 'postgres', 'PAGER': ''}
conn = ['-h', 'host.docker.internal', '-p', '54322', '-U', 'postgres', '-d', 'postgres']

def run_sql(sql):
    r = subprocess.run(['psql'] + conn + ['-t', '-A', '-F|', '-c', sql],
                       capture_output=True, text=True, env=env, timeout=15)
    return r.stdout.strip()

# 1. Get all public tables (excluding NocoDB and notification)
print("=== TABLES ===")
tables_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT t.table_name, 
         string_agg(c.column_name || '::' || c.udt_name || '::' || c.is_nullable, ';' ORDER BY c.ordinal_position)
  FROM information_schema.tables t
  JOIN information_schema.columns c ON c.table_schema = t.table_schema AND c.table_name = t.table_name
  WHERE t.table_schema = 'public' AND t.table_type = 'BASE TABLE'
    AND t.table_name NOT LIKE 'nc_%' AND t.table_name NOT LIKE 'xc_%' AND t.table_name != 'notification'
  GROUP BY t.table_name ORDER BY t.table_name;
""")
for line in tables_raw.split('\n'):
    if '|' in line:
        name, cols = line.split('|', 1)
        col_list = [c.split('::') for c in cols.split(';')]
        print(f"\n## {name} ({len(col_list)} cols)")
        for c in col_list:
            print(f"  {c[0]:30s} {c[1]:15s} {'NULL' if c[2]=='YES' else 'NOT NULL'}")

# 2. Get views
print("\n\n=== VIEWS ===")
views_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_schema = 'public' ORDER BY table_name;
""")
for v in views_raw.split('\n'):
    if v.strip():
        print(f"  {v.strip()}")

# 3. Get foreign keys
print("\n\n=== FOREIGN KEYS ===")
fk_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT tc.table_name, kcu.column_name, ccu.table_name, ccu.column_name
  FROM information_schema.table_constraints tc
  JOIN information_schema.key_column_usage kcu ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name AND tc.table_schema = kcu.table_schema
  JOIN information_schema.constraint_column_usage ccu ON tc.constraint_name = ccu.constraint_name AND tc.table_schema = ccu.table_schema
  WHERE tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY' AND tc.table_schema = 'public'
    AND tc.table_name NOT LIKE 'nc_%' AND tc.table_name NOT LIKE 'xc_%'
  ORDER BY tc.table_name;
""")
for line in fk_raw.split('\n'):
    if '|' in line:
        parts = line.split('|')
        print(f"  {parts[0]}.{parts[1]} → {parts[2]}.{parts[3]}")

# 4. Get RLS status
print("\n\n=== RLS STATUS ===")
rls_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT relname, relrowsecurity FROM pg_class 
  WHERE relnamespace = 'public'::regnamespace AND relkind = 'r' 
    AND relname NOT LIKE 'nc_%' AND relname NOT LIKE 'xc_%'
  ORDER BY relname;
""")
for line in rls_raw.split('\n'):
    if '|' in line:
        name, rls = line.split('|')
        if rls.strip() == 't':
            print(f"  {name}: RLS ENABLED")
"
    }
}

import subprocess, os

env = {**os.environ, 'PGPASSWORD': 'postgres', 'PAGER': ''}
conn = ['-h', 'host.docker.internal', '-p', '54322', '-U', 'postgres', '-d', 'postgres']

def run_sql(sql):
    r = subprocess.run(['psql'] + conn + ['-t', '-A', '-F|', '-c', sql],
                       capture_output=True, text=True, env=env, timeout=15)
    return r.stdout.strip()

# 1. Get all public tables (excluding NocoDB and notification)
print("=== TABLES ===")
tables_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT t.table_name, 
         string_agg(c.column_name || '::' || c.udt_name || '::' || c.is_nullable, ';' ORDER BY c.ordinal_position)
  FROM information_schema.tables t
  JOIN information_schema.columns c ON c.table_schema = t.table_schema AND c.table_name = t.table_name
  WHERE t.table_schema = 'public' AND t.table_type = 'BASE TABLE'
    AND t.table_name NOT LIKE 'nc_%' AND t.table_name NOT LIKE 'xc_%' AND t.table_name != 'notification'
  GROUP BY t.table_name ORDER BY t.table_name;
""")
for line in tables_raw.split('\n'):
    if '|' in line:
        name, cols = line.split('|', 1)
        col_list = [c.split('::') for c in cols.split(';')]
        print(f"\n## {name} ({len(col_list)} cols)")
        for c in col_list:
            print(f"  {c[0]:30s} {c[1]:15s} {'NULL' if c[2]=='YES' else 'NOT NULL'}")

# 2. Get views
print("\n\n=== VIEWS ===")
views_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_schema = 'public' ORDER BY table_name;
""")
for v in views_raw.split('\n'):
    if v.strip():
        print(f"  {v.strip()}")

# 3. Get foreign keys
print("\n\n=== FOREIGN KEYS ===")
fk_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT tc.table_name, kcu.column_name, ccu.table_name, ccu.column_name
  FROM information_schema.table_constraints tc
  JOIN information_schema.key_column_usage kcu ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name AND tc.table_schema = kcu.table_schema
  JOIN information_schema.constraint_column_usage ccu ON tc.constraint_name = ccu.constraint_name AND tc.table_schema = ccu.table_schema
  WHERE tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY' AND tc.table_schema = 'public'
    AND tc.table_name NOT LIKE 'nc_%' AND tc.table_name NOT LIKE 'xc_%'
  ORDER BY tc.table_name;
""")
for line in fk_raw.split('\n'):
    if '|' in line:
        parts = line.split('|')
        print(f"  {parts[0]}.{parts[1]} → {parts[2]}.{parts[3]}")

# 4. Get RLS status
print("\n\n=== RLS STATUS ===")
rls_raw = run_sql("""
  SELECT relname, relrowsecurity FROM pg_class 
  WHERE relnamespace = 'public'::regnamespace AND relkind = 'r' 
    AND relname NOT LIKE 'nc_%' AND relname NOT LIKE 'xc_%'
  ORDER BY relname;
""")
for line in rls_raw.split('\n'):
    if '|' in line:
        name, rls = line.split('|')
        if rls.strip() == 't':
            print(f"  {name}: RLS ENABLED")

    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "python",
        "session": 1,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "doc = r"""# VoiceLog Scaffold — Database Reference

> **Updated**: 2026-02-18 | **Schema**: `public` | **Engine**: Supabase (PostgreSQL 15)
> **Purpose**: Developer reference for understanding table roles, relationships, and data flow.
> Not intended for schema recreation — see `supabase/migrations/` for that.

---

## Quick Reference

| Domain | Tables | Purpose |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **Core / Tenancy** | 4 | Multi-tenant foundation, projects, rooms catalog, template libraries |
| **Work Orders** | 4 | Trade checklists — templates, instances, tasks, notes |
| **Units & Rooms** | 3 | Physical spaces — apartments, room instances, document links |
| **Measurements & Costs** | 3 | Room dimensions, cost calculations, metric definitions |
| **Documents & Media** | 3 | Uploaded files, extracted images, polymorphic image links |
| **Tenant Portal** | 1 | Per-org table access configuration for external portal |
| **AI / Agent** | 1 | Agent Zero long-term memory with vector embeddings |
| **Views** | 5 | Pre-built reporting: cost sheets, budgets, trade comparisons |

**Total**: 19 application tables · 5 views · 2 NocoDB residual tables

---

## Entity Relationship Overview

```
organizations (tenant root)
 ├── wo_projects (job sites / properties)
 │    ├── units (apartments / physical spaces)
 │    │    ├── unit_rooms → rooms
 │    │    │    ├── unit_room_measurements
 │    │    │    └── unit_room_calculations
 │    │    ├── unit_documents → project_documents
 │    │    └── work_orders → work_order_templates
 │    │         ├── work_order_tasks → rooms
 │    │         └── work_order_notes
 │    ├── project_documents
 │    │    └── document_images
 │    │         └── image_links (polymorphic)
 │    └── work_order_templates (project-scoped)
 ├── rooms (master room catalog)
 ├── template_libraries
 │    └── work_order_templates (library-scoped)
 ├── portal_table_config
 └── work_order_templates (company-scoped)

agent_memories (standalone — Agent Zero)
metric_definitions (standalone — reference catalog)
```

---

## 1. Core / Tenancy

### `organizations`
**The multi-tenant root.** Every business entity (contractor, property manager) is an organization. Nearly all other tables reference this directly or indirectly via foreign keys. RLS policies use `organization_id` to isolate tenant data.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid PK | Tenant identifier |
| `name` | text | Display name (e.g., "Acme Renovations") |
| `slug` | text | URL-safe identifier, unique |
| `settings` | jsonb | Tenant-specific configuration (feature flags, preferences) |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |
| `updated_at` |

<< 12883 Characters hidden >>

oice` · `labor` |
| `trade_key` | text | Associated trade |
| `unit_of_measure` | text | Default units |
| `default_formula` | text | Suggested calculation (e.g., `wall_area_sf / 350`) |
| `description` | text | Explanation for developers/users |
| `sort_order` | int4 | Display ordering |

---

## 5. Documents & Media

### `project_documents`
**Uploaded files — PDFs, architectural drawings, specs.** Stored in Supabase Storage with metadata tracked here. The Doc Parser service processes these to extract images and text.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid PK | — |
| `project_id` | uuid FK → wo_projects | Which project |
| `title` | text | Document display name |
| `description` | text | Notes about the document |
| `file_url` | text | Supabase Storage URL |
| `file_type` | text | MIME type or extension |
| `file_size_bytes` | int8 | File size |
| `uploaded_by` | uuid | User who uploaded |
| `uploaded_at` | timestamptz | Upload timestamp |
| `metadata` | jsonb | Parsing results, page count, etc. |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |

🔒 RLS Enabled

---

### `document_images`
**Extracted page images from parsed documents.** When the Doc Parser processes a PDF, each page/figure/floor plan becomes a row here with its image URL and classification.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid PK | — |
| `document_id` | uuid FK → project_documents | Source document |
| `image_url` | text | Supabase Storage URL for the extracted image |
| `page_number` | int4 | Page in the source PDF |
| `image_type` | text | `page` · `floor_plan` · `figure` · `table` |
| `caption` | text | AI-generated or manual caption |
| `metadata` | jsonb | Layout analysis results, bounding boxes |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |

🔒 RLS Enabled

---

### `image_links`
**Polymorphic junction table linking images to any entity.** An extracted floor plan image can be linked to a unit, a room, or any other entity type. The `entity_type` + `entity_id` pattern allows flexible associations without additional foreign keys.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid PK | — |
| `image_id` | uuid FK → document_images | The image |
| `entity_type` | text | Target type: `unit`, `room`, `project`, etc. |
| `entity_id` | uuid | Target row ID (not a FK — polymorphic) |
| `link_type` | text | Relationship qualifier (e.g., `primary_floor_plan`) |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |

🔒 RLS Enabled

---

## 6. Tenant Portal

### `portal_table_config`
**Per-organization table access configuration.** Controls which database tables are visible to tenants in the Tenant Portal app, and at what access level. Admins configure this to expose specific data views to external stakeholders.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid PK | — |
| `organization_id` | uuid FK → organizations | Which tenant this config applies to |
| `table_name` | text | PostgreSQL table name to expose |
| `display_name` | text | Friendly name shown in portal UI |
| `access_level` | text | `hidden` · `readonly` · `editable` |
| `visible_columns` | text[] | Whitelist of columns to show (NULL = all) |
| `editable_columns` | text[] | Which columns allow inline editing |
| `hidden_columns` | text[] | Columns to explicitly hide |
| `column_order` | text[] | Custom column display sequence |
| `sort_column` | text | Default sort column |
| `sort_direction` | text | `asc` · `desc` |
| `filter_conditions` | jsonb | Pre-applied filters for this table |
| `page_size` | int4 | Rows per page (default 25) |
| `display_order` | int4 | Table card position on dashboard |
| `icon` | text | UI icon for the table card |
| `description` | text | Help text shown to tenants |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |
| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |

🔒 RLS Enabled · **Unique**: (`organization_id`, `table_name`)

---

## 7. AI / Agent

### `agent_memories`
**Agent Zero long-term memory store.** Enables semantic search over past interactions, solutions, and learned knowledge using pgvector embeddings. Each memory has an area tag for scoping (e.g., `main`, project-specific areas).

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `id` | uuid PK | — |
| `content` | text | The memory text content |
| `metadata` | jsonb | Structured tags: timestamp, area, source, custom fields |
| `area` | text | Memory scope (e.g., `main`, project name) |
| `source` | text | Origin: `user`, `agent`, `tool_result` |
| `source_memory_id` | text | Links to related/parent memory |
| `embedding` | vector | pgvector embedding for semantic similarity search |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | — |
| `updated_at` | timestamptz | — |

**Requires**: `pgvector` extension

---

## 8. Views (Reporting)

These are read-only PostgreSQL views that flatten and aggregate data for reporting. They can be queried directly or exposed through NocoDB/Supabase Studio.

### `v_unit_cost_sheet`
**The main working spreadsheet.** One row per unit-room with measurements and the most common cost metrics pivoted into columns. This is the primary view for day-to-day cost tracking.

**Joins**: `units` → `unit_rooms` → `rooms` + `unit_room_measurements` + `unit_room_calculations` (pivoted)

**Key columns**: unit info, room name, all measurements, paint gallons, flooring cost, baseboard cost, appliance cost, labor cost, invoice allocations, total cost.

---

### `v_project_cost_summary`
**Executive dashboard — one row per unit.** Rolls up all room-level costs into unit totals. Shows room count, total areas, total costs by category, and cost-per-SF.

**Aggregates from**: `v_unit_cost_sheet` (or equivalent joins)

---

### `v_budget_tracker`
**Budget vs actual tracking.** Compares estimated costs against actual expenditures, showing variances and percent complete per unit or trade.

**Key columns**: estimated vs actual prices, hours, and costs with variance calculations.

---

### `v_trade_cost_comparison`
**Cross-project trade analysis.** Every calculation row with full context (unit, room, trade). Filter by `trade_key` to see a single trade's costs across the entire project. Includes `cost_per_sf` where applicable (NULL for per-item costs).

---

### `v_all_calculations`
**Raw audit trail.** Flat list of every `unit_room_calculations` record with unit/room names joined in. Unprocessed EAV data for debugging, auditing, and ad-hoc analysis. Shows source, formula, and editability flags.

---

## 9. NocoDB Residual Tables

These tables were created by NocoDB when it previously used the Supabase database for metadata storage. NocoDB has since been reconfigured to use internal SQLite. **These can be safely dropped** if no longer needed.

| Table | Rows | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `workspace` | ~1 | NocoDB workspace definition |
| `workspace_user` | ~1 | NocoDB workspace membership |

> **Note**: The ~86 `nc_*` and `xc_*` NocoDB metadata tables were cleaned up in a previous session. Only these two remain.

---

## 10. RLS (Row-Level Security) Status

RLS is enabled on all tables that contain tenant-scoped data. Policies filter by `organization_id` to ensure tenants can only access their own data.

| Table | RLS | Isolation Key |
|-------|-----|---------------|
| `organizations` | ✅ | Self (id) |
| `wo_projects` | — | Via `organization_id` |
| `rooms` | ✅ | `organization_id` |
| `units` | ✅ | `organization_id` |
| `unit_rooms` | ✅ | Via parent `units` |
| `unit_documents` | ✅ | Via parent `units` |
| `work_order_templates` | ✅ | `organization_id` |
| `work_orders` | ✅ | `organization_id` |
| `work_order_tasks` | ✅ | Via parent `work_orders` |
| `work_order_notes` | ✅ | Via parent `work_orders` |
| `project_documents` | ✅ | Via parent `wo_projects` |
| `document_images` | ✅ | Via parent `project_documents` |
| `image_links` | ✅ | Via parent `document_images` |
| `portal_table_config` | ✅ | `organization_id` |
| `unit_room_measurements` | — | Via parent `unit_rooms` |
| `unit_room_calculations` | — | Via parent `unit_rooms` |
| `metric_definitions` | — | Global reference (no tenant scope) |
| `agent_memories` | — | Scoped by `area` field, not RLS |
| `template_libraries` | — | `organization_id` (nullable for app-scope) |

---

## 11. Foreign Key Map

```
portal_table_config.organization_id  → organizations.id
rooms.organization_id                → organizations.id
template_libraries.organization_id   → organizations.id
wo_projects.organization_id          → organizations.id
wo_projects.library_id               → template_libraries.id
units.organization_id                → organizations.id
units.project_id                     → wo_projects.id
unit_rooms.unit_id                   → units.id
unit_rooms.room_id                   → rooms.id
unit_room_measurements.unit_room_id  → unit_rooms.id
unit_room_calculations.unit_room_id  → unit_rooms.id
unit_documents.unit_id               → units.id
unit_documents.document_id           → project_documents.id
project_documents.project_id         → wo_projects.id
document_images.document_id          → project_documents.id
image_links.image_id                 → document_images.id
work_order_templates.organization_id → organizations.id
work_order_templates.project_id      → wo_projects.id
work_order_templates.library_id      → template_libraries.id
work_order_templates.parent_template_id      → work_order_templates.id (self)
work_order_templates.copied_from_template_id → work_order_templates.id (self)
work_orders.organization_id          → organizations.id
work_orders.unit_id                  → units.id
work_orders.template_id              → work_order_templates.id
work_order_tasks.work_order_id       → work_orders.id
work_order_tasks.room_id             → rooms.id
work_order_notes.work_order_id       → work_orders.id
```

---

*This document reflects the live database as of 2026-02-18. Update when tables are added, modified, or removed.*
