To avoid conflicts during development, we strictly assign specific ports to each application in the monorepo.

## Assigned Ports

| Port      | Application         | Path                  | Notes                                                                 |
| :-------- | :------------------ | :-------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **3000**  | **API**             | `apps/api`            | The Backend (Hono) + A2A Protocol (`/a2a`, `/.well-known/agent.json`) |
| **3001**  | **Web Dashboard**   | `apps/web-dashboard`  | Admin/Office View (Legacy/Reference)                                  |
| **3002**  | **Material Admin**  | `apps/material-admin` | Material & Taxonomy Manager                                           |
| **3003**  | **Site Companion**  | `apps/site-companion` | Mobile Companion App (Voice Log)                                      |
| **3004**  | **Work Orders**     | `apps/work-orders`    | Work Orders Management                                                |
| **5000**  | **Doc Parser**      | `doc-parser`          | Python FastAPI Service for LayoutParser                               |
| **8082**  | **NocoDB**          | -                     | No-code Database Interface (Internal Tools)                           |
| **54321** | **Supabase**        | -                     | Local Database (Postgres)                                             |
| **54323** | **Supabase Studio** | -                     | Local Dashboard UI                                                    |
| **5080**  | **Agent Zero**      | `agent-zero`          | Agent Zero                                                            |

## Rules

1.  **Strict Ports**: In `vite.config.ts`, always set `server.port` to the assigned value.
2.  **No Random Ports**: Do not let Vite pick a random port. This breaks CORS configurations.
3.  **Check this doc**: Before creating a new app, claim the next available port here.

## Troubleshooting

### Windows "Zombie" Processes

If you encounter `EADDRINUSE` or random port assignments (e.g., 3009 instead of 3002), it is likely that a previous Node process is still running in the background.

**Fix**: Run this command in PowerShell to Force-Kill all Node processes:

```powershell
taskkill /F /IM node.exe
```

---

## Docker / Compose port mapping (local development)

The repository provides a `docker-compose.yml` (root of `agent-zero`) that maps the service ports for the single "agent-zero" service. By default the compose file maps:

- Container port 80 -> Host 80
- Container port 9000 -> Host 9000
- Container port 22 -> Host 22

If you run multiple services from the monorepo in parallel, ensure these host ports are free or modify the `docker-compose.yml` port mappings to avoid conflicts.

Tip: use `docker compose ps` and `docker compose logs -f <service>` to inspect running services and assigned ports.

(venv) root@2c00128c0233:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app# # Voicelog Scaffold — Comprehensive Platform Analysis

> **Generated**: 2026-02-18 | **Author**: Agent Zero (Developer)
> **Scope**: Full monorepo architecture, data model, services, current state, and strategic direction

---

## 1. What You're Building

A **construction renovation management platform** — a suite of interconnected applications that digitize the entire lifecycle of multi-unit renovation projects, from initial scope definition through work execution, inspection, and closeout.

The platform solves a specific industry pain point: construction teams juggle spreadsheets, paper checklists, voice memos, and disconnected tools. This system unifies everything into a single data model where:

- **Office staff** define project scopes, manage templates, upload architectural drawings, and track progress from dashboards
- **Field workers** execute work orders room-by-room using mobile-optimized checklists, log time via voice, and capture site conditions
- **Tenants** access their project data through a configurable portal with read-only or editable views
- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol
- **NocoDB** provides a spreadsheet-like interface for bulk data management and ad-hoc queries without custom UI development

The platform is **multi-tenant** (organizations), **multi-project** (wo_projects), and **multi-trade** (painting, plumbing, carpentry, etc.).

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

### 2.1 Philosophy

Three core principles drive every technical decision:

1. **Schema-First**: Zod schemas in `packages/schemas` are the single source of truth. If it's not in a schema, it doesn't exist. This guarantees type safety from database to UI.
2. **Monorepo with Shared DNA**: Multiple apps share the same data model, validation logic, and configuration. A change to a schema propagates instantly to all consumers.
3. **Hybrid Relational + JSONB**: Relational columns for indexing, foreign keys, and permissions. JSONB columns for deeply nested hierarchical data (template definitions, scope trees, overrides).

### 2.2 Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm | Fast parallel builds, workspace linking, dependency deduplication |
| **Frontend** | SolidJS + Vite | True reactivity via signals (no virtual DOM), sub-second HMR |
| **Backend** | Hono (OpenAPIHono) | Lightweight, web-standard, runs on Node/Edge/Deno. Built-in OpenAPI generation |
| **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) | Managed Postgres with Auth, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Row-Level Security |
| **Validation** | Zod | Runtime validation + static TypeScript inference from a single definition |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first, consistent design system across apps |
| **Voice/AI** | Anthropic Claude + OpenAI Whisper | Voice transcription → LLM structured extraction |
| **Document Parsing** | FastAPI + Detectron2 + LayoutParser | GPU-accelerated PDF parsing, floor plan detection, image extraction |
| **Spreadsheet UI** | NocoDB | No-code database interface for bulk editing relational data |
| **Agent Protocol** | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) | JSON-RPC interoperability between AI agents |

### 2.3 Service Topology

```

│                        USER DEVICES                             │
├──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────────────────┤
│ Work     │ Material │ Site     │ Web      │ Tenant   │ NocoDB   │
│ Orders   │ Admin    │ Companion│ Dashboard│ Portal   │ Spread   │
│ :3004    │ :3002    │ :3003    │ :3001    │ :3005    │ :8082    │
│ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ Docker   │
│ Tailwind │ Tailwind │ PWA      │ Legacy   │ Tailwind │          │
└────┬─────┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴──────────┬──────────┘

logs.heading: 5 memories and 1 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo. It follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Architecture & Structure:
- Three-tier template hierarchy: App, Company, Project (manages defaults and overrides).
- Apps: web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app (:3003), work-orders (:3004), tenant-portal (:3005). API on :3000.
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Services & AI Pipeline:
- doc-parser: FastAPI (:5000) using Detectron2 for PDF/floor plan analysis.
- Voice-to-Timesheet: Designed AI-driven pipeline; currently noted as not yet implemented.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend: Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL), NocoDB, Zod.

Key Docs: docs/general/platform_analysis.md, docs/general/dbTables.md.

Local Supabase development environment configuration for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Connection Details:
- API URL: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL Port: 54322 (Host: host.docker.internal, User: postgres, Password: postgres, DB: postgres)
- Studio Port: 54323

Authentication Keys:
- Anon Key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJleHAiOjE5ODM4MTI5OTZ9.CRXP1A7WOeoJeXxjNni43kdQwgnWNReilDMblYTn_I0
- Service Role Key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImV4cCI6MTk4MzgxMjk5Nn0.EGIM96RAZx35lJzdJsyH-qQwv8Hdp7fsn3W0YpN81IU

Features & Integration:
- Includes pgvector support for agent memories.
- Serves as the external data source for NocoDB (connected via host.docker.internal:54322).

The database restoration plan for 'voicelog-scaffold' is implemented via 12 migration files in 'supabase/migrations/' and a 2.7MB seed file ('supabase/seed.sql'). The migrations cover core tables, functions, views (e.g., 'v_budget_tracker'), and pgvector support. Row Level Security (RLS) is enforced across the database for tenant isolation, utilizing a `current_user_organization_id()` function to filter data by the 'organization_id' column. The 'seed.sql' file contains 2,095 INSERT statements ordered by foreign key dependencies and uses 'SET session_replication_role = replica' to bypass constraints during restoration.

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation (e.g., `platform_analysis.md`) stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file covering Service Topology, Port Allocation, Applications, Database Tables, Status, and File Structure.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning and generation dates.
    - Modify ASCII Service Topology diagrams to include the new service.
    - Insert new rows into Port Allocation and Service Status tables.
    - Add detailed application subsections (e.g., `### 5.6 Tenant Portal`) detailing framework (SolidJS), features (Auth, Dynamic Table Viewer, Inline Editing), and pages.
    - Append new database tables (e.g., `portal_table_config`) to the data model section.
    - Add 'Data Flow Pattern' sections illustrating the access flow (Admin config -> Login -> RLS filtering -> CRUD).
4. **Verification**: Use `grep` or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly and all references are placed.

# Voicelog Scaffold — Comprehensive Platform Analysis

> **Generated**: 2026-02-18 | **Author**: Agent Zero (Developer)
> **Scope**: Full monorepo architecture, data model, services, current state, and strategic direction

---

## 1. What You're Building

A **construction renovation management platform** — a suite of interconnected applications that digitize the entire lifecycle of multi-unit renovation projects, from initial scope definition through work execution, inspection, and closeout.

The platform solves a specific industry pain point: construction teams juggle spreadsheets, paper checklists, voice memos, and disconnected tools. This system unifies everything into a single data model where:

- **Office staff** define project scopes, manage templates, upload architectural drawings, and track progress from dashboards
- **Field workers** execute work orders room-by-room using mobile-optimized checklists, log time via voice, and capture site conditions
- **Tenants** access their project data through a configurable portal with read-only or editable views
- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol
- **NocoDB** provides a spreadsheet-like interface for bulk data management and ad-hoc queries without custom UI development

The platform is **multi-tenant** (organizations), **multi-project** (wo_projects), and **multi-trade** (painting, plumbing, carpentry, etc.).

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

### 2.1 Philosophy

Three core principles drive every technical decision:

1. **Schema-First**: Zod schemas in `packages/schemas` are the single source of truth. If it's not in a schema, it doesn't exist. This guarantees type safety from database to UI.
2. **Monorepo with Shared DNA**: Multiple apps share the same data model, validation logic, and configuration. A change to a schema propagates instantly to all consumers.
3. **Hybrid Relational + JSONB**: Relational columns for indexing, foreign keys, and permissions. JSONB columns for deeply nested hierarchical data (template definitions, scope trees, overrides).

### 2.2 Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm | Fast parallel builds, workspace linking, dependency deduplication |
| **Frontend** | SolidJS + Vite | True reactivity via signals (no virtual DOM), sub-second HMR |
| **Backend** | Hono (OpenAPIHono) | Lightweight, web-standard, runs on Node/Edge/Deno. Built-in OpenAPI generation |
| **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) | Managed Postgres with Auth, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Row-Level Security |
| **Validation** | Zod | Runtime validation + static TypeScript inference from a single definition |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first, consistent design system across apps |
| **Voice/AI** | Anthropic Claude + OpenAI Whisper | Voice transcription → LLM structured extraction |
| **Document Parsing** | FastAPI + Detectron2 + LayoutParser | GPU-accelerated PDF parsing, floor plan detection, image extraction |
| **Spreadsheet UI** | NocoDB | No-code database interface for bulk editing relational data |
| **Agent Protocol** | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) | JSON-RPC interoperability between AI agents |

### 2.3 Service Topology

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        USER DEVICES                             │
├──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────────────────┤
│ Work     │ Material │ Site     │ Web      │ Tenant   │ NocoDB   │
│ Orders   │ Admin    │ Companion│ Dashboard│ Portal   │ Spread   │
│ :3004    │ :3002    │ :3003    │ :3001    │ :3005    │ :8082    │
│ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ SolidJS  │ Docker   │
│ Tailwind │ Tailwind │ PWA      │ Legacy   │ Tailwind │          │
└────┬─────┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴────┬─────┴──────────┬──────────┘
     │          │          │          │                │
     └──────────┴──────────┴──────────┘                │
                      │                                │
              ┌───────▼────────┐                       │
              │   Hono API     │                       │
              │   :3000        │                       │
              │                │                       │
              │ /work-orders   │                       │
              │ /materials     │      

<< 11436 Characters hidden >>

ages:**
- `LoginPage` — Email/password + magic link toggle
- `DashboardPage` — Card grid of available tables with access level badges
- `TableViewPage` — Dynamic data grid with sort, pagination, inline editing
- `AdminConfigPage` — Per-organization table access configuration (uses service role key)

**Data Flow:**
```
Tenant → Login (Supabase Auth) → Dashboard
  → portal_table_config (RLS filtered) → shows available tables
  → Click table → supabase.from(tableName).select() (RLS filtered)
  → DataGrid renders with readonly/editable mode per config
  → Editable cells → supabase.from(tableName).update() → RLS enforced
```

---

## 6. Supporting Services

### 6.1 Doc Parser (`doc-parser/`)

**Framework**: FastAPI (Python)
**Port**: 5000
**Infrastructure**: Docker with GPU support (Detectron2, LayoutParser)

**Capabilities:**
- PDF upload and page rendering
- Layout analysis (text blocks, tables, figures)
- Floor plan detection and extraction
- Image upload to Supabase Storage
- Text extraction via PyMuPDF

**Integration**: Called via HTTP from the Hono API or directly from Agent Zero. Uploads extracted images to Supabase Storage and returns structured metadata.

### 6.2 NocoDB (`nocodb/`)

**Port**: 8082
**Purpose**: Spreadsheet interface directly connected to the Supabase PostgreSQL database

**Use Cases:**
- Bulk editing relational data (units, rooms, taxonomy)
- Viewing flattened SQL views of JSONB data (`view_project_bom`)
- Ad-hoc queries without building custom UI
- Deep linking to the Material Admin app for complex JSONB editing

### 6.3 A2A Protocol

The API exposes an Agent-to-Agent JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a` with an agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`. This enables:
- External AI agents (like Agent Zero) to discover and interact with the platform
- Structured task delegation (e.g., "create a work order for unit 101")
- Multi-agent workflows across different AI systems

---

## 7. Current State Assessment

### 7.1 What's Built and Working

| Component | Status | Maturity |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Monorepo infrastructure | ✅ Complete | Production-ready |
| Supabase schema + migrations | ✅ Complete | 6 migrations, well-structured |
| Shared schemas (Zod) | ✅ Complete | Comprehensive type coverage |
| API routes (Hono) | ✅ Complete | 10 route modules, OpenAPI docs |
| Work Orders app | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD, template hierarchy, documents |
| Material Admin app | ✅ Complete | Suppliers, products, taxonomy, JSON editor |
| Site Companion app | ⚠️ Scaffold | Basic unit list, voice test page |
| Web Dashboard | ⚠️ Legacy | Login + todos only |
| Doc Parser | ✅ Complete | GPU pipeline, Supabase integration |
| NocoDB integration | ✅ Complete | Connected to Supabase DB |
| Tenant Portal | ✅ Complete | Auth, dynamic tables, inline editing, admin config |
| A2A Protocol | ✅ Complete | Agent card + JSON-RPC endpoint |
| Template hierarchy (3-tier) | ✅ Complete | App/Company/Project scoping |
| Document storage | ✅ Complete | Upload, extraction, linking |
| Voice processing | ⚠️ Partial | Transcription works, structured extraction in progress |
| Timesheet system | 📋 Designed | Schema designed, not yet implemented |
| Auth / RLS | ⚠️ Partial | Supabase Auth configured, RLS policies need hardening |
| Realtime subscriptions | ⚠️ Partial | Infrastructure ready, limited app integration |

### 7.2 What's Designed but Not Yet Implemented

**Timesheet System** (documented in `docs/timesheet/`):
- `timesheets` table — daily time containers per user
- `work_logs` table — links hours to specific BOM items (`site_item_id`)
- Milestone tracking (e.g., "Prime 30%", "Finish 100%")
- Earned value calculations
- Voice-to-timesheet pipeline (speak → transcribe → extract hours + items → create work_log)

**Materials/BOM System** (partially documented):
- `site_items` table — exploded BOM from JSONB definitions
- Product-to-scope matching via JSONB attributes
- Supplier order management
- Project supplies tracking

---

## 8. Data Flow Patterns

### 8.1 Work Order Lifecycle

```
1. Admin creates Template (definition JSONB with sections/columns)
         ↓
2. Admin creates Work Order for Unit + Trade
         ↓
3. System "snapshots" template → creates WorkOrderTasks (one per room)
         ↓
4. Field worker opens Site Companion → sees room checklist
         ↓
5. Worker completes steps (task_data JSONB updated)
         ↓
6. Inspector reviews → signs off → work order marked complete
```

### 8.2 Voice Processing Pipeline

```
1. Field worker records voice memo in Site Companion
         ↓
2. Audio sent to API /voice endpoint
         ↓
3. Whisper transcribes audio → text
         ↓
4. Anthropic Claude extracts structured data:
   - Hours worked
   - Items completed
   - Issues/notes
         ↓
5. Structured data creates/updates work_logs, work_order_tasks
```

### 8.3 Document Processing Pipeline

```
1. Admin uploads PDF (architectural drawings) via Work Orders app
         ↓
2. File stored in Supabase Storage, metadata in project_documents
         ↓
3. Doc Parser service called:
   - Renders pages to images
   - Detects layout (tables, figures, floor plans)
   - Extracts text
   - Uploads images to Supabase Storage
         ↓
4. Extracted images stored in document_images table
         ↓
5. Images linked to units/rooms via image_links table
```

### 8.4 NocoDB ↔ Custom App Integration

```
1. NocoDB reads relational data directly from PostgreSQL
         ↓
2. Complex JSONB data exposed via SQL views (view_project_bom)
         ↓
3. "Edit in App" deep links open Material Admin with URL params
         ↓
4. Material Admin's JsonTreeEditor modifies JSONB directly
         ↓
5. Changes visible in NocoDB immediately (same database)
```

### 8.5 Tenant Portal Access Flow

```
1. Admin configures portal_table_config for an organization
   (sets tables as hidden/readonly/editable, column visibility)
         ↓
2. Tenant logs in via Supabase Auth (email/password or magic link)
         ↓
3. Dashboard fetches portal_table_config (RLS filters by org)
         ↓
4. Tenant clicks a table → TableViewPage queries supabase.from(tableName)
   (RLS automatically filters to tenant's organization data)
         ↓
5. For editable tables: click cell → inline edit → supabase.update()
   (RLS prevents cross-tenant writes)
```

---

## 9. Strategic Direction & Recommendations

### 9.1 Immediate Next Steps

1. **Implement Timesheet Tables**: The schema is designed (`docs/timesheet/schema_design.md`). Create the migration for `timesheets`, `work_logs`, and `site_items` tables.

2. **Voice-to-Timesheet Pipeline**: Connect the existing voice transcription to the timesheet data model. This is the platform's killer feature — workers speak their hours and the system creates structured time entries.

3. **Site Companion Buildout**: The PWA scaffold exists but needs the full work order execution UI (room checklists, step completion, photo capture).

4. **RLS Policy Hardening**: Current policies are permissive for development. Production requires proper `organization_id` scoping and role-based access.

### 9.2 Architecture Strengths

- **Schema-first approach** prevents data model drift across 5+ applications
- **JSONB hybrid** elegantly handles the tension between structured queries and deeply nested domain data
- **Template hierarchy** is a sophisticated solution to the "every project is slightly different" problem
- **Monorepo with Turborepo** enables rapid iteration without dependency hell
- **A2A protocol** future-proofs for AI agent integration

### 9.3 Architecture Risks

- **JSONB complexity**: The flattened SQL views are read-only. Any write path for nested data requires the custom app, creating a dependency on the Material Admin for certain operations.
- **NocoDB coupling**: ~90 NocoDB tables in the same database adds metadata overhead and potential migration conflicts.
- **Config package hardcoding**: Supabase URLs and keys are hardcoded in `packages/config`. Should migrate to environment variables for deployment flexibility.
- **Single API server**: All routes in one Hono instance. As the platform grows, consider splitting into domain-specific services.

---

## 10. File Structure Reference

```
voicelog-scaffold/
├── apps/
│   ├── api/                  # Hono backend (The Brain)
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── routes/       # 10 route modules
│   │   │   ├── services/     # Business logic
│   │   │   ├── middleware/   # Auth, validation
│   │   │   └── index.ts      # App entry + route mounting
│   │   └── scripts/          # 50+ utility scripts
│   ├── work-orders/          # Work order management UI
│   ├── material-admin/       # Supplier/product/taxonomy UI
│   ├── site-companion/       # Mobile PWA for field workers
│   ├── web-dashboard/        # Legacy admin reference
│   └── tenant-portal/        # Configurable tenant data portal
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── pages/        # Login, Dashboard, TableView, AdminConfig
│       │   ├── components/   # Layout, DataGrid, EditableCell, Pagination
│       │   └── lib/          # Supabase clients, router, types
│       └── [config files]    # Vite, Tailwind, TypeScript
├── packages/
│   ├── schemas/              # Zod schemas (THE CONTRACT)
│   ├── db/                   # Auto-generated Supabase types
│   ├── config/               # Centralized configuration
│   ├── ui/                   # Shared SolidJS components
│   ├── logger/               # Structured logging
│   ├── utils/                # Shared utilities
│   └── tsconfig/             # Base TypeScript config
├── supabase/
│   ├── config.toml           # Supabase local config
│   └── migrations/           # 6 SQL migrations
├── doc-parser/               # FastAPI document parsing service
├── nocodb/                   # NocoDB Docker config
├── docs/                     # Comprehensive documentation
│   ├── general/              # This analysis
│   ├── workorders/           # Work order implementation docs
│   ├── timesheet/            # Timesheet design docs
│   └── a2a/                  # Agent protocol docs
├── turbo.json                # Turborepo build config
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml       # Workspace definition
└── package.json              # Root dependencies
```

---

kvps.memories:
The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo. It follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Architecture & Structure:
- Three-tier template hierarchy: App, Company, Project (manages defaults and overrides).
- Apps: web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app (:3003), work-orders (:3004), tenant-portal (:3005). API on :3000.
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Services & AI Pipeline:
- doc-parser: FastAPI (:5000) using Detectron2 for PDF/floor plan analysis.
- Voice-to-Timesheet: Designed AI-driven pipeline; currently noted as not yet implemented.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend: Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL), NocoDB, Zod.

Key Docs: docs/general/platform_analysis.md, docs/general/dbTables.md.

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation (e.g., `platform_analysis.md`) stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file covering Service Topology, Port Allocation, Applications, Database Tables, Status, and File Structure.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning and generation dates.
    - Modify ASCII Service Topology diagrams to include the new service.
    - Insert new rows into Port Allocation and Service Status tables.
    - Add detailed application subsections (e.g., `### 5.6 Tenant Portal`) detailing framework (SolidJS), features (Auth, Dynamic Table Viewer, Inline Editing), and pages.
    - Append new database tables (e.g., `portal_table_config`) to the data model section.
    - Add 'Data Flow Pattern' sections illustrating the access flow (Admin config -> Login -> RLS filtering -> CRUD).
4. **Verification**: Use `grep` or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly and all references are placed.

bash>cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold && cat docs/ports.md

# Port Allocation Contract

To avoid conflicts during development, we strictly assign specific ports to each application in the monorepo.

## Assigned Ports

| Port      | Application         | Path                  | Notes                                                                 |
| :-------- | :------------------ | :-------------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **3000**  | **API**             | `apps/api`            | The Backend (Hono) + A2A Protocol (`/a2a`, `/.well-known/agent.json`) |
| **3001**  | **Web Dashboard**   | `apps/web-dashboard`  | Admin/Office View (Legacy/Reference)                                  |
| **3002**  | **Material Admin**  | `apps/material-admin` | Material & Taxonomy Manager                                           |
| **3003**  | **Site Companion**  | `apps/site-companion` | Mobile Companion App (Voice Log)                                      |
| **3004**  | **Work Orders**     | `apps/work-orders`    | Work Orders Management                                                |
| **5000**  | **Doc Parser**      | `doc-parser`          | Python FastAPI Service for LayoutParser                               |
| **8082**  | **NocoDB**          | -                     | No-code Database Interface (Internal Tools)                           |
| **54321** | **Supabase**        | -                     | Local Database (Postgres)                                             |
| **54323** | **Supabase Studio** | -                     | Local Dashboard UI                                                    |
| **5080**  | **Agent Zero**      | `agent-zero`          | Agent Zero                                                            |

## Rules

1.  **Strict Ports**: In `vite.config.ts`, always set `server.port` to the assigned value.
2.  **No Random Ports**: Do not let Vite pick a random port. This breaks CORS configurations.
3.  **Check this doc**: Before creating a new app, claim the next available port here.

## Troubleshooting

### Windows "Zombie" Processes

If you encounter `EADDRINUSE` or random port assignments (e.g., 3009 instead of 3002), it is likely that a previous Node process is still running in the background.

**Fix**: Run this command in PowerShell to Force-Kill all Node processes:

```powershell
taskkill /F /IM node.exe
```

---

## Docker / Compose port mapping (local development)

The repository provides a `docker-compose.yml` (root of `agent-zero`) that maps the service ports for the single "agent-zero" service. By default the compose file maps:

- Container port 80 -> Host 80
- Container port 9000 -> Host 9000
- Container port 22 -> Host 22

If you run multiple services from the monorepo in parallel, ensure these host ports are free or modify the `docker-compose.yml` port mappings to avoid conflicts.

Tip: use `docker compose ps` and `docker compose logs -f <service>` to inspect running services and assigned ports.

595 docs/general/platform_analysis.md
# Voicelog Scaffold — Comprehensive Platform Analysis

> **Generated**: 2026-02-18 | **Author**: Agent Zero (Developer)
> **Scope**: Full monorepo architecture, data model, services, current state, and strategic direction

---

## 1. What You're Building

A **construction renovation management platform** — a suite of interconnected applications that digitize the entire lifecycle of multi-unit renovation projects, from initial scope definition through work execution, inspection, and closeout.

The platform solves a specific industry pain point: construction teams juggle spreadsheets, paper checklists, voice memos, and disconnected tools. This system unifies everything into a single data model where:

- **Office staff** define project scopes, manage templates, upload architectural drawings, and track progress from dashboards
- **Field workers** execute work orders room-by-room using mobile-optimized checklists, log time via voice, and capture site conditions
- **Tenants** access their project data through a configurable portal with read-only or editable views
- **AI agents** process voice recordings into structured data, parse architectural documents, and interoperate via the A2A protocol
- **NocoDB** provides a spreadsheet-like interface for bulk data management and ad-hoc queries without custom UI development

The platform is **multi-tenant** (organizations), **multi-project** (wo_projects), and **multi-trade** (painting, plumbing, carpentry, etc.).

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

### 2.1 Philosophy

Three core principles drive every technical decision:

1. **Schema-First**: Zod schemas in `packages/schemas` are the single source of truth. If it's not in a schema, it doesn't exist. This guarantees type safety from database to UI.
2. **Monorepo with Shared DNA**: Multiple apps share the same data model, validation logic, and configuration. A change to a schema propagates instantly to all consumers.
3. **Hybrid Relational + JSONB**: Relational columns for indexing, foreign keys, and permissions. JSONB columns for deeply nested hierarchical data (template definitions, scope trees, overrides).

### 2.2 Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Monorepo** | Turborepo + pnpm | Fast parallel builds, workspace linking, dependency deduplication |
| **Frontend** | SolidJS + Vite | True reactivity via signals (no virtual DOM), sub-second HMR |
| **Backend** | Hono (OpenAPIHono) | Lightweight, web-standard, runs on Node/Edge/Deno. Built-in OpenAPI generation |
| **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) | Managed Postgres with Auth, Realtime subscriptions, Storage, and Row-Level Security |
| **Validation** | Zod | Runtime validation + static TypeScript inference from a single definition |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first, consistent design system across apps |
| **Voice/AI** | Anthropic Claude + OpenAI Whisper | Voice transcription → LLM structured extraction |
| **Document Parsing** | FastAPI + Detectron2 + LayoutParser | GPU-accelerated PDF parsing, floor plan detection, image extraction |
| **Spreadsheet UI** | NocoDB | No-code database interface for bulk editing relational data |
| **Agent Protocol** | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) | JSON-RPC interoperability between AI agents |

### 2.3 Service Topology

The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo. It follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Architecture & Structure:
- Three-tier template hierarchy: App, Company, Project (manages defaults and overrides).
- Apps: web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app (:3003), work-orders (:3004), tenant-portal (:3005). API on :3000.
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Services & AI Pipeline:
- doc-parser: FastAPI (:5000) using Detectron2 for PDF/floor plan analysis.
- Voice-to-Timesheet: Designed AI-driven pipeline; currently noted as not yet implemented.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend: Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL), NocoDB, Zod.

Key Docs: docs/general/platform_analysis.md, docs/general/dbTables.md.

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation (e.g., `platform_analysis.md`) stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file covering Service Topology, Port Allocation, Applications, Database Tables, Status, and File Structure.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning and generation dates.
    - Modify ASCII Service Topology diagrams to include the new service.
    - Insert new rows into Port Allocation and Service Status tables.
    - Add detailed application subsections (e.g., `### 5.6 Tenant Portal`) detailing framework (SolidJS), features (Auth, Dynamic Table Viewer, Inline Editing), and pages.
    - Append new database tables (e.g., `portal_table_config`) to the data model section.
    - Add 'Data Flow Pattern' sections illustrating the access flow (Admin config -> Login -> RLS filtering -> CRUD).
4. **Verification**: Use `grep` or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly and all references are placed.

```

### 8.5 Tenant Portal Access Flow

```
1. Admin configures portal_table_config for an organization
   (sets tables as hidden/readonly/editable, column visibility)
         ↓
2. Tenant logs in via Supabase Auth (email/password or magic link)
         ↓
3. Dashboard fetches portal_table_config (RLS filters by org)
         ↓
4. Tenant clicks a table → TableViewPage queries supabase.from(tableName)
   (RLS automatically filters to tenant's organization data)
         ↓
5. For editable tables: click cell → inline edit → supabase.update()
   (RLS prevents cross-tenant writes)
```

---

## 9. Strategic Direction & Recommendations

### 9.1 Immediate Next Steps

1. **Implement Timesheet Tables**: The schema is designed (`docs/timesheet/schema_design.md`). Create the migration for `timesheets`, `work_logs`, and `site_items` tables.

2. **Voice-to-Timesheet Pipeline**: Connect the existing voice transcription to the timesheet data model. This is the platform's killer feature — workers speak their hours and the system creates structured time entries.

3. **Site Companion Buildout**: The PWA scaffold exists but needs the full work order execution UI (room checklists, step completion, photo capture).

4. **RLS Policy Hardening**: Current policies are permissive for development. Production requires proper `organization_id` scoping and role-based access.

api_readme = '''# API — The Brain

Central backend API powering the voicelog-scaffold construction renovation platform.

> `Port: 3000` | `Framework: Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js` | Part of [voicelog-scaffold](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md)

---

## Overview

The API is the central nervous system of the platform — every frontend app, AI agent, and external integration communicates through it. Built on **Hono** with full **OpenAPI** spec generation, it provides type-safe routes, Swagger UI documentation, and an A2A (Agent-to-Agent) JSON-RPC endpoint for AI interoperability.

## Role in the Platform

- **Single source of truth** for all business logic and data access
- **Gateway** between frontends and Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **AI orchestration hub** — processes voice recordings, parses PDFs, and hosts the A2A protocol
- **Placeholder recovery engine** — auto-generates smart defaults for missing entities and queues them for review

## Features

- 12 route modules covering work orders, materials, taxonomy, projects, units, voice, delivery, todos, A2A, documents, entity creation, and reviews
- OpenAPI spec auto-generation with Swagger UI at `/ui`
- A2A Protocol: JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a`, agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`
- PDF parsing via `pdfjs-dist` and Claude AI
- Placeholder recovery system with review queue
- 50+ utility scripts for seeding, migration, and testing

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | Hono (OpenAPIHono) |
| Runtime | Node.js with tsx (watch mode) |
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL) via `@supabase/supabase-js` |
| AI | `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (Claude) |
| PDF | `pdfjs-dist`, `@napi-rs/canvas` |
| Validation | Zod (via `@repo/schemas`) |

## Project Structure

```
apps/api/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                    # App entry + route mounting
│   ├── routes/
│   │   ├── work-orders.ts          # Work order CRUD + lifecycle
│   │   ├── materials.ts            # Supplier/product catalog
│   │   ├── taxonomy.ts             # Category management
│   │   ├── project-definitions.ts  # JSONB scope trees
│   │   ├── units.ts                # Unit management
│   │   ├── voice.ts                # Audio → transcription → extraction
│   │   ├── delivery.ts             # Delivery tracking
│   │   ├── todos.ts                # Simple CRUD (reference)
│   │   ├── a2a.ts                  # Agent-to-Agent JSON-RPC
│   │   ├── documents.ts            # Document upload/management
│   │   ├── entity-creation.ts      # Placeholder generation
│   │   └── reviews.ts              # Review queue management
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── a2a/
│   │   │   ├── agentCard.ts        # A2A agent card definition
│   │   │   ├── skillRouter.ts      # Skill dispatch
│   │   │   ├── taskStore.ts        # Task persistence
│   │   │   ├── types.ts            # A2A type definitions
│   │   │   └── skills/
│   │   │       ├── documents.ts    # Document skill
│   │   │       ├── progress.ts     # Progress skill
│   │   │       ├── units.ts        # Units skill
│   │   │       └── workOrders.ts   # Work orders skill
│   │   ├── claudePdfParser.ts      # Claude-powered PDF extraction
│   │   ├── pdfParser.ts            # PDF.js-based parsing
│   │   ├── placeholderGenerator.ts # Smart default entity creation
│   │   └── reviewService.ts        # Review queue business logic
│   └── middleware/
│       ├── auth.ts                 # Authentication middleware
│       └── supabase.ts             # Supabase client middleware
├── scripts/                        # 50+ utility scripts (see scripts/README.md)
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
```

## Getting Started

```bash
# From monorepo root
pnpm install

# Start the API in watch mode
cd apps/api
pnpm dev
```

The API starts on `http://localhost:3000`.

## Available Scripts

| Script | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `pnpm dev` | Start with `tsx watch src/index.ts` (hot reload) |
| `pnpm build` | TypeScript compilation |

See [`scripts/README.md`](scripts/README.md) for the full catalog of 50+ utility scripts.

## API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /ui` | Swagger UI — interactive API documentation |
| `GET /doc` | OpenAPI JSON spec |
| `POST /a2a` | A2A JSON-RPC endpoint |
| `GET /.well-known/agent.json` | A2A agent card |

## Related Documentation

- [Platform Analysis](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md) — Full architecture context
- [Port Allocation](../../docs/ports.md) — Service port assignments
- [A2A Protocol](../../docs/a2a/README.md) — Agent-to-Agent integration
- [Scripts README](scripts/README.md) — Utility script catalog
'''

material_admin_readme = '''# Material Admin

Supplier catalog, product management, taxonomy editing, and project definition interface.

> `Port: 3002` | `Framework: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS + Vite` | Part of [voicelog-scaffold](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md)

---

## Overview

Material Admin is the back-office management interface for maintaining the platform\'s supplier catalog, product inventory, taxonomy categories, project definitions, and unit overrides. It features a powerful **JsonTreeEditor** for editing complex nested JSONB template definitions directly.

## Role in the Platform

- **Catalog management** — CRUD for suppliers and products used across all projects
- **Taxonomy control** — Define and organize action types, room types, and item categories
- **Project definitions** — Edit JSONB scope trees that drive work order template generation
- **Unit overrides** — Configure per-unit customizations to standard templates
- **Todo tracking** — Simple task management for admin workflows

## Features

- Tab-based navigation: Materials, Taxonomy, Project Definitions, Unit Overrides, Todos
- Inline JSONB editing via the custom `JsonTreeEditor` component
- Supabase Auth integration with login/logout flow
- Real-time data operations against the API backend
- Responsive Tailwind CSS styling

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | SolidJS 1.8+ |
| Build | Vite 5 |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS |
| Auth | Supabase Auth |
| API Client | Hono RPC client |
| Shared | @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/ui, @repo/utils |

## Project Structure

```
apps/material-admin/
├── src/
│   ├── index.tsx                # App entry point
│   ├── index.css                # Global styles (Tailwind imports)
│   ├── App.tsx                  # Main app with tab navigation
│   ├── Login.tsx                # Supabase Auth login page
│   ├── client.ts                # Hono RPC client configuration
│   └── components/
│       ├── MaterialsView.tsx    # Supplier & product management
│       ├── ProductForm.tsx      # Product creation/editing form
│       ├── SupplierForm.tsx     # Supplier creation/editing form
│       ├── TaxonomyView.tsx     # Category tree management
│       ├── ProjectDefinitionsView.tsx  # JSONB scope tree editor
│       ├── UnitOverridesView.tsx       # Per-unit customizations
│       ├── TodoList.tsx         # Simple todo management
│       └── common/
│           └── JsonTreeEditor.tsx  # Reusable JSONB tree editor
├── public/
├── index.html
├── vite.config.ts
├── tailwind.config.js
├── postcss.config.js
├── tsconfig.json
└── package.json
```

## Getting Started

```bash
# From monorepo root
pnpm install

# Start Material Admin
cd apps/material-admin
pnpm dev
```

The app starts on `http://localhost:3002`.

## Available Scripts

| Script | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `pnpm dev` | Start Vite dev server on port 3002 |
| `pnpm build` | Production build |
| `pnpm preview` | Preview production build |

- [Platform Analysis](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md) — Full architecture context
- [Port Allocation](../../docs/ports.md) — Service port assignments
- [Template Hierarchy Design](../../docs/workorders/Template_Hierarchy_Design.md) — How templates and definitions work
'''

kvps.memories:
The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo. It follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Architecture & Structure:
- Three-tier template hierarchy: App, Company, Project (manages defaults and overrides).
- Apps: web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app (:3003), work-orders (:3004), tenant-portal (:3005). API on :3000.
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Services & AI Pipeline:
- doc-parser: FastAPI (:5000) using Detectron2 for PDF/floor plan analysis.
- Voice-to-Timesheet: Designed AI-driven pipeline; currently noted as not yet implemented.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend: Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL), NocoDB, Zod.

Key Docs: docs/general/platform_analysis.md, docs/general/dbTables.md.

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation (e.g., `platform_analysis.md`) stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file covering Service Topology, Port Allocation, Applications, Database Tables, Status, and File Structure.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning and generation dates.
    - Modify ASCII Service Topology diagrams to include the new service.
    - Insert new rows into Port Allocation and Service Status tables.
    - Add detailed application subsections (e.g., `### 5.6 Tenant Portal`) detailing framework (SolidJS), features (Auth, Dynamic Table Viewer, Inline Editing), and pages.
    - Append new database tables (e.g., `portal_table_config`) to the data model section.
    - Add 'Data Flow Pattern' sections illustrating the access flow (Admin config -> Login -> RLS filtering -> CRUD).
4. **Verification**: Use `grep` or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly and all references are placed.

tenant_portal_readme = '''# Tenant Portal

Configurable, embeddable data portal for external tenants with dynamic table access.

> `Port: 3005` | `Framework: SolidJS + Tailwind CSS + Vite` | Part of [voicelog-scaffold](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md)

---

## Overview

The Tenant Portal provides external tenants (clients, subcontractors, property managers) with controlled access to their project data. Access is **configuration-driven** — administrators define which tables each organization can see, and whether access is read-only or editable. The portal uses **hash-based routing** for seamless iframe embedding.

## Role in the Platform

- **External data access** — Tenants view their project data without needing internal tools
- **Config-driven permissions** — Per-organization table visibility and editability via `portal_table_config`
- **Embeddable** — Hash-based routing enables iframe integration into external sites
- **Tenant isolation** — Supabase RLS ensures organizations only see their own data

## Features

- **Authentication**: Email/password and magic link login via Supabase Auth
- **Dynamic dashboard**: Card grid showing all accessible tables for the logged-in organization
- **Data grid**: Sortable, paginated table viewer with inline cell editing for editable tables
- **Admin configuration**: Per-organization table access management (hidden / readonly / editable)
- **Column-level control**: Configure visible columns and editable columns per table
- **Hash-based routing**: `#/login`, `#/dashboard`, `#/table/:name`, `#/admin` — iframe-friendly
- **Dual Supabase clients**: Anon client for RLS-scoped queries, service-role client for admin operations

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | SolidJS 1.8+ |
| Build | Vite 5 |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS |
| Auth | Supabase Auth (email/password + magic link) |
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL) with RLS |
| Routing | @solidjs/router (hash mode) |
| Shared | @repo/config, @repo/db |

## Project Structure

```
apps/tenant-portal/
├── src/
│   ├── index.tsx              # App entry point
│   ├── index.css              # Global styles (Tailwind imports)
│   ├── App.tsx                # Root component with hash router
│   ├── pages/
│   │   ├── LoginPage.tsx      # Email/password + magic link auth
│   │   ├── DashboardPage.tsx  # Card grid of accessible tables
│   │   ├── TableViewPage.tsx  # Dynamic data grid with sort/pagination/editing
│   │   └── AdminConfigPage.tsx # Per-org table access configuration
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── DataGrid.tsx       # Sortable, paginated data table
│   │   ├── EditableCell.tsx   # Inline cell editing component
│   │   ├── Layout.tsx         # App shell with navigation
│   │   ├── LoadingSpinner.tsx # Loading state indicator
│   │   └── Pagination.tsx     # Page navigation controls
│   └── lib/
│       ├── supabase.ts        # Dual clients (anon + service role)
│       ├── router.ts          # Hash-based routing configuration
│       └── types.ts           # TypeScript type definitions
├── public/
├── index.html
├── vite.config.ts
├── tailwind.config.js
├── postcss.config.js
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.app.json
├── tsconfig.node.json
└── package.json
```

## Getting Started

```bash
# From monorepo root
pnpm install

# Start Tenant Portal
cd apps/tenant-portal
pnpm dev
```

The app starts on `http://localhost:3005`.

## Available Scripts

| Script | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `pnpm dev` | Start Vite dev server on port 3005 |
| `pnpm build` | Production build |
| `pnpm preview` | Preview production build |

## Access Levels

The `portal_table_config` table controls per-organization access:

| Level | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `hidden` | Table not visible to the organization |
| `readonly` | Table visible, data is read-only |
| `editable` | Table visible, inline editing enabled for configured columns |

## Related Documentation

- [Platform Analysis](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md) — Full architecture context
- [Port Allocation](../../docs/ports.md) — Service port assignments
- [Tenant Portal Migration](../../supabase/migrations/20260219000001_tenant_portal_config.sql) — Database schema
'''

with open(os.path.join(base, 'tenant-portal', 'README.md'), 'w') as f:
    f.write(tenant_portal_readme)
print('✅ apps/tenant-portal/README.md written')

# README 5: apps/web-dashboard/README.md
web_dashboard_readme = '''# Web Dashboard

Legacy reference admin dashboard — the original prototype implementation.

> `Port: 3001` | `Framework: SolidJS (minimal, no Tailwind)` | Part of [voicelog-scaffold](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md)

---

## Overview

The Web Dashboard is the **original prototype** of the platform\'s admin interface. It serves as a **reference implementation** demonstrating Supabase Auth integration and Hono RPC client patterns. While superseded by the more capable Material Admin and Work Orders apps, it remains useful as a minimal working example.

## Role in the Platform

- **Reference implementation** — Demonstrates Supabase Auth + Hono RPC integration patterns
- **Legacy prototype** — Original admin interface, now superseded by specialized apps
- **Minimal example** — Simplest working SolidJS app in the monorepo for onboarding

## Features

- Supabase Auth login/logout flow
- Basic todo list management (CRUD)
- Voice log list display
- Hono RPC client integration example

└── package.json

## Overview

The Work Orders app is the **primary management interface** for creating, configuring, and tracking work orders across renovation projects. It provides a full-featured UI for managing the work order lifecycle — from project creation and template configuration through room-by-room checklist execution.

## Role in the Platform

- **Work order management** — Full CRUD for work orders with template-driven generation
- **Template hierarchy** — View and edit the 3-tier template system (App → Company → Project)
- **Project management** — Create projects, manage job sites, upload architectural documents
- **Execution tracking** — Room-by-room checklist completion with status tracking

## Features

- **Dashboard**: Overview of all work orders with status summaries and quick actions
- **Units browser**: Navigate spaces/rooms within projects
- **Template management**: View and edit template definitions with section/column deep dives
- **Work order detail**: Room-by-room checklist execution with step completion tracking
- **Project management**: Create projects, view details, manage job site information
- **Document management**: Upload and manage architectural drawings and project documents
- **Project creation wizard**: Step-by-step new project setup
- **Hono RPC client**: Type-safe API communication

- **Tenant isolation** — Supabase RLS ensures organizations only see their own data

- **Dynamic dashboard**: Card grid showing all accessible tables for the logged-in organization

- **Admin configuration**: Per-organization table access management

logs.heading: 3 memories and 1 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo. It follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Architecture & Structure:
- Three-tier template hierarchy: App, Company, Project (manages defaults and overrides).
- Apps: web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app (:3003), work-orders (:3004), tenant-portal (:3005). API on :3000.
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Services & AI Pipeline:
- doc-parser: FastAPI (:5000) using Detectron2 for PDF/floor plan analysis.
- Voice-to-Timesheet: Designed AI-driven pipeline; currently noted as not yet implemented.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend: Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL), NocoDB, Zod.

Key Docs: docs/general/platform_analysis.md, docs/general/dbTables.md.

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation (e.g., `platform_analysis.md`) stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file covering Service Topology, Port Allocation, Applications, Database Tables, Status, and File Structure.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning and generation dates.
    - Modify ASCII Service Topology diagrams to include the new service.
    - Insert new rows into Port Allocation and Service Status tables.
    - Add detailed application subsections (e.g., `### 5.6 Tenant Portal`) detailing framework (SolidJS), features (Auth, Dynamic Table Viewer, Inline Editing), and pages.
    - Append new database tables (e.g., `portal_table_config`) to the data model section.
    - Add 'Data Flow Pattern' sections illustrating the access flow (Admin config -> Login -> RLS filtering -> CRUD).
4. **Verification**: Use `grep` or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly and all references are placed.

kvps.memories:
The voicelog-scaffold monorepo is a construction renovation management platform with a multi-tenant, multi-project, and multi-trade architecture.

The platform's tech stack includes SolidJS + Tailwind CSS + Vite for frontends, Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js for the API, and Supabase (PostgreSQL) for the database.

The API (Port 3000) serves as 'The Brain', featuring 12 route modules including work-orders, materials, taxonomy, projects, units, voice, delivery, todos, a2a, documents, entity-creation, and reviews.

The Material Admin app (Port 3002) manages supplier catalogs, product inventory, and project definitions using a custom JsonTreeEditor for JSONB templates.

The Site Companion app (Port 3003) is a mobile-optimized PWA for field workers, featuring unit navigation and voice-to-structured-data capture.

The Tenant Portal (Port 3005) is a configurable data portal for external clients with access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) managed via the portal_table_config table.

The Work Orders app (Port 3004) is the primary management interface for the work order lifecycle, including template hierarchy and project document management.

The platform includes a Placeholder Recovery system that auto-generates smart defaults for missing entities (templates/rooms) and manages them via a review queue (/reviews API).

The A2A (Agent-to-Agent) Protocol uses a JSON-RPC endpoint at /a2a and an agent card at /.well-known/agent.json for AI interoperability.

kvps.solutions:
{'problem': 'Update a large documentation file (e.g., 600+ lines) with multiple targeted changes without rewriting the entire file or losing existing content.', 'solution': "1. Read the file content into a variable using Python.
2. Identify unique 'anchor' strings or blocks of text surrounding the target insertion/replacement points.
3. Use Python's `.replace(old_string, new_string)` method to perform targeted updates. For example, to insert a new row in a table, replace the last existing row with itself plus the new row: `content.replace(last_row, last_row + '\n' + new_row)`.
4. Use conditional checks (e.g., `if new_string not in content:`) to ensure idempotency and prevent duplicate insertions.
5. Write the modified string back to the file."}

{'problem': 'Create standardized README.md files for multiple applications in a monorepo with specific metadata and structure requirements.', 'solution': "1. Scan each application's directory structure using `find` or `ls -R` to identify actual source files and components.
2. Define a template for the README including: H1 Title, Badge line (Port, Framework, Platform Link), Overview, Role in Platform, Features, Tech Stack table, Project Structure (tree format), Getting Started, and Related Documentation.
3. Populate the template with app-specific details: 
   - **API**: List routes, services (e.g., Hono, OpenAPI), and utility scripts.
   - **Frontend**: List pages, components, and shared packages (e.g., SolidJS, Tailwind).
   - **Status**: Explicitly mark apps as 'Scaffold', 'Legacy', or 'Production-ready'.
4. Use a Python script to write the generated strings to `apps/[app-name]/README.md` to ensure consistent formatting across the monorepo."}

kvps.memories:
The 'voicelog-scaffold' project (/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold) is a construction renovation management platform organized as a pnpm/Turborepo monorepo. It follows a 'schema-first' philosophy with a hybrid relational-JSONB data model.

Architecture & Structure:
- Three-tier template hierarchy: App, Company, Project (manages defaults and overrides).
- Apps: web-dashboard (:3001), material-admin (:3002), mobile-app (:3003), work-orders (:3004), tenant-portal (:3005). API on :3000.
- Shared Packages: @repo/config, @repo/db, @repo/ui, @repo/logger, @repo/schemas, @repo/utils.

Services & AI Pipeline:
- doc-parser: FastAPI (:5000) using Detectron2 for PDF/floor plan analysis.
- Voice-to-Timesheet: Designed AI-driven pipeline; currently noted as not yet implemented.

Tech Stack:
- Frontend: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS.
- Backend: Hono, Supabase (PostgreSQL), NocoDB, Zod.

Key Docs: docs/general/platform_analysis.md, docs/general/dbTables.md.

The local development environment for 'voicelog-scaffold' uses Docker with bind mounts. The project's local Windows path is 'C:\Users\Casey\voicelog-scaffold', which is bind-mounted to '/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold' inside the AI's container. Environment variables are used for path management.

Project: Tenant Portal for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Location: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/tenant-portal/` (Port 3005).

Tech Stack: SolidJS (1.8+), Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase (^2.90.1).

Core Requirements & Features:
- Architecture: Custom-built web interface integrated into the monorepo, designed to be embeddable via iframe.
- Routing: Hash-based using @solidjs/router (#/login, #/dashboard, #/table/:tableName, #/admin).
- Authentication: Supabase Auth supporting Email/Password and Magic Links.
- Data Management: Configuration-driven UI featuring dynamic table viewing and inline editing. Table visibility and editability are controlled per organization via the 'portal_table_config' table.
- Configuration Details: The 'portal_table_config' table manages per-organization/tenant access levels (hidden, readonly, editable) and column-level visibility (visible_columns, editable_columns, display preferences).
- Security: Strict tenant isolation using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies. The 'portal_table_config' table uses RLS and a `current_user_organization_id()` function to ensure organizations only access their own configuration.
- Administration: Admin section for organization-level configuration and managing table permissions.

Local Supabase development environment configuration for 'voicelog-scaffold'.

Connection Details:
- API URL: http://127.0.0.1:54321
- PostgreSQL Port: 54322 (Host: host.docker.internal, User: postgres, Password: postgres, DB: postgres)
- Studio Port: 54323

Features & Integration:
- Includes pgvector support for agent memories.
- Serves as the external data source for NocoDB (connected via host.docker.internal:54322).

The database restoration plan for 'voicelog-scaffold' is implemented via 12 migration files in 'supabase/migrations/' and a 2.7MB seed file ('supabase/seed.sql'). The migrations cover core tables, functions, views (e.g., 'v_budget_tracker'), and pgvector support. Row Level Security (RLS) is enforced across the database for tenant isolation, utilizing a `current_user_organization_id()` function to filter data by the 'organization_id' column. The 'seed.sql' file contains 2,095 INSERT statements ordered by foreign key dependencies and uses 'SET session_replication_role = replica' to bypass constraints during restoration.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
The user needs to maintain and update a comprehensive technical analysis of a complex Turborepo monorepo (SolidJS, Hono, Supabase), ensuring documentation (e.g., `platform_analysis.md`) stays synchronized with new architectural additions like the 'Tenant Portal'.

# Solution
1. **Initial Analysis**: Conduct a systematic review of the monorepo structure (`apps/`, `packages/`, `supabase/`), Zod schemas, and database migrations to understand the 'Schema-First' approach.
2. **Documentation Strategy**: Maintain a structured Markdown file covering Service Topology, Port Allocation, Applications, Database Tables, Status, and File Structure.
3. **Automated Updates**: When adding new services (like the Tenant Portal on port 3005), use Python scripts for targeted string replacements to maintain document integrity:
    - Update versioning and generation dates.
    - Modify ASCII Service Topology diagrams to include the new service.
    - Insert new rows into Port Allocation and Service Status tables.
    - Add detailed application subsections (e.g., `### 5.6 Tenant Portal`) detailing framework (SolidJS), features (Auth, Dynamic Table Viewer, Inline Editing), and pages.
    - Append new database tables (e.g., `portal_table_config`) to the data model section.
    - Add 'Data Flow Pattern' sections illustrating the access flow (Admin config -> Login -> RLS filtering -> CRUD).
4. **Verification**: Use `grep` or file reads to verify that all multi-section changes (15+) are applied correctly and all references are placed.

bash>head -50 /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/api/README.md

# API — The Brain

Central backend API powering the voicelog-scaffold construction renovation platform.

> `Port: 3000` | `Framework: Hono (OpenAPIHono) on Node.js` | Part of [voicelog-scaffold](../../docs/general/platform_analysis.md)

---

## Overview

The API is the central nervous system of the platform — every frontend app, AI agent, and external integration communicates through it. Built on **Hono** with full **OpenAPI** spec generation, it provides type-safe routes, Swagger UI documentation, and an A2A (Agent-to-Agent) JSON-RPC endpoint for AI interoperability.

## Role in the Platform

- **Single source of truth** for all business logic and data access
- **Gateway** between frontends and Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **AI orchestration hub** — processes voice recordings, parses PDFs, and hosts the A2A protocol
- **Placeholder recovery engine** — auto-generates smart defaults for missing entities and queues them for review

## Features

- 12 route modules covering work orders, materials, taxonomy, projects, units, voice, delivery, todos, A2A, documents, entity creation, and reviews
- OpenAPI spec auto-generation with Swagger UI at `/ui`
- A2A Protocol: JSON-RPC endpoint at `/a2a`, agent card at `/.well-known/agent.json`
- PDF parsing via `pdfjs-dist` and Claude AI
- Placeholder recovery system with review queue
- 50+ utility scripts for seeding, migration, and testing

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | Hono (OpenAPIHono) |
| Runtime | Node.js with tsx (watch mode) |
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL) via `@supabase/supabase-js` |
| AI | `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (Claude) |
| PDF | `pdfjs-dist`, `@napi-rs/canvas` |
| Validation | Zod (via `@repo/schemas`) |

## Project Structure

```
apps/api/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                    # App entry + route mounting
│   ├── routes/
│   │   ├── work-orders.ts          # Work order CRUD + lifecycle
│   │   ├── materials.ts            # Supplier/product catalog
│   │   ├── taxonomy.ts             # Category management
│   │   ├── project-definitions.ts  # JSONB scope trees
(venv) root@2c00128c0233:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app#
